514888.name: Jose Altuve
514888.background: A beloved veteran anchor for the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets, Jose Altuve is a future Hall of Famer and one of the most accomplished second basemen in baseball history — a seven-time All-Star, batting champion, ALCS MVP, and the cornerstone of Houston's dynasty. Even in the latter years of his career he provides elite plate discipline, solid power, and legitimate stolen base contributions from one of the game's most complete offensive profiles. The Ferrets have benefited from his steady excellence over multiple CBA seasons.
514888.recentAnalysis: Altuve has had a solid start for Ft. Meade: .278/.388/.444 (0.862 OPS) with 3 HR, 7 RBI, 16 runs, and 15 walks through 21 games — the elite plate discipline is fully intact and he's scoring runs at an excellent rate.

518692.name: Freddie Freeman
518692.background: Freddie Freeman is a former NL MVP and World Series champion who has been one of the most consistent first basemen in baseball for over a decade, a Dodger cornerstone whose contact, on-base skills, and clutch hitting make him one of the most valuable fantasy assets available. The Portland Chinook have him as their premier offensive weapon alongside Turner and Olson, counting on his elite batting average and consistent power to anchor their lineup from the cleanup spot. Freeman's career-long consistency makes him one of the safest keepers in the CBA.
518692.recentAnalysis: Freeman is off to a strong start with a .296 AVG, .879 OPS, 3 HR, and 14 RBI through 20 games — steady production that the Chinook can set their clock to.

518876.name: Merrill Kelly
518876.background: A veteran rotation depth piece for the Ghent Whistlepigs, Merrill Kelly has been one of Arizona's most reliable workhorses — a contact-management specialist who generates groundballs, limits walks, and consistently posts ERA figures in the mid-3.00s through elite command and pitch mix. The Whistlepigs have used him as a rotation safety net, knowing he can fill in for injured starters while posting useful ERA and WHIP numbers. His durability and professionalism make him a steady floor play.
518876.recentAnalysis: Kelly has made just 1 start so far in 2026 (5.1 IP, 3.38 ERA) — he appears to be ramping up slowly and Ghent is easing him into the rotation.

519242.name: Chris Sale
519242.background: Chris Sale is one of the most decorated lefties of his generation, a tall, whippy strike-thrower who spent years as an elite Red Sox ace before injuries derailed his career — now with the Braves, he's recaptured his elite form and reminded everyone why he was once the most feared pitcher in the AL. The North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms landed him as their staff ace, and when Sale is healthy and dealing he gives the Fuzzy Bottoms a top-shelf SP1 capable of shutting down any lineup. His strikeout ability remains elite.
519242.recentAnalysis: Sale is off to a strong start for Atlanta with a 2.79 ERA, 29 K, and 1.00 WHIP across 29 innings over 5 starts — the Fuzzy Bottoms' ace is delivering.

519317.name: Giancarlo Stanton
519317.background: A veteran power bat on the Sugar Land Space Cowboys roster, Giancarlo Stanton has been one of the most physically imposing hitters in baseball history — an NL MVP whose raw power generates some of the highest exit velocities and longest home runs the sport has ever measured. His injury history has been extensive, but when healthy he's a .250/30-HR threat who changes the tenor of any lineup. The Space Cowboys have kept him on their roster knowing that even a partial season of Stanton's power can swing CBA matchups.
519317.recentAnalysis: Stanton has had a modest start for Sugar Land: .240/.302/.353 with 2 HR and 10 RBI through 20 games — the enormous power that defines his value hasn't fully arrived yet in the early going.

521692.name: Salvador Perez
521692.background: A beloved veteran and Kansas City legend on the West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls roster, Salvador Perez is a six-time All-Star and World Series MVP whose durability and power from behind the plate have made him one of the most accomplished catchers of his generation. Now in his mid-30s, he provides a reliable floor with 20-homer upside and consistent RBI production for the Pepperoni Rolls' lineup. West Virginia counts on his professional leadership and power bat to anchor their catcher slot.
521692.recentAnalysis: Perez has had a slow start for the Pepperoni Rolls: .158/.195/.276 (0.510 OPS) with 3 HR and 6 RBI through 21 games — the power is trickling in but the batting average has been well below his career norms in the early going.

542303.name: Marcell Ozuna
542303.background: A veteran free agent DH/outfielder in CBA who still carries legitimate power, Marcell Ozuna has been one of the most prolific home run hitters in the NL throughout his career — posting multiple 35-plus homer seasons with Atlanta and producing RBI totals that consistently rank among the best in the league. Available for any CBA team looking to add DH power, he profiles as a .250-hitting right-handed bat with 25-HR upside when playing regularly.
542303.recentAnalysis: Ozuna has had a slow start: .188/.240/.281 with 2 HR and 7 RBI through 17 games — a disappointing early line that doesn't reflect his power history, though there's reason to expect improvement.

543037.name: Gerrit Cole
543037.background: The anchor of the Syracuse Sky Chiefs' pitching staff and one of the most accomplished right-handed starters of his generation, Gerrit Cole has built a career on elite fastball-slider combinations, exceptional strikeout rates, and multiple Cy Young Award finishes. His 2023 ALCS injury was a setback, but when fully healthy he remains a genuine ace who provides the Sky Chiefs with top-3 SP value. A full season from Cole at the top of Syracuse's rotation is a genuine CBA championship weapon.
543037.recentAnalysis: Cole is on the 15-day IL to open 2026 and has not yet taken the mound — Syracuse is hoping for a swift return from their most important starting pitcher.

543135.name: Nathan Eovaldi
543135.background: Nathan Eovaldi is a veteran right-hander for the Rangers with elite velocity and a fearless demeanor, a power pitcher who has remained one of the most reliable SP2/SP3 options in fantasy when healthy. The Syracuse Sky Chiefs have him pairing with Bryan Woo as their top two starters, giving them a rotation that can dominate matchups with pure arm strength. Eovaldi's consistency and ability to eat innings make him a valuable asset for the Sky Chiefs in a format that rewards starter volume.
543135.recentAnalysis: Eovaldi has a 5.06 ERA over 26.2 innings through 5 starts — a rocky stretch for the Sky Chiefs, who are expecting his ratios to normalize as his velocity and command stabilize.

543243.name: Sonny Gray
543243.background: A veteran rotation piece for the Portland Chinook, Sonny Gray is a craftsman who has reinvented himself multiple times over a long career — most recently as a groundball-heavy, command-oriented starter whose mature pitch mix and exceptional competitiveness have kept him productive well into his 30s. His Cardinals tenure produced some of the best work of his career, and Portland has relied on his steadiness and durability to provide innings at the top of their rotation. He's the kind of reliable veteran every CBA team covets.
543243.recentAnalysis: Gray has had a decent start for the Chinook: 4 GS, 20.1 IP, 4.43 ERA, 1.28 WHIP, 11 K — the ERA is a touch elevated but his 2 wins have been valuable, and the low strikeout rate reflects his contact-management approach.

543760.name: Marcus Semien
543760.background: A veteran presence and leader in the Sugar Land Space Cowboys clubhouse, Marcus Semien is a two-time All-Star whose elite defensive metrics at shortstop are matched by consistent 25-30 homer power and reliable run production at the top of a lineup. His Rangers tenure produced back-to-back elite offensive seasons before age began to temper the counting stats, but he remains a useful contributor with excellent durability. The Space Cowboys rely on his professionalism and floor production as part of their veteran core.
543760.recentAnalysis: Semien has had a slow start for Sugar Land: .216/.269/.308 (0.577 OPS) with 1 HR, 8 RBI, and 3 runs through 21 games — the power and run production that defines his value haven't shown up yet in the early going.

543807.name: George Springer
543807.background: A prized veteran on the Ghent Whistlepigs roster, George Springer is a former World Series MVP and five-time All-Star who brings elite leadoff skills, 25-plus homer power, and legitimate stolen base speed. His transition to Toronto was followed by multiple injury-shortened seasons, but when healthy he remains a top-20 outfielder with the full five-category profile. The Whistlepigs have kept faith through his injury history because the ceiling remains undeniably elite.
543807.recentAnalysis: Springer is on the 10-day IL after appearing in just 14 games (.185 AVG, 2 HR, 6 RBI) — limited production before the injury and Ghent is waiting for a healthy return.

545361.name: Mike Trout
545361.background: The greatest player in West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls history and arguably the best baseball player of his generation, Mike Trout is a three-time MVP whose combination of elite power, speed, on-base skills, and defense defined the template for five-category dominance for over a decade. Injury has limited his recent availability, but West Virginia has held his rights through it all — knowing that even a partial season of Trout contributes at an elite level across every offensive category. When healthy, there is no better player in CBA.
545361.recentAnalysis: Trout has been excellent for the Pepperoni Rolls when available: .237/.424/.553 (0.980 OPS) with 7 HR, 16 RBI, 22 runs, and 21 walks through 22 games — the power and elite walk rates are fully present even if the average is running below his career norms.

547180.name: Bryce Harper
547180.background: Bryce Harper is a former two-time NL MVP and one of the biggest stars in baseball, a Phillies first baseman whose combination of power, on-base skills, and star power make him one of the most valuable fantasy players in the game. The Syracuse Sky Chiefs have him anchoring their lineup alongside Judge and Bregman, giving them a devastating trio of right-handed bats capable of winning any matchup on their own. When Harper is healthy and locked in, few hitters in the CBA are more dangerous.
547180.recentAnalysis: Harper has a .270 AVG, .884 OPS, and 4 HR through 20 games — a strong early start that has the Sky Chiefs' offense firing on all cylinders.

547973.name: Aroldis Chapman
547973.background: The closer for the Bristol Banshees and a legendary figure in baseball history, Aroldis Chapman has thrown the fastest pitches ever recorded — routinely hitting 105-106 mph — and has converted saves at an elite rate throughout a career that has produced multiple World Series appearances and All-Star selections. Now in the veteran chapter of his career, he brings experience, a still-dangerous fastball, and professional save conversion ability to Bristol's ninth inning. The Banshees have built their bullpen identity around his late-game dominance.
547973.recentAnalysis: Chapman has been effective for Bristol early: 7 G, 7.0 IP, 1.29 ERA, 1.00 WHIP, 7 K, 3 SV — clean save conversions and good run prevention from the veteran closer.

553993.name: Eugenio Suarez
553993.background: A power-hitting third baseman for the Bristol Banshees, Eugenio Suarez has been one of the most reliable home run hitters in the NL for nearly a decade — posting six consecutive 20-plus HR seasons with a free-swinging approach that sacrifices average for power. The Banshees have leaned on his pop and RBI production to stay competitive in the power categories, and he provides the kind of 25-30 HR floor that is difficult to replicate at third base in CBA. He bats in the middle of Seattle's lineup with consistent RBI opportunities.
553993.recentAnalysis: Suarez has had a solid early run for Bristol: .263/.329/.434 with 3 HR, 11 RBI, and 9 walks through 22 games — the power is building and his usual production profile looks to be right on track.

554430.name: Zack Wheeler
554430.background: Zack Wheeler is a former Cy Young Award winner and one of the most dominant power pitchers in baseball, a Phillies ace whose triple-digit fastball and devastating slider have made him an annual fantasy SP1 when healthy. The Sugar Land Space Cowboys grabbed him as their co-ace alongside Max Fried, and when Wheeler is pitching at full strength there's no more imposing arm in the CBA. His strikeout rate and ground-ball ability make him truly elite on the days he takes the mound.
554430.recentAnalysis: Wheeler is currently on the D15 IL — the Space Cowboys are holding their breath and monitoring his return timeline, as he's one of their most critical pitching assets.

571448.name: Nolan Arenado
571448.background: A veteran cornerstone and future Hall of Famer on the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets roster, Nolan Arenado is the greatest defensive third baseman of his generation — a ten-time Gold Glove winner whose elite glovework is matched by consistent 25-30 HR, 90-RBI seasons in the middle of a productive lineup. His Cardinals tenure has been defined by continued offensive excellence and postseason leadership, and Ft. Meade has relied on his floor production to stay competitive in the power and RBI categories year after year.
571448.recentAnalysis: Arenado has had a slow offensive start for the Ferrets: .214/.222/.371 (0.579 OPS) with 3 HR and 11 RBI through 20 games — the power is there but a .214 average and virtually no walks is a concerning early profile.

571970.name: Max Muncy
571970.background: A free agent first baseman in CBA with elite power and walks, Max Muncy has been one of the most dangerous left-handed hitters in the NL for the Dodgers — combining 35-homer upside with one of the best walk rates in baseball to produce elite OBP figures year after year. Available to any CBA team looking to add first base power, he profiles as a .240-hitting first baseman with 25-plus home runs and outstanding on-base skills. His CBA value is concentrated in HR, walks, and runs.
571970.recentAnalysis: Muncy has been solid: .257/.333/.529 (0.862 OPS) with 6 HR, 7 RBI, and 14 runs through 20 games — the power is very much live and he's one of the more appealing free agent power bats available in CBA.

572233.name: Christian Walker
572233.background: A first base cornerstone for the West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls, Christian Walker has been one of the most underrated first basemen in the NL — consistently posting 30-plus homer seasons with elite defense and solid on-base skills in Arizona's lineup. His combination of power, walks, and run production has made him a reliable CBA contributor year after year, and the Pepperoni Rolls have counted on his floor production to anchor their corner infield slot. Walker's consistency is as dependable as any player on their roster.
572233.recentAnalysis: Walker has been solid for West Virginia: .256/.363/.487 (0.839 OPS) with 4 HR, 16 RBI, and 11 walks through 23 games — consistent power and on-base production from a reliable veteran who has delivered for the Pepperoni Rolls.

575929.name: Willson Contreras
575929.background: The Delmarva Emus' starting catcher and one of their most passionate contributors, Willson Contreras has been one of the most productive catchers in the NL for the better part of a decade — combining 20-plus homer power with excellent arm strength and a take-charge presence behind the plate. His St. Louis tenure has produced continued offensive production in a lineup built around his RBI opportunities. For Delmarva, he's the rare CBA catcher who can legitimately anchor a starting lineup slot.
575929.recentAnalysis: Contreras has made an excellent early showing for the Emus: .283/.421/.500 (0.921 OPS) with 3 HR, 11 RBI, and 11 walks through 18 games — the walks and power are both there in what's been one of the better catcher starts in the league.

579328.name: Yusei Kikuchi
579328.background: A rotation piece for the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets, Yusei Kikuchi arrived from Japan with a plus arsenal that has produced multiple strikeout-heavy seasons for Seattle and Toronto. His fastball-slider combination is effective against left-handed hitters in particular, and the Ferrets have relied on his strikeout upside to boost their weekly K totals. An inconsistent command profile creates volatility, but the Ferrets are invested in his upside as a double-digit wins, high-K starter.
579328.recentAnalysis: Kikuchi has had a rough start for the Ferrets: 5 GS, 24.0 IP, 5.63 ERA, 1.63 WHIP — though 27 K in those 24 innings shows the strikeout upside is intact despite poor run prevention early on.

592332.name: Kevin Gausman
592332.background: The ace of the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets rotation, Kevin Gausman's late-career reinvention around his split-finger fastball has made him one of the most dangerous starters in the NL. He generates elite strikeout rates and weak contact from one of the most effective pitches in the game, and his Giants tenure has been defined by consistent sub-3.50 ERA campaigns with Cy Young-caliber strikeout numbers. For the Ferrets, he's the crown jewel of their staff — a true fantasy ace they can pencil in every fifth day.
592332.recentAnalysis: Gausman has been outstanding in 2026: 4 GS, 22.1 IP, 2.42 ERA, 0.85 WHIP, 31 K — a 12.5 K/9 with almost no free passes is peak Gausman, and the Ferrets are getting full ace value from their top starter.

592450.name: Rob Refsnyder
592450.background: A journeyman outfielder who carved out a useful bench role with Boston, Rob Refsnyder is a right-handed contact hitter with some positional flexibility who periodically gets hot enough to be a streaming option. He lacks the power or speed to be a regular starter in fantasy but his contact skills give him some floor when playing daily.
592450.recentAnalysis: Refsnyder is off to a slow start, hitting .121 with a .514 OPS through 38 PA — not enough production to justify a roster spot unless injuries force his hand into the lineup.

592518.name: Manny Machado
592518.background: Manny Machado is a perennial all-star third baseman for the Padres and one of the most skilled all-around players of his generation, combining elite defense with consistent .280/.280/.480 offensive production that makes him a perennial fantasy top-10 at his position. The North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms have him as a lineup anchor alongside Sale, Imanaga, and deGrom, giving them a formidable combination of pitching depth and a dangerous corner bat. Machado's floor as a consistent 25-HR, 85-RBI producer is what the Fuzzy Bottoms are counting on.
592518.recentAnalysis: Machado has a .194 AVG and .654 OPS through 21 games — a slow start for the Fuzzy Bottoms' third baseman, who historically elevates his production as the season deepens.

592662.name: Robbie Ray
592662.background: A former AL Cy Young winner on the Bristol Banshees rotation, Robbie Ray won the 2021 award with Toronto by posting a 2.84 ERA and 248 strikeouts — one of the great single-season pitching performances of the decade. He's battled injuries and inconsistency since but remains a power left-hander whose fastball-slider combination can be elite when his mechanics are right. The Banshees have committed to him as a rotation anchor, and a full healthy season from Ray would dramatically elevate Bristol's CBA pitching profile.
592662.recentAnalysis: Ray has been excellent for Bristol in his return: 5 GS, 28.1 IP, 2.86 ERA, 1.09 WHIP, 31 K — a 9.8 K/9 with solid ratios is exactly the kind of frontline production the Banshees needed from their veteran ace.

592885.name: Christian Yelich
592885.background: A cornerstone of the West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls outfield, Christian Yelich won the 2018 NL MVP with one of the most dominant offensive seasons in Brewers history — .326/36 HR/22 SB — and remains a .280-caliber hitter with 20-homer upside in Milwaukee's lineup. Injuries and some mechanical adjustments reduced his production in subsequent years, but the Pepperoni Rolls have held his rights banking on a return toward that elite form. When healthy, there are few more dangerous outfielders on any CBA roster.
592885.recentAnalysis: Yelich is currently on the 10-day IL; he appeared in 15 games before the injury, hitting .314/.375/.451 with 1 HR, 10 RBI, 10 runs, and 3 SB — a promising start before going down.

593428.name: Xander Bogaerts
593428.background: A cornerstone of the Portland Chinook infield, Xander Bogaerts is a two-time World Series champion and perennial All-Star who built his reputation as one of the most consistent offensive shortstops in baseball over a decade in Boston before signing a massive deal with San Diego. His elite contact skills, gap power, and on-base ability have consistently produced .280-plus averages with 20-plus homers, and Portland has benefited from his steady production as a middle-infield anchor for multiple CBA seasons.
593428.recentAnalysis: Bogaerts has had a solid start for Portland: .260/.325/.411 with 3 HR, 13 RBI, and 2 SB through 19 games — consistent contact and counting stats with his usual elite bat-to-ball skills keeping strikeouts minimal.

593871.name: Jorge Polanco
593871.background: A free agent second baseman in CBA with solid power and batting average history, Jorge Polanco has been one of the more consistent offensive second basemen in the AL during his Minnesota tenure — posting .270-plus averages with 20-plus homers in multiple seasons. Available for any CBA team looking for second base depth, he profiles as a steady veteran bat with 18-HR upside and useful plate discipline. His injury history is a concern but his floor production is well-established.
593871.recentAnalysis: Polanco has had a slow start: .179/.246/.268 with 1 HR and 2 RBI in 61 PA through 14 games — a significantly below-expectations line that may reflect early-season rust rather than a true decline.

594798.name: Jacob deGrom
594798.background: Jacob deGrom is a two-time Cy Young Award winner and one of the most dominant pitchers of his generation, a right-hander for the Rangers whose elite fastball-slider combination and devastating command made him the benchmark for what an ace can be. The North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms have him as a premium rotation piece alongside Imanaga and Sale, and when healthy deGrom gives the Fuzzy Bottoms a legitimate three-ace rotation that can dominate any weekly matchup. His return to health is one of the most anticipated storylines for the Fuzzy Bottoms.
594798.recentAnalysis: deGrom has been sharp in his return with a 2.29 ERA, 25 K, and 1.07 WHIP over 19.2 innings through 4 starts — the Fuzzy Bottoms are thrilled to see their ace healthy and dealing.

595014.name: Blake Treinen
595014.background: A veteran power reliever for the Syracuse Sky Chiefs, Blake Treinen was one of the most dominant setup arms in the AL during his peak — featuring a plus sinker that generated elite groundball rates and strikeouts in the most crucial situations. The Sky Chiefs have held his rights through multiple injury-shortened seasons knowing his upside when healthy is that of a top-10 fantasy reliever. He pairs with Mason Miller to give Syracuse one of the most formidable late-game bullpen combinations in all of CBA.
595014.recentAnalysis: Treinen has been excellent for the Sky Chiefs in his early appearances: 8 G, 6.2 IP, 0.00 ERA, 0.90 WHIP, 6 K, 4 HD — clean outings and holds contributions in exactly the setup role Syracuse needs from him.

596019.name: Eloy Jimenez
596019.background: A big left-handed power hitter who has shown elite bat speed and raw power in his best seasons with the White Sox, Eloy Jimenez has been one of the most injury-plagued players of his generation. His pectoral and hamstring injuries have wiped out significant chunks of his career, but his 35+ HR ceiling when healthy is one of the highest in baseball.
596019.recentAnalysis: Jimenez is showing early signs of his elite contact with .360/.414/.360 through 29 PA — no home runs yet but the batting average and contact quality are encouraging. The power should arrive as he gets more comfortable.

596115.name: Trevor Story
596115.background: A high-upside gamble on the Portland Chinook roster, Trevor Story was one of the most dangerous shortstops in baseball during his Colorado years — combining 30-plus homers with 25-plus steals in a five-category profile that made him perennially top-10 at his position. His Red Sox tenure has been marred by injuries including Tommy John surgery, but Portland has held his rights banking on a return to that elite form. When healthy, Story remains one of the best power-speed shortstops CBA has ever seen.
596115.recentAnalysis: Story has had a very tough start for Portland: .198/.223/.311 (0.533 OPS) with 2 HR and 17 RBI through 19 games — 26 strikeouts in 85 PA is alarming, and the Chinook are still waiting for the breakout they drafted him for.

601713.name: Nick Pivetta
601713.background: Nick Pivetta is a right-handed starter for the Padres whose mid-90s fastball and devastating curveball give him legitimate strikeout upside when he's commanding his stuff, though his tendency for home run balls and occasional walks keep his fantasy ceiling in check. The Manhattan Mega Rats have him as a rotation piece alongside Yamamoto and Suarez, counting on his strikeout ability to contribute K totals even in starts where he's not at his sharpest. Pivetta's raw stuff makes him a fantasy-relevant arm on his best days.
601713.recentAnalysis: Pivetta is on the D15 IL after 4 starts — the Mega Rats are monitoring his status and hoping for a quick return from their swing starter.

605141.name: Mookie Betts
605141.background: Mookie Betts is a former MVP and one of the most complete baseball players of his generation, a Dodger outfielder whose combination of hitting, fielding, baserunning, and clutch production have made him a perennial all-star and top-five fantasy asset. The North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms secured him as a premium keeper alongside Sale and Arraez, knowing that when Mookie is healthy and locked in he's a matchup-winning machine across all five categories. His floor is as high as anyone in the CBA.
605141.recentAnalysis: Betts is on the D10 IL after a slow 8-game start — the Fuzzy Bottoms are monitoring his return and expecting his typical elite production once he's healthy.

605280.name: Clay Holmes
605280.background: A rotation conversion success story on the Portland Chinook staff, Clay Holmes built his career as one of the best relievers in the AL — featuring an elite sinker that generated near-historic groundball rates and saves for the Yankees. The Chinook acquired him knowing he was transitioning to a starting role, and his sinker-heavy approach translates well to longer outings where he can work through lineups without revealing a fastball-only profile. Portland is betting on his groundball dominance holding up over 5-6 innings.
605280.recentAnalysis: Holmes has been impressive in his starter conversion for the Chinook: 4 GS, 23.0 IP, 1.96 ERA, 1.09 WHIP, 16 K — excellent run prevention and a 2 wins already in what has been a very strong transition to the rotation.

605397.name: Joe Musgrove
605397.background: A veteran rotation piece for the Ghent Whistlepigs, Joe Musgrove has been one of the most reliable starters in the NL during his San Diego tenure — a crafty right-hander with elite command and a full arsenal of secondary pitches who has delivered multiple sub-3.50 ERA seasons including a no-hitter. The Whistlepigs have relied on his durability and consistency to anchor their rotation, though his injury history has created some anxiety. When healthy, he's a dependable fantasy SP who rarely has blow-up outings.
605397.recentAnalysis: Musgrove is on the 15-day IL to start 2026 — Ghent is waiting on their veteran starter to return and provide the steady innings they count on from him.

605400.name: Aaron Nola
605400.background: A veteran ace and one of the most consistent starting pitchers in the game, Aaron Nola has anchored the Philadelphia Phillies' rotation through multiple postseason runs and delivered reliable sub-3.50 ERA seasons with elite strikeout rates year after year. The Ghent Whistlepigs have benefited from his workhorse mentality and ability to pitch deep into games, providing a steady hand at the top of their CBA rotation. Nola's durability and consistency make him one of the safest picks in all of CBA.
605400.recentAnalysis: Nola has been solid for the Whistlepigs: 4 GS, 22.1 IP, 4.03 ERA, 1.30 WHIP, 24 K — the strikeout rate is right on profile (9.7 K/9) but the ERA and WHIP suggest some hard-hit ball issues in the early going.

605488.name: Jeffrey Springs
605488.background: A crafty left-handed starter who has rejuvenated his career for the Bristol Banshees, Jeffrey Springs transformed himself from a bullpen piece into one of the better rotation bargains in the AL with Tampa Bay, relying on deception, plus changeup tunneling, and elite command to get outs with well-below-average velocity. His Tommy John surgery cost him 2024, but Bristol held his rights knowing a healthy Springs is a legitimate fantasy SP with elite WHIP and ERA potential. The Banshees are reaping the rewards of that patient investment.
605488.recentAnalysis: Springs has been outstanding in his return for Bristol: 4 GS, 24.2 IP, 1.46 ERA, 0.77 WHIP, 20 K — one of the best early-season ERA lines in CBA and a huge boost for the Banshees' pitching staff.

605540.name: Brandon Woodruff
605540.background: Brandon Woodruff is one of the most talented pitchers in baseball when healthy, a Brewers right-hander with a mid-90s fastball and devastating slider who has dealt with significant shoulder injuries but remains a top-15 starter when right. The Ghent Whistlepigs built their rotation around him as their de facto ace alongside Sheehan and Peralta, and when Woodruff is dealing he gives the Whistlepigs a genuine top-of-the-rotation weapon. His healthy return has been one of the most anticipated storylines in the CBA.
605540.recentAnalysis: Woodruff has a 3.42 ERA, 20 K, and 0.93 WHIP over 23.2 innings through 4 starts — a strong and encouraging return for the Whistlepigs' ace.

606192.name: Teoscar Hernandez
606192.background: A power bat in the Delmarva Emus outfield, Teoscar Hernandez has developed into one of the more dangerous right-handed hitters in the NL, combining 30-plus homer upside with a high-energy approach that generates big run-scoring opportunities. His time with Toronto, Seattle, and now the Dodgers has demonstrated consistent power production in any environment, and the Emus are benefiting from his placement in one of baseball's best lineups. In a stacked Delmarva roster, Hernandez provides the power punch that completes their outfield.
606192.recentAnalysis: Hernandez has been productive early for the Emus: .286/.324/.524 with 4 HR, 12 RBI, and 12 runs through 17 games — the power is showing up right on schedule in one of baseball's most productive lineups.

606466.name: Ketel Marte
606466.background: Ketel Marte is one of the most versatile and underrated hitters in baseball, a switch-hitting sparkplug for the Diamondbacks who can play second base, shortstop, and center field with elite production at the plate. The Delmarva Emus have him plugged into their middle infield as a consistent, multi-category producer alongside Ohtani and Seager, giving them one of the more potent offensive cores in the CBA. His 2023 breakout cemented him as a legitimate fantasy star.
606466.recentAnalysis: Marte is off to a slow start in 2026 with a .213 AVG and .664 OPS through 21 games — the Emus are expecting him to regress toward his typical strong production soon.

607043.name: Brandon Nimmo
607043.background: A key piece of the Delmarva Emus outfield, Brandon Nimmo is one of baseball's elite on-base presences — a leadoff hitter whose extraordinary patience and walk rates produce OBP figures consistently above .380. His re-signing with the Mets confirmed his status as a franchise anchor, and he provides solid counting stat accumulation from the top of New York's order with 20-homer upside as a bonus. The Emus rely on his runs, walks, and reliability to help power one of CBA's more stacked rosters.
607043.recentAnalysis: Nimmo is off to a terrific start for Delmarva: .313/.396/.537 (0.934 OPS) with 4 HR, 11 RBI, and 13 runs through 20 games — delivering on his elite OBP profile with added punch in the power department.

607074.name: Carlos Rodon
607074.background: A high-upside starter on the Portland Chinook rotation, Carlos Rodon's career trajectory has been defined by electric stuff and persistent injury — a 2021 near no-hitter and a 2022 All-Star campaign serving as peaks in between multiple shoulder and elbow procedures. When healthy, his fastball-slider combination is among the most dominant in the AL, and Portland has held his rights through yet another IL stint knowing a full season of Rodon is a legitimate fantasy ace. The Chinook are waiting on the healthy version they know exists.
607074.recentAnalysis: Rodon is on the 15-day IL and has not yet appeared in 2026 — Portland is hoping for a return that puts their ace candidate back on the mound for a full stretch of games.

607192.name: Tyler Glasnow
607192.background: Tyler Glasnow is one of the most devastating pitchers in baseball when healthy, a Dodger ace whose 100-mph fastball and elite curveball combination gives him one of the highest K/9 rates in the game when he's on the mound. The Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets have him as their rotation centerpiece alongside Logan Webb, and when Glasnow is fully locked in he's a matchup-winning ace capable of shutting down any lineup. His strikeout ceiling is among the highest of any pitcher available to the CBA.
607192.recentAnalysis: Glasnow has a 3.24 ERA, 29 K, and 0.84 WHIP over 25 innings through 4 starts — a strong start for the Folksy Ferrets' ace, who looks healthy and dominant.

607208.name: Trea Turner
607208.background: Trea Turner is one of the fastest players in baseball, a perennial 30-steal shortstop for the Phillies who combines his elite speed with consistent power and a .280+ average to deliver across all five fantasy categories. The Portland Chinook landed him as their most dynamic offensive weapon, pairing him with Daylen Lile in an outfield/infield combo that gives the Chinook premium speed at the top of their lineup. Turner's track record as a multi-category producer makes him one of the most reliable keepers in the league.
607208.recentAnalysis: Turner has a .693 OPS and 2 SB through 20 games — a modest start for the Chinook's franchise player, but his speed and contact suggest better numbers are coming.

607625.name: Seth Lugo
607625.background: A veteran rotation anchor for the Delmarva Emus, Seth Lugo reinvented himself from a high-leverage reliever into a legitimate starting pitcher with Kansas City — posting a sub-2.00 ERA in his debut starting season with elite command and an excellent cutter-sinker combination. The Emus secured him as a reliable mid-rotation piece knowing his floor as a keeper is among the safest in CBA. Delmarva relies on his durability and consistent ratios to anchor their staff alongside Bradly and the younger arms they've accumulated.
607625.recentAnalysis: Lugo has been outstanding for the Emus: 4 GS, 24.1 IP, 1.48 ERA, 0.99 WHIP, 21 K — a sterling start that shows his 2025 breakout was no fluke and makes him one of the most reliable starters in CBA.

608032.name: Carlos Estevez
608032.background: The closer for the Delmarva Emus and a high-leverage reliever with a premium fastball, Carlos Estevez has built a save accumulation record with the Angels and Phillies by combining plus velocity with a sharp breaking ball that generates strikeouts in the ninth inning. The Emus have paired him with Ryan Helsley to create one of the most formidable late-game options in CBA, and his save production has been a key factor in Delmarva's competitive success. When healthy, he's a reliable double-digit save contributor.
608032.recentAnalysis: Estevez is on the 15-day IL after making just one appearance in 2026 — Delmarva is waiting for their reliever to return healthy and get back into their save rotation.

608070.name: Jose Ramirez
608070.background: A perennial CBA free agent gem and one of the most consistent five-category hitters in baseball over the past decade, Jose Ramirez is a switch-hitting third baseman who annually delivers 25-plus home runs, 20-plus steals, excellent on-base skills, and MVP-caliber overall production from the heart of Cleveland's lineup. Available for any CBA team to claim, he is arguably the best third baseman on the free agent market and represents an enormous upgrade at the position. Multiple CBA championship teams have been built around his steady excellence.
608070.recentAnalysis: Ramirez has been excellent in the early going: .229/.374/.542 (0.848 OPS) with 6 HR, 10 SB, 14 runs, and 16 walks through 23 games — the power, speed, and walks are fully live even as the batting average lags behind his usual norms.

608324.name: Alex Bregman
608324.background: Alex Bregman is a two-time World Series champion and one of the smartest hitters in baseball, a disciplined third baseman now with the Red Sox whose elite plate patience and consistent power have made him a fantasy cornerstone for years. The Syracuse Sky Chiefs have him in the middle of their lineup alongside Judge and Turang, counting on his on-base skills and 25-HR pop to provide steady run production. Bregman's walk rate and baseball IQ make him a floor-raising asset on any roster.
608324.recentAnalysis: Bregman has a .233 AVG and .623 OPS through 21 games for Boston — a quiet start for the Sky Chiefs' veteran third baseman, with better production expected as the season unfolds.

608331.name: Max Fried
608331.background: Max Fried is one of the best left-handed starters in baseball, a crafty Yankee whose elite command, plus curveball, and deceptive delivery allow him to dominate lineups without overpowering them with pure velocity. The Sugar Land Space Cowboys have him anchoring their pitching staff alongside Zack Wheeler, and when Fried is dealing the Space Cowboys have a genuine top-of-the-rotation weapon. His ability to miss bats while also generating weak contact makes him effective in any ballpark.
608331.recentAnalysis: Fried has a 2.97 ERA, 23 K, and 0.81 WHIP over 33.1 innings across 5 starts — a clean and dominant early run for the Space Cowboys' ace.

608348.name: Carson Kelly
608348.background: The veteran catcher for the Syracuse Sky Chiefs, Carson Kelly is a dependable two-way backstop who provides solid defense and a patient, contact-oriented offensive approach that produces useful batting averages and walk rates. The Sky Chiefs rely on his professionalism and consistency at a position that can be volatile week to week in fantasy, and his ability to post .280-plus averages while providing adequate power makes him a stable floor at the C slot. He pairs well with Alejandro Kirk to give Syracuse genuine depth behind the plate.
608348.recentAnalysis: Kelly has had a strong start for the Sky Chiefs: .321/.444/.500 (0.920 OPS) with 2 HR, 10 RBI, and 10 walks through 18 games — excellent contact and plate discipline contributing nicely across multiple categories.

608369.name: Corey Seager
608369.background: Corey Seager is a two-time World Series MVP and one of the most dangerous left-handed hitters in baseball, a cornerstone shortstop for the Rangers who combines plus power with excellent on-base skills. The Delmarva Emus pair him with Ketel Marte and Ohtani as part of a formidable offensive core, giving them three premium bats to anchor every week. When healthy, Seager is one of the most productive shortstops in all of fantasy baseball.
608369.recentAnalysis: Seager has 5 HR and 12 RBI through 21 games but is hitting just .205 — the Emus are waiting for his average to climb to match his power output.

608379.name: Michael Wacha
608379.background: A reliable innings-eater and rotation piece for the Ghent Whistlepigs, Michael Wacha has rebuilt his career as a contact-management specialist who generates weak contact and consistently posts ERA figures well below his stuff would suggest. The Whistlepigs have relied on his ability to go deep into games and protect their bullpen, and his durability across multiple seasons has been one of their rotation's quiet competitive advantages. Ghent values his floor production even if the ceiling is modest.
608379.recentAnalysis: Wacha has been outstanding for the Whistlepigs: 4 GS, 27.0 IP, 1.00 ERA, 0.78 WHIP, 23 K — a remarkable start with an elite ERA and WHIP that has given Ghent one of the best early-season rotation contributions in the league.

609280.name: Miguel Andujar
609280.background: A free agent corner infielder/DH in CBA with veteran pop, Miguel Andujar was once one of the most anticipated Yankees prospects and finished second in AL Rookie of the Year voting in 2018. Injuries derailed his trajectory but he remains a solid contact hitter with gap power and professional at-bats. Available for any CBA team looking for DH/corner infield depth, he profiles as a .290-hitting right-handed bat who contributes average and RBI without hurting in the other categories.
609280.recentAnalysis: Andujar has made excellent contact in limited action: .341/.360/.500 (0.860 OPS) with 0 HR, 2 RBI, and 1 SB across 15 games in 47 PA — the contact skills are fully present and he'd be a useful add for any team needing lineup depth.

621020.name: Dansby Swanson
621020.background: Dansby Swanson is a veteran shortstop for the Cubs whose above-average bat, solid power, and Gold Glove defense have made him one of the NL's most well-rounded middle infielders since arriving in Chicago. The Sugar Land Space Cowboys have him as additional middle infield depth alongside Cruz and Lindor, giving them quality production across their infield even when injuries or rests arise. Swanson's consistent approach and leadership give the Space Cowboys a reliable veteran presence.
621020.recentAnalysis: Swanson has 4 HR and 12 RBI through 21 games but is hitting just .191 — some average struggles for the Space Cowboys, but the power production is keeping him useful.

621043.name: Carlos Correa
621043.background: A franchise-level acquisition for the Delmarva Emus, Carlos Correa is a former World Series champion and ALCS MVP who has maintained his status as one of the best offensive shortstops in baseball through consistent 25-homer, 75-RBI seasons with elite on-base skills. His patience at the plate, postseason excellence, and defensive reputation have made him a cornerstone wherever he plays. The Emus count on his steady middle-of-the-lineup production to anchor their CBA roster year after year.
621043.recentAnalysis: Correa has had a solid start: .271/.348/.383 with 1 HR, 9 RBI, and 15 runs through 18 games — excellent run-scoring from a productive spot in Minnesota's lineup, though the power has been quiet early.

621439.name: Byron Buxton
621439.background: Byron Buxton is the most gifted all-around player in baseball when healthy, a center fielder for the Twins whose elite speed, power, and defense make him a unicorn who can win fantasy matchups on his own during hot streaks. The Portland Chinook kept him as a high-ceiling risk-reward play, knowing that even a half-season of Buxton production makes him worth the roster spot. When he's locked in, there's no more exciting player to watch in the CBA.
621439.recentAnalysis: Buxton has 3 HR and a .716 OPS through 20 games for Minnesota — solid early production that the Chinook will happily take as long as he stays off the injury list.

621493.name: Taylor Ward
621493.background: A patient, high-OBP bat for the Portland Chinook outfield, Taylor Ward emerged as a late-blooming hitter who maximized his power and on-base skills after changing his approach at the plate. His ability to work counts and draw walks at an above-average rate gives him a strong OBP profile, and he's shown legitimate 20-homer upside in his best seasons. Portland values him as a reliable floor play who won't hurt you in any category and contributes in runs and walks.
621493.recentAnalysis: Ward has had a nice early stretch for the Chinook: .291/.404/.430 with 0 HR but 13 walks and 10 runs through 20 games — the OBP approach is excellent and the power figures to arrive as the season progresses.

621566.name: Matt Olson
621566.background: Matt Olson is one of the premier left-handed power hitters in baseball, a first baseman for the Braves whose 50-homer season and consistent elite production have made him a perennial fantasy cornerstone. The Portland Chinook have him anchoring their lineup alongside Turner and Gilbert, relying on his 40-HR ceiling and walk-heavy approach to produce elite run totals every week. Olson's combination of power and on-base skills makes him the rock that the Chinook's offense is built around.
621566.recentAnalysis: Olson is off to a hot start with a .296 AVG, .999 OPS, 5 HR, and 15 RBI through 21 games — the Chinook's big first baseman is already producing at a pace that sets them up well.

622491.name: Luis Castillo
622491.background: A free agent starting pitcher in CBA and a perennial All-Star when healthy, Luis Castillo features one of the most devastating two-seam fastball-changeup combinations in baseball — a pitch pairing that generates elite groundball rates and strikeouts throughout deep starts. His Seattle tenure has produced continued excellence, and he remains a top-15 SP when fully healthy. Available to any CBA team, he represents one of the best starting pitcher upgrades in the free agent pool.
622491.recentAnalysis: Castillo has had a rough early stretch: 4 GS, 18.1 IP, 5.40 ERA, 1.80 WHIP, 17 K — the strikeout stuff is intact but the ERA and WHIP are significantly elevated above his usual elite level.

622554.name: Seranthony Dominguez
622554.background: A power reliever and save contributor for the Manhattan Mega Rats, Seranthony Dominguez features elite velocity and a plus slider that have made him one of Philadelphia's most dangerous late-game options. The Mega Rats acquired his rights knowing his closer history and strikeout ability translate directly to fantasy value, and his saves and holds have been a meaningful part of Manhattan's weekly strategy. His electric stuff gives him the ceiling of a top-10 fantasy closer when he's fully healthy and in command.
622554.recentAnalysis: Dominguez has had a rough start for the Mega Rats: 8 G, 7.0 IP, 6.43 ERA, 1.86 WHIP, 9 K, 3 SV — the saves are coming in despite poor ratios, and the underlying strikeout rate gives hope for improvement.

623149.name: Paul Sewald
623149.background: The closer for the Delmarva Emus and one of the most reliable save sources in the AL, Paul Sewald has posted dominant save totals for Seattle with a plus fastball and a changeup that generates elite whiff rates in the highest-leverage situations. The Emus have committed significant roster value to him knowing his save conversion ability is among the best in baseball, and his presence in the back of Delmarva's bullpen gives them a weekly CBA edge. He and Helsley give the Emus the most formidable late-game combination in the league.
623149.recentAnalysis: Sewald has been outstanding for the Emus: 10 G, 8.1 IP, 2.16 ERA, 0.48 WHIP, 11 K, 7 SV — a phenomenal start with 7 saves and an elite WHIP, exactly what Delmarva's roster construction is built around.

623352.name: Josh Hader
623352.background: The premier closer on the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets roster and one of the most dominant relief pitchers of his generation, Josh Hader has converted saves at an elite rate throughout his career with a fastball-slider combination that renders lineups helpless. His ability to generate strikeouts in the ninth inning while protecting slim leads has made him a fantasy closer royalty, and the Ferrets have leaned on his save production as a weekly CBA advantage. Even into his 30s, Hader's stuff remains among the best in any bullpen.
623352.recentAnalysis: Hader is on the 15-day IL to open 2026 — the Ferrets are waiting on their closer to return and reclaim the save opportunities that make him so valuable.

624133.name: Ranger Suarez
624133.background: Ranger Suarez is a left-handed starter for the Phillies who has quietly established himself as one of the more effective mid-rotation arms in the NL, relying on excellent sink and arm-side run to generate ground balls and limit walks in his typically efficient outings. The Manhattan Mega Rats have him in their rotation alongside Yamamoto and Crochet, giving them a dependable innings-eater who can hold his own in most matchups. Suarez's consistency and ability to work deep into games make him a reliable fantasy SP3.
624133.recentAnalysis: Suarez has a 3.22 ERA with 15 K over 22.1 innings through 4 starts — solid efficiency for the Mega Rats' rotation depth piece.

624413.name: Pete Alonso
624413.background: Pete Alonso is the Polar Bear, a beloved first baseman for the Mets who has been one of the most consistent home run hitters in baseball since his record-setting 2019 rookie campaign. The West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls have him as a lineup anchor alongside De La Cruz and Rodriguez, counting on his 40-HR ceiling and RBI production to give their offense an imposing presence in the middle of the order. Alonso's walk rate and disciplined approach make him one of the most reliable power producers in the CBA.
624413.recentAnalysis: Alonso has 2 HR and a .664 OPS through 22 games — a slow start for the Pepperoni Rolls' big first baseman, who typically heats up as the season progresses.

624585.name: Jorge Soler
624585.background: A veteran power bat on the Bristol Banshees roster, Jorge Soler is best remembered for his walk-off home run in the 2021 World Series and three consecutive 30-plus HR seasons that made him one of the most dangerous right-handed power bats in fantasy baseball. The Banshees have held his rights through a suspension, expecting his power to carry significant CBA value when he returns to action. His 35-homer ceiling and RBI production at full health remain among the best on any roster.
624585.recentAnalysis: Soler is currently serving a suspension (SUSP) but managed 19 games before it began: .227/.338/.500 (0.838 OPS) with 5 HR and 18 RBI — strong power production that previews what the Banshees can expect once he returns.

624641.name: Edmundo Sosa
624641.background: A free agent utility infielder in CBA with multi-position flexibility, Edmundo Sosa has carved out a dependable bench role through his ability to play every infield position and contribute useful batting average in part-time situations. Available for any CBA team in need of infield depth, he profiles as a .265-hitting utility player whose positional flexibility gives rosters valuable insurance. A safe, low-variance depth addition.
624641.recentAnalysis: Sosa has been solid in limited action: .250/.310/.458 with 1 HR and 5 RBI in 30 PA across 11 games — useful production off the bench in a part-time role.

625643.name: Reynaldo Lopez
625643.background: A key mid-rotation arm for the North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms, Reynaldo Lopez transformed himself from a hard-throwing reliever into a legitimate starting pitcher for Atlanta with an improved pitch mix and much better command. His ability to generate strikeouts and keep the ball on the ground has made him a reliable SP with upside as his development continues, and the Fuzzy Bottoms benefit from his placement in Atlanta's excellent pitching infrastructure. North Georgia views him as a steady contributor who can quietly win CBA pitching weeks.
625643.recentAnalysis: Lopez has been solid for the Fuzzy Bottoms: 4 GS, 20.2 IP, 2.18 ERA, 1.11 WHIP, 19 K — a 2.18 ERA is excellent even if the strikeout rate is modest, and he's been one of North Georgia's most reliable rotation pieces.

628452.name: Raisel Iglesias
628452.background: The closer for the Manhattan Mega Rats and one of the most reliable save sources in the AL, Raisel Iglesias features elite late-game stuff with a mid-90s fastball and a devastating curveball that generates strikeouts in the highest-pressure situations. The Mega Rats have built their bullpen around his ability to close out leads consistently, and his save conversion rate has been one of the best in baseball for multiple seasons. Manhattan views his save production as a weekly CBA competitive advantage.
628452.recentAnalysis: Iglesias has been absolutely dominant for the Mega Rats: 7 G, 7.2 IP, 0.00 ERA, 0.52 WHIP, 10 K, 4 SV — one of the most dominant closer stretches in early 2026 and a huge asset for Manhattan.

641343.name: Jake Bauers
641343.background: A versatile bat on the West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls roster, Jake Bauers brings a well-rounded offensive profile with surprising power upside and solid plate discipline that makes him a useful contributor in CBA's multi-category scoring. His ability to play first base and outfield gives the Pepperoni Rolls positional flexibility, and his power-speed combination at the plate profiles toward 20 HR and double-digit steals when everything is working. West Virginia has valued him as a depth piece with upside.
641343.recentAnalysis: Bauers has been a pleasant surprise for the Pepperoni Rolls: .234/.310/.500 (0.794 OPS) with 5 HR, 13 RBI, 13 runs, and 3 SB through 19 games — the power and speed are both contributing in the early going.

641355.name: Tyler O'Neill
641355.background: A massive right-handed slugger who won a Gold Glove and hit 34 HR for St. Louis in 2021, Tyler O'Neill has been limited by injuries ever since but carries legitimate 30-HR upside when healthy. He signed with Baltimore hoping to recapture his power stroke in a strong lineup. The injury risk is real but the ceiling when locked in is a top-20 outfielder.
641355.recentAnalysis: O'Neill is off to a quiet start with .216/.279/.328 and 1 HR through 42 PA — well below his power potential. He may be working his way into form after injury time missed and the power should eventually appear.

641584.name: Jake Fraley
641584.background: A free agent outfielder in CBA with solid plate discipline and gap power, Jake Fraley has produced useful batting average and on-base numbers during his stints with Tampa Bay and Cincinnati — featuring a patient approach and the ability to hit to all fields. Available for any CBA team looking for outfield depth, he profiles as a .265-hitting OF with modest power and a disciplined approach that generates walks. His best value comes in on-base and run-scoring categories.
641584.recentAnalysis: Fraley has had a modest early showing: .227/.292/.386 with 1 HR, 2 RBI, and 2 SB in 48 PA across 15 games — limited playing time and modest counting stats so far in 2026.

641778.name: Eric Lauer
641778.background: A free agent starting pitcher in CBA with useful upside, Eric Lauer is a crafty left-hander whose command and secondary stuff have made him a reliable mid-rotation option during his Milwaukee and New York stints. Available for any CBA team to claim, he provides a solid floor as an innings-eater with modest K upside and good WHIP potential. He projects best as a rotation piece for a team looking to add depth rather than a frontline ace, but his consistency gives him value as a streaming or back-end option.
641778.recentAnalysis: Lauer has had a tough early stretch: 4 appearances (3 GS), 17.2 IP, 7.13 ERA, 1.47 WHIP — though 16 K shows the stuff is there and the ERA may be running hotter than his underlying numbers suggest.

641816.name: Tyler Mahle
641816.background: A free agent starting pitcher in CBA with high strikeout upside, Tyler Mahle features a premium four-seamer and excellent slider that have produced some of the best K/9 rates in the NL when he's healthy. His career has been defined by persistent injuries but the stuff has never been in question — a healthy Mahle is a genuine fantasy SP2. Available for any CBA team to claim, he profiles as a high-variance, high-ceiling add for teams willing to gamble on his health.
641816.recentAnalysis: Mahle has had a rough start: 4 GS, 18.2 IP, 7.23 ERA, 1.93 WHIP — though 21 K shows the strikeout stuff is fully intact and the ERA may be running hotter than his underlying skills suggest.

641941.name: Emilio Pagan
641941.background: The closer for the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets and a veteran save accumulator, Emilio Pagan has built a late-career closer role with a deceptive delivery and plus slider that generate strikeouts in the ninth inning despite below-elite velocity. The Ferrets have relied on his save production as a consistent CBA advantage, and his ability to convert leads in high-pressure situations has proven more reliable than his modest stuff might suggest. Ft. Meade's bullpen strategy is built around his closer role.
641941.recentAnalysis: Pagan has been solid for the Ferrets: 10 G, 10.1 IP, 4.35 ERA, 1.16 WHIP, 10 K, 6 SV — the ERA is a touch elevated but 6 saves is a meaningful early contribution to Ft. Meade's weekly totals.

642201.name: Eli White
642201.background: A free agent utility outfielder in CBA with speed and positional flexibility, Eli White has contributed as a bench piece and occasional starter with Texas and Chicago — bringing stolen base ability and multi-position coverage to any roster. Available for any CBA team looking for outfield depth with speed upside, he profiles as a .250-hitting utility OF with 15-SB potential when given regular opportunities.
642201.recentAnalysis: White has had extremely limited action in 2026 — just 21 PA across 9 games (.158 AVG) — and is not generating meaningful fantasy production in a reserve role.

642207.name: Devin Williams
642207.background: The closer for the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets and the owner of one of the most unhittable pitches in baseball, Devin Williams' 'Airbender' changeup generates the highest whiff rate of any pitch thrown regularly in the majors — a sinker-screwball hybrid that seemingly defies physics. Injuries have created some drama in his closer timeline, but the Ferrets have committed to him as their primary save source knowing the ceiling is historically dominant. When healthy, he's the most swing-and-miss closer in all of CBA.
642207.recentAnalysis: Williams has had a rough early stretch for the Ferrets: 6 G, 5.1 IP, 6.75 ERA, 2.06 WHIP, 8 K, 2 SV — the ratios are poor but the strikeouts are still coming, and a small sample may be masking his true level.

642397.name: Gregory Soto
642397.background: A hard-throwing setup reliever for the Bristol Banshees, Gregory Soto features triple-digit velocity and a slider that generates strikeouts in the highest-leverage situations for Philadelphia's bullpen. The Banshees have used him to accumulate holds and bolster their late-game depth, and his pure stuff makes him one of the more dangerous arms they carry. Bristol has built one of the most formidable late-game combinations in CBA with Soto, Chapman, and Fairbanks.
642397.recentAnalysis: Soto has been dominant for the Banshees: 12 G, 11.2 IP, 0.77 ERA, 0.69 WHIP, 16 K, 5 HD — one of the best early relief performances in the league with elite ratios and a huge holds contribution.

642547.name: Freddy Peralta
642547.background: Freddy Peralta is one of the most electrifying strikeout artists in baseball, a compact right-hander for the Brewers who generates elite whiff rates with a high-spin fastball and devastating changeup that confounds hitters. The Ghent Whistlepigs have him as a key piece of their rotation alongside Woodruff and Sheehan, and when Peralta is dealing he can put up dominant strikeout totals that carry an entire fantasy pitching staff. His raw strikeout ceiling is among the highest of any SP in the CBA.
642547.recentAnalysis: Peralta has a 4.05 ERA with 28 K over 26.2 innings through 5 starts — the strikeouts are there for the Whistlepigs, with the ERA expected to improve as his command sharpens.

642701.name: Dennis Santana
642701.background: A setup arm and multi-role reliever for the Portland Chinook, Dennis Santana has built a career on elite velocity and plus secondary stuff that makes him a useful high-leverage option in multiple bullpen configurations. The Chinook have used him to bridge to their closer while accumulating holds and occasional saves, and his ability to miss bats with a plus slider gives him consistent strikeout value. Portland values his reliability and rubber-arm availability across the grueling CBA regular season.
642701.recentAnalysis: Santana has been excellent for the Chinook: 11 G, 11.0 IP, 0.82 ERA, 0.82 WHIP, 7 K, 2 SV, 2 HD — outstanding ratios and multi-category relief contributions in the early going.

642708.name: Amed Rosario
642708.background: A free agent shortstop and outfielder in CBA with solid multi-position value, Amed Rosario has been a consistent contact hitter with developing power throughout his career — posting useful batting averages and RBI contributions as an everyday player for Cleveland and the Dodgers. Available for any CBA team looking to add middle infield depth, he profiles as a .265-hitting utility player with pop and enough positional flexibility to cover multiple spots. His recent power surge makes him an intriguing add.
642708.recentAnalysis: Rosario has been solid in limited action: .262/.283/.571 (0.852 OPS) with 3 HR and 9 RBI in 46 PA across 15 games — unexpectedly strong power production makes him an appealing CBA free agent pickup.

642715.name: Willy Adames
642715.background: A power-hitting shortstop and one of the West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls' middle-infield anchors, Willy Adames has consistently produced 20-plus homers from the position across Tampa Bay, Milwaukee, and now San Francisco. His willingness to walk combined with 25-homer potential makes him one of the better offensive shortstops in the NL, and the Pepperoni Rolls have benefited from his reliable production at a position of strength. His new Giants ballpark has proven home run-friendly for right-handed hitters.
642715.recentAnalysis: Adames has gotten off to a nice start with San Francisco: .269/.303/.493 (0.834 OPS) with 3 HR, 6 RBI, and 10 runs through 20 games — the power is showing up and counting stats should build as the season progresses.

643217.name: Andrew Benintendi
643217.background: A free agent outfielder in CBA with strong contact skills, Andrew Benintendi has been a consistent .280-plus hitter throughout his career with excellent gap power and professional at-bats in multiple AL lineups. Available for any CBA team looking for a reliable contact bat, he profiles as a .275-hitting OF with 15-HR upside and consistent on-base production. His contact approach and durability make him a safe floor addition.
643217.recentAnalysis: Benintendi has had a slow start: .200/.277/.369 with 1 HR and 6 RBI through 19 games — 23 strikeouts in 65 PA is atypically high for a player known for elite contact skills.

643289.name: Mauricio Dubon
643289.background: A reliable utility contributor for the North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms, Mauricio Dubon has built a long career on consistent contact, multi-position eligibility, and smart baserunning that helps teams in counting stats without being a liability anywhere. He plays outfield and infield equally well and brings solid batting average to the Fuzzy Bottoms' lineup from a spot where many teams play defensive liabilities. North Georgia values his flexibility and reliability as a veteran contributor who keeps their roster construction options open.
643289.recentAnalysis: Dubon has been one of North Georgia's bright spots early: .315/.346/.493 with 2 HR, 14 RBI, and 11 runs through 20 games — solid contact and counting stat production from a player in an everyday lineup role.

643446.name: Jeff McNeil
643446.background: A veteran contact bat for the Ghent Whistlepigs, Jeff McNeil is a former NL batting champion who brings one of the best pure contact profiles in the NL with a slap-and-gap approach and exceptional strikeout avoidance. His .326 batting title season in 2022 showcased his ability to hit for elite average across a full season, and while his power upside is limited, his batting average contribution is genuinely reliable. The Whistlepigs rely on him to hold up their team batting average and keep their strikeouts manageable.
643446.recentAnalysis: McNeil has had a quiet early stretch for Ghent: .266/.349/.342 with 0 HR and 4 RBI through 20 games — the contact skills are there (only 11 K in 73 PA) but the counting stats haven't materialized yet.

645261.name: Sandy Alcantara
645261.background: The workhorse ace of the Syracuse Sky Chiefs rotation and a former NL Cy Young winner, Sandy Alcantara is known for throwing the most innings of any pitcher in baseball — routinely completing 7-8 innings on elite pitch efficiency. Tommy John surgery cost him 2024-25, but the Sky Chiefs held his rights knowing that when healthy, his combination of innings, groundballs, and elite durability is unmatched in CBA. Syracuse needs him to be their anchor, and his return to the rotation has been one of their most important storylines.
645261.recentAnalysis: Alcantara has been outstanding for the Sky Chiefs in his return: 5 GS, 35.1 IP, 3.06 ERA, 1.05 WHIP, 23 K — the elite innings total (35.1 in 5 starts!) is exactly the durability that made him a Cy Young winner and a cornerstone for Syracuse.

645277.name: Ozzie Albies
645277.background: Ozzie Albies is a dynamic second baseman for the Braves who combines elite athleticism with surprising power, consistently posting 25+ home runs while adding stolen base contributions from the top of Atlanta's lineup. The Portland Chinook have him as a key piece of their infield alongside Paredes and Turner, counting on his power-speed combo to produce multi-category value week after week. Albies' combination of speed, pop, and a Gold Glove-caliber glove make him one of the most exciting second basemen in fantasy baseball.
645277.recentAnalysis: Albies has a .280 AVG, .784 OPS, 4 HR, and 9 RBI through 21 games — a productive start that has the Chinook's infield producing across the board.

646240.name: Rafael Devers
646240.background: Rafael Devers is one of the premier left-handed power hitters in baseball, a third baseman for the Red Sox who can hit 30+ home runs while maintaining a .270 average and driving in 100-plus runs in a full healthy season. The Delmarva Emus have him rounding out a loaded lineup alongside Ohtani and Seager, giving them a power threat from the left side to complement their other premium bats. When Devers is right, the Emus have one of the most dangerous offenses in the CBA.
646240.recentAnalysis: Devers has a .225 AVG and .581 OPS through 22 games for Boston — a slow start that the Emus are chalking up to early-season rust from a power hitter with a long track record of production.

647304.name: Josh Naylor
647304.background: Josh Naylor is a beloved first baseman for the Guardians who has made himself into one of baseball's most underrated sluggers, a left-handed power bat with elite on-base skills and the kind of clubhouse energy that drives winning. The North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms kept him as a core piece of their lineup alongside Chris Sale and Andy Pages, banking on his 30-HR upside and patient approach to produce big run totals. Naylor's gritty approach makes him a fan favorite and a dependable fantasy asset.
647304.recentAnalysis: Naylor is off to a very slow start with a .146 AVG and .451 OPS through 21 games — the Fuzzy Bottoms will need him to heat up quickly to stay competitive.

650333.name: Jorge Mateo
650333.background: A speedster utility player for Baltimore with legitimate 30+ SB ability, Jorge Mateo is the kind of player whose stolen base volume alone can make him fantasy-relevant even with a low batting average. His bat has never been reliable but he can be a useful speed specialist in deep formats when playing regularly.
650333.recentAnalysis: Mateo is off to a nice start with .286/.333/.429 and 1 HR and 2 SB through 30 PA — hitting well above his usual average, though the sample is small and regression is likely. The speed is the asset to watch.

650402.name: Gleyber Torres
650402.background: Gleyber Torres is a middle infielder now with the Tigers who has posted multiple 25-HR seasons with the Yankees and brings consistent power and contact from the second base position. The Ghent Whistlepigs have him as a lineup complement alongside Hoerner and Soderstrom, counting on his power from the middle infield and his ability to produce in a loaded Tigers lineup. Torres' consistent extra-base hit production gives the Whistlepigs a reliable run-producing option at second.
650402.recentAnalysis: Torres has a .234 AVG and .647 OPS through 21 games for Detroit — a modest start for the Whistlepigs' veteran second baseman, with more power expected as the season warms up.

650490.name: Yandy Diaz
650490.background: Yandy Diaz is one of baseball's most disciplined and contact-oriented hitters, a first baseman for the Rays whose remarkably low strikeout rate and consistently high batting average give him one of the safest fantasy floors of any first base option in the game. The Manhattan Mega Rats have him as a lineup complement to Acuna and Caminero, using his on-base skills to set the table for their power hitters and keep the offense moving. Diaz's elite contact and walk rate make him a floor-raising keeper for the Mega Rats.
650490.recentAnalysis: Diaz is off to a hot start with a .337 AVG, .933 OPS, 3 HR, and 16 RBI through 21 games — the Mega Rats are getting more than expected from their contact-first first baseman.

650556.name: Bryan Abreu
650556.background: A high-leverage setup arm for the Sugar Land Space Cowboys, Bryan Abreu has carved out an indispensable role in Houston's bullpen with a mid-to-upper-90s fastball and a slider that generates elite swing-and-miss rates. The Space Cowboys rely on his ability to bridge from the starter to the closer in high-stakes situations, and his holds and occasional save opportunities contribute meaningfully to their weekly CBA totals. He's the kind of reliable power reliever that championship CBA rosters are built around.
650556.recentAnalysis: Abreu has had a rough start for the Space Cowboys: 9 G, 7.1 IP, 14.73 ERA, 3.00 WHIP — a brutal stretch that obscures the 12 K and 1 SV; the ratios will need to improve dramatically for him to fulfill his expected role.

650633.name: Michael King
650633.background: A free agent starting pitcher with strong upside available in the CBA, Michael King converted from one of the Yankees' best relievers into a quality starter for San Diego — immediately posting sub-3.00 ERA figures with excellent command and a diverse pitch mix. Available to any CBA team looking to add a reliable mid-rotation arm, he represents one of the best starting pitcher options in the free agent pool with a high floor and genuine SP2 upside. Any manager who adds him immediately strengthens their rotation.
650633.recentAnalysis: King has been solid in 2026: 5 GS, 23.2 IP, 2.66 ERA, 1.18 WHIP, 21 K — strong early rotation results that confirm his value as one of the better free agent pitching additions available in CBA.

650911.name: Cristopher Sanchez
650911.background: Cristopher Sanchez is a quietly elite left-handed starter for the Phillies who has emerged as one of the best pitchers in the NL, combining a low-90s sinker with excellent deception and pinpoint command to generate soft contact and miss bats. The Bristol Banshees added him to one of the deepest pitching staffs in the CBA, and when Sanchez is in a groove he can carry a matchup against any lineup. His durability and innings-eating ability are especially valuable in a 6-SP format.
650911.recentAnalysis: Sanchez has been dominant with a 1.59 ERA, 39 K, and 1.41 WHIP over 28.1 innings across 5 starts — a brilliant start that has the Banshees in great shape.

656302.name: Dylan Cease
656302.background: The ace of the Manhattan Mega Rats rotation and one of the most electric starters in the AL, Dylan Cease combines a triple-digit fastball with one of the best sliders in baseball to generate elite strikeout rates and regularly post double-digit K outings. His San Diego tenure has made him a perennial top-15 SP in all fantasy formats, and the Mega Rats secured him knowing he provides the kind of ceiling arm that wins CBA championships. When Cease is on, no lineup in the league is comfortable facing him.
656302.recentAnalysis: Cease has been dominant for the Mega Rats despite 0 wins: 4 GS, 20.2 IP, 1.74 ERA, 1.26 WHIP, 32 K — a 13.9 K/9 is elite by any measure and he's been one of the best pitchers in early 2026.

656305.name: Nathaniel Lowe
656305.background: A left-handed first baseman for Texas known for excellent contact, walk rates, and an analytical approach at the plate, Nathaniel Lowe offers high batting average and on-base percentage with 20-plus HR potential in the strong Rangers lineup. He's never quite lived up to his lofty projections but is a reliable producer who avoids the batting average damage that hurts many power hitters.
656305.recentAnalysis: Lowe is off to a hot start with .256/.458/.482 and 4 HR through 48 PA — the walk rate has been exceptional and he's producing well in the heart of the Texas lineup.

656464.name: Kevin Ginkel
656464.background: A veteran setup reliever for the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets, Kevin Ginkel has carved out a consistent role in Arizona's bullpen with a power sinker and reliable command that generates groundballs and keeps high-leverage runners from scoring. The Ferrets have used him as a flexible bullpen piece who can contribute in multiple leverage situations, and his holds production has been a meaningful part of Ft. Meade's weekly strategy. He's the kind of unsung bullpen workhorse that helps CBA teams stay competitive in relief categories.
656464.recentAnalysis: Ginkel has had a mixed start for the Ferrets: 9 G, 8.2 IP, 5.19 ERA, 1.50 WHIP, 12 K, 2 HD — the strikeouts are there but the ERA reflects some hard contact in the early going.

656546.name: Jeff Hoffman
656546.background: A power reliever in the Sugar Land Space Cowboys bullpen, Jeff Hoffman transformed himself from a struggling starter into one of the more dominant setup arms in the NL — featuring a plus fastball and slider that generate strikeout rates among the highest of any reliever in Philadelphia's bullpen. The Space Cowboys acquired his rights to add elite relief depth, and his combination of K upside and high-leverage opportunities in Houston's win-driven bullpen gives him meaningful CBA value. He's a key piece of Sugar Land's championship-caliber staff.
656546.recentAnalysis: Hoffman has not yet appeared in 2026 — Sugar Land is awaiting his availability as the season gets underway.

656605.name: Mitch Keller
656605.background: A rotation anchor for the Portland Chinook, Mitch Keller has developed into one of the more reliable starting pitchers in the NL after spending his early years working through mechanical issues and inconsistency. His improved sinker usage and better command have transformed him into a solid fantasy SP who eats innings, limits walks, and posts ERA figures well below 4.00. Portland has relied on his durability and steadiness to provide the rotation depth that gives them flexibility to compete in CBA week after week.
656605.recentAnalysis: Keller has been solid for the Chinook: 5 GS, 29.0 IP, 2.79 ERA, 1.10 WHIP, 19 K — a strong innings total with good ratios, though the modest strikeout rate (5.9 K/9) reflects his groundball-first approach.

656716.name: Zach McKinstry
656716.background: A free agent utility player in CBA with multi-position eligibility, Zach McKinstry has carved out a bench role with his ability to play outfield, second base, and third base while posting decent contact numbers. Available for any CBA team in need of positional flexibility, he profiles as a .265-hitting utility bat whose roster flexibility is his primary value. A safe but unexciting depth piece.
656716.recentAnalysis: McKinstry has had modest production in limited action: .209/.261/.302 with 1 HR and 5 RBI across 14 games — filling a utility role without generating standout fantasy numbers.

656730.name: Trevor Megill
656730.background: The closer for the Portland Chinook and one of their most important bullpen pieces, Trevor Megill is a power reliever who has worked his way into a save situation in a major league bullpen through elite strikeout rates and a fastball that plays up late in games. The Chinook have relied on his save production to stay competitive in the bullpen categories on a weekly basis. His ERA can be volatile but his strikeout-to-walk rates give him a legitimate path to double-digit saves in a full season.
656730.recentAnalysis: Megill has had a very rough start for the Chinook: 8 G, 7.0 IP, 10.29 ERA, 1.86 WHIP, but 4 SV — he's been hit hard but has still managed to convert saves, which is the primary contribution Portland needs from him.

656775.name: Cedric Mullins
656775.background: A free agent outfielder in CBA and former 30-30 performer for Baltimore, Cedric Mullins has been one of the most exciting speed-power combinations in the AL when healthy — delivering elite stolen base totals alongside 25-homer pop from the leadoff spot. Available for any CBA team to claim, a fully healthy Mullins is a genuine difference-maker with five-category upside. His injury history is the main concern but the ceiling remains top-20 outfielder.
656775.recentAnalysis: Mullins has had a very difficult start: .155/.177/.239 with 2 HR, 8 RBI, and 3 SB through 19 games — one of the roughest early stretches of his career and far below his established performance level.

656811.name: Ryan O'Hearn
656811.background: Ryan O'Hearn is a left-handed first baseman for the Orioles who has developed into a reliable run-producer with solid power and an improving batting average, giving Baltimore a veteran presence at first base who quietly contributes across multiple categories. The Bristol Banshees have him as a lineup piece alongside their power hitters, counting on his consistent contact and extra-base hit production to round out their already deep offensive core. O'Hearn's improving walk rate and power make him a reliable contributor for the Banshees.
656811.recentAnalysis: O'Hearn is off to a hot start with a .329 AVG, .960 OPS, 4 HR, and 15 RBI through 20 games — a pleasant surprise that has the Banshees very happy with their first baseman.

656876.name: Drew Rasmussen
656876.background: A high-upside rotation piece for the Ghent Whistlepigs, Drew Rasmussen has shown the raw stuff to be a dominant starter — featuring a mid-90s sinker that generates elite groundball rates and a slider that misses bats at a strong clip. He's been limited by Tommy John surgery but when fully healthy he profiles as a frontline arm who could dramatically elevate the Whistlepigs' pitching staff. Ghent has been patient through the rehabilitation knowing what he's capable of when locked in.
656876.recentAnalysis: Rasmussen has been outstanding in his return for Ghent: 4 GS, 19.2 IP, 2.75 ERA, 0.66 WHIP, 20 K — the stuff looks healthy and the elite contact-suppression that defines his best outings is fully present.

656941.name: Kyle Schwarber
