656941.background: Kyle Schwarber is one of the most dangerous pure power hitters in baseball, a left-handed slugger for the Phillies whose legendary launch angle and elite chase avoidance make him a perennial 40-HR threat, even if his batting average sits below .240. The Manhattan Mega Rats have him in their lineup as a cornerstone power bat alongside Acuna and Caminero, giving them a fearsome middle-of-the-order presence that opposing pitchers dread. Schwarber's home run and walk totals consistently carry fantasy lineups.
656941.recentAnalysis: Schwarber has 6 HR, 12 RBI, and an .871 OPS through 20 games — the power is producing nicely for the Mega Rats in the early going.

657277.name: Logan Webb
657277.background: Logan Webb is a crafty right-hander for the Giants who has quietly established himself as one of the best starters in the NL, relying on an elite sinker and precise command to generate ground balls and miss bats without premium velocity. The Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets have him as their staff ace alongside Tyler Glasnow, giving them a rotation with both a power arm and a ground-ball artist who can dominate in any ballpark. Webb's durability and innings total make him especially valuable in CBA's deep SP format.
657277.recentAnalysis: Webb has a 5.40 ERA through 5 starts over 30 IP — a rough early stretch for the Folksy Ferrets, who are expecting Webb's elite contact management to right the ship.

657656.name: Ramon Laureano
657656.background: Ramon Laureano is a versatile, contact-hitting outfielder with a cannon arm and surprising extra-base pop, a player whose contributions across multiple categories quietly add up to excellent fantasy production. The Delmarva Emus have him filling a valuable lineup role alongside Ohtani, Seager, and Marte, counting on his across-the-board production to round out their already deep offense. Laureano's ability to steal bases and drive in runs makes him one of the Emus' most underappreciated contributors.
657656.recentAnalysis: Laureano is hitting .280 with 4 HR, 15 RBI, and 3 SB through 20 games — productive early numbers that have the Emus humming offensively.

657746.name: Joe Ryan
657746.background: Joe Ryan is a crafty right-hander for the Twins who generates elite strikeout totals through a unique combination of an elevated fastball and a devastating splitter that keeps hitters off balance at every count. The Delmarva Emus have him as a key piece of their rotation alongside Framber Valdez, relying on his consistently excellent ratios and strikeout upside to anchor their pitching staff. Ryan's ability to miss bats while limiting walks makes him one of the more reliable SP options in the CBA.
657746.recentAnalysis: Ryan has a 3.29 ERA, 28 K, and 0.88 WHIP over 27.1 innings through 5 starts — a strong early run that has the Emus' rotation looking very solid.

657757.name: Gavin Sheets
657757.background: A left-handed power bat on the Manhattan Mega Rats roster, Gavin Sheets provides a patient approach and platoon-friendly profile that generates solid home run production against right-handed pitching. His ability to drive the ball into the gaps and draw walks makes him a useful lineup piece for the Mega Rats, and his first base/DH eligibility gives them flexibility in constructing their roster. Manhattan has used him as a reliable counting-stat contributor who won't hurt them in walks.
657757.recentAnalysis: Sheets has had a modest start for the Mega Rats: .238/.273/.381 with 2 HR, 6 RBI, and 8 runs through 19 games — respectable contributions but the walk rate and power have been below his usual profile so far.

660271.name: Shohei Ohtani
660271.background: Shohei Ohtani is the most unique player in baseball history, a true two-way superstar who dominated as both an elite starter and power hitter before joining the Dodgers on a record contract. The Delmarva Emus landed him as the crown jewel of their roster, giving them a weapon at the plate unlike anything else in the CBA. His fantasy value as a pure hitter is extraordinary — the combination of power, on-base skills, and run production puts him in a category by himself.
660271.recentAnalysis: Through 21 games for the Dodgers, Ohtani is slashing .267/.901 OPS with 5 HR and 11 RBI — a solid early pace that should accelerate as the season deepens.

660670.name: Ronald Acuna Jr.
660670.background: Ronald Acuna Jr. is a generational talent for the Braves who won the 2023 NL MVP before a torn ACL cut his 2024 short — he came back healthy in 2025 and is chasing another historic season. The Manhattan Mega Rats landed him as a centerpiece of their offense, and when Acuna is locked in, no player in the CBA produces across more categories. His speed, power, and batting average make him the prototype for fantasy dominance.
660670.recentAnalysis: Acuna is hitting .247 with 1 HR, 5 RBI, and 4 SB through 21 games — the power has been slow to develop but his on-base skills and legs keep him relevant for the Mega Rats.

660821.name: Jesus Sanchez
660821.background: A free agent outfielder in CBA with solid power and contact balance, Jesus Sanchez has shown the ability to hit for both average and power during his Marlins tenure — posting useful batting averages with 20-homer upside in a hitter's park. Available for any CBA team looking for outfield depth, he profiles as a .275-hitting OF with 18-HR upside who contributes across multiple categories without being a liability anywhere.
660821.recentAnalysis: Sanchez has had a decent start: .270/.299/.419 with 2 HR and 11 RBI through 20 games — solid contact and counting stat contributions that make him a useful CBA free agent pickup.

660844.name: Leo Rivas
660844.background: A free agent utility infielder in CBA with patience at the plate, Leo Rivas has shown solid on-base skills and positional versatility across multiple infield spots during his MLB opportunities. Available for any CBA team looking for multi-position depth, he profiles as a .250-hitting utility bat who draws walks and contributes in runs when given playing time. His floor is modest but his positional flexibility gives him real roster value.
660844.recentAnalysis: Rivas has had a tough start: .149/.276/.204 with 0 HR and 4 RBI through 17 games — the walk rate is present (10 BB) but the contact and power have been very poor in the early going.

661388.name: William Contreras
661388.background: The starting catcher for the Ghent Whistlepigs and one of their more consistent offensive contributors, William Contreras has established himself as a legitimate offensive catcher with a strong arm and above-average hit tool. His ability to maintain a .290-plus average with 20-homer upside makes him one of the better CBA catchers, and Ghent benefits enormously from a backstop who actively helps in batting average and on-base rate rather than being a liability. He and Matt Chapman give Whistlepigs one of the better veteran cores in the league.
661388.recentAnalysis: Contreras has been excellent for the Whistlepigs early: .306/.398/.444 (0.870 OPS) with 2 HR, 11 RBI, and 11 walks through 18 games — a great start combining average, on-base, and counting stat contributions.

661395.name: Jhoan Duran
661395.background: The closer for the West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls and one of the most fearsome relievers in baseball, Jhoan Duran features a 102-mph sinker and a plus curveball that generate elite groundball rates and strikeout numbers in the highest-leverage situations. The Pepperoni Rolls have one of the best save situations in CBA, and Duran's ability to convert in the ninth inning has been a major competitive advantage. His combination of saves and dominant ratios makes him one of the most valuable relievers in the entire league.
661395.recentAnalysis: Duran has been excellent for the Pepperoni Rolls: 7 G, 6.2 IP, 1.35 ERA, 0.75 WHIP, 8 K, 5 SV — dominant ratios and save conversions are exactly what West Virginia needs from their elite closer.

662139.name: Daulton Varsho
662139.background: One of the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets' most versatile weapons, Daulton Varsho holds both catcher and outfield eligibility while contributing 20-plus home runs and solid stolen base numbers. His ability to play multiple positions while maintaining fantasy-relevant production makes him exceptionally valuable in CBA's positional structure, and the Ferrets have leveraged his dual eligibility to stay flexible on roster construction. Toronto's lineup provides solid run-scoring opportunities to back up his solid bat.
662139.recentAnalysis: Varsho has made a solid early showing: .262/.354/.433 with 3 HR, 7 RBI, and 1 SB through 19 games — steady multi-category contributions from one of fantasy's most flexible positional assets.

662253.name: Andres Munoz
662253.background: The closer for the Ghent Whistlepigs and one of the most electric relievers in the AL, Andres Munoz combines a 100-mph fastball with an elite slider to generate dominant swing-and-miss rates as Seattle's primary save man. The Whistlepigs have leaned on his save totals as a major advantage in weekly CBA matchups, and his pure stuff makes him one of the most reliable closers in the game when healthy. Ghent built their bullpen strategy around having one of the best save opportunities in baseball.
662253.recentAnalysis: Munoz has had an uncharacteristically rough start for Ghent: 8 G, 7.0 IP, 9.00 ERA, 1.86 WHIP, 11 K, 2 SV — the strikeout rate remains elite but the runs have been coming in at an alarming rate in the early going.

663158.name: Robert Suarez
663158.background: The closer for the West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls and one of the more reliable save sources in the NL, Robert Suarez features a power sinker that generates elite groundball rates and keeps the ball in the park even in big moments. His late-blooming career has produced multiple consecutive seasons of double-digit saves for San Diego, and the Pepperoni Rolls have counted on his consistency in the ninth inning to win CBA weeks in the saves and holds categories. He's a dependable, professional presence in one of the best bullpen environments in the NL.
663158.recentAnalysis: Suarez has been excellent for the Pepperoni Rolls: 9 G, 8.2 IP, 1.04 ERA, 0.92 WHIP, 10 K, 1 SV, 3 HD — elite run prevention across multiple roles in San Diego's bullpen.

663330.name: Jahmai Jones
663330.background: A versatile utility free agent in the CBA, Jahmai Jones has carved out a bench role in the majors with his ability to play multiple infield and outfield positions and provide pop-up at-bats with decent athleticism. Available for any CBA team in need of roster flexibility, he profiles best as a multi-position eligibility contributor who can cover multiple lineup slots in an emergency. He's a depth piece rather than a featured contributor.
663330.recentAnalysis: Jones has seen very limited action in 2026 with just 17 PA across 9 games (.176 AVG, 0 HR) — he's on the fringes of the roster and not generating meaningful fantasy production at this point.

663460.name: Kris Bubic
663460.background: Kris Bubic is a left-handed starter for the Royals who has reinvented himself as an effective major leaguer through improved command and a deceptive delivery, relying on movement and location rather than pure velocity to miss bats and generate weak contact. The Bristol Banshees added him as a rotation depth piece alongside Skubal and Sanchez, giving them a durable innings-eater who can hold his own in most matchups. Bubic's strikeout rate and improving walk totals make him a useful SP in deeper fantasy formats.
663460.recentAnalysis: Bubic has a 3.97 ERA with 26 K over 22.2 innings through 4 starts — a solid early run for the Banshees' back-end starter.

663538.name: Nico Hoerner
663538.background: Nico Hoerner is one of the most dependable middle infielders in baseball, a contact-first second baseman for the Cubs who pairs elite bat-to-ball skills with sneaky stolen base totals and excellent positional versatility. The Ghent Whistlepigs kept him as a cornerstone of their infield, trusting his consistent .280+ averages and double-digit steal pace to produce reliable counting stats week over week. Hoerner's low strikeout rate and high floor make him the definition of a steady keeper for the Whistlepigs.
663538.recentAnalysis: Hoerner is delivering in a big way with a .325 AVG, .924 OPS, 3 HR, 20 RBI, and 7 SB through 21 games — one of the best values in the CBA so far in 2026.

663556.name: Shane McClanahan
663556.background: Shane McClanahan is a hard-throwing left-hander for the Rays who has quickly established himself as one of the game's best starters when healthy, generating elite strikeout totals with a dominant fastball-slider combination. The West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls have him anchoring their rotation alongside Andrew Abbott, and when McClanahan is on the mound he gives the Pepperoni Rolls a genuine SP1 capable of shutting down any lineup. His strikeout ability and ground-ball tendencies make him a fantasy ace.
663556.recentAnalysis: McClanahan has a 5.00 ERA over 18 innings through 4 starts — an uncharacteristically rough early run for the Pepperoni Rolls, though his stuff remains elite.

663574.name: Tony Santillan
663574.background: A dominant high-leverage reliever for the North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms, Tony Santillan has transformed himself into one of the most effective setup men in the NL with a power sinker-slider combination that generates elite groundball rates and strikeouts in the toughest situations. The Fuzzy Bottoms have leaned on his holds production as a meaningful CBA advantage, and his ability to shut down high-leverage innings without allowing inherited runners to score has made him indispensable to North Georgia's weekly pitching plan.
663574.recentAnalysis: Santillan has been dominant for the Fuzzy Bottoms: 10 G, 10.0 IP, 0.00 ERA, 0.90 WHIP, 12 K, 1 SV, 7 HD — a scoreless start with a massive holds total is exactly the kind of elite relief contribution North Georgia built their bullpen around.

663586.name: Austin Riley
663586.background: Austin Riley is a power-hitting third baseman for the Braves who has established himself as one of the most dangerous right-handed power hitters in the NL, consistently slugging 30+ home runs while providing solid run production and an above-average batting average. The Bristol Banshees have him as a lineup piece alongside Henderson and Carroll, counting on his power ceiling and run-producing ability to add punch to a lineup that's already deep. Riley's strength and bat speed make him a matchup-winning force when he gets hot.
663586.recentAnalysis: Riley has 3 HR, 14 RBI, and a .739 OPS through 21 games for Atlanta — steady early production that the Banshees can rely on from their veteran third baseman.

663604.name: Brandon Lockridge
663604.background: A speed-first outfielder on the North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms roster, Brandon Lockridge brings elite base-stealing ability and a scrappy approach at the plate that makes him a specialist in the stolen base and run-scoring categories. The Fuzzy Bottoms have leaned on his speed to stay competitive in those categories, and his value is concentrated in situations where he gets on base and immediately becomes a threat on the basepaths. He won't hurt you in AVG if deployed correctly.
663604.recentAnalysis: Lockridge has contributed what the Fuzzy Bottoms need from him: 4 SB in 17 games with a .255 average and 5 runs through 58 PA — the speed is real even if the power profile (.627 OPS) is limited.

663616.name: Trevor Larnach
663616.background: A free agent corner outfielder in CBA with solid plate discipline, Trevor Larnach brings an excellent walk rate and developing power to Minnesota's lineup — featuring a patient approach that generates high OBP even when his batting average dips. Available for any CBA team looking for outfield depth with on-base skills, he profiles as a .265-hitting OF with 18-HR upside and excellent walk production. A useful batting average and walks contributor.
663616.recentAnalysis: Larnach has been patient and productive in his opportunities: .265/.500/.441 (0.880 OPS) with 1 HR, 7 RBI, and 13 walks through 16 games in 47 PA — extraordinary OBP driven by elite plate discipline.

663656.name: Kyle Tucker
663656.background: Kyle Tucker is one of the premier right-handed power hitters in baseball, a steady run-producer who put up elite numbers for the Astros before being traded to the Cubs, where he's continued his excellence as a lineup anchor. The Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets kept him as a valued keeper, counting on his consistent 30-HR, 90-RBI production to carry their middle of the lineup. Tucker's elite barrel rate and disciplined approach make him a model of reliability.
663656.recentAnalysis: Tucker has 3 HR, 13 RBI, and a .755 OPS through 21 games for the Cubs — a solid if not spectacular start for the Folksy Ferrets' anchor bat.

663697.name: Jonathan India
663697.background: A former NL Rookie of the Year on the North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms roster, Jonathan India arrived in the big leagues with an elite approach — drawing walks at a prodigious rate while making consistent contact — and immediately delivered 20-plus homers as Cincinnati's everyday second baseman. He's had injury and consistency issues since that debut, but the Fuzzy Bottoms have held his rights banking on a return to that patient, productive form. His combination of walks, power, and multi-position eligibility has real CBA value.
663697.recentAnalysis: India has had a difficult start for North Georgia: .167/.310/.361 (0.623 OPS) with 2 HR and 8 RBI through 17 games — the walks are showing up (8 BB) but the batting average is well below expectations in the early going.

663728.name: Cal Raleigh
663728.background: The power-hitting catcher for the Portland Chinook, Cal Raleigh set the MLB home run record for the position in 2022 and has repeatedly demonstrated plus raw power from behind the plate. His willingness to swing for the fences creates a massive strikeout profile, but the home run production makes him one of the few catchers who can anchor a lineup slot. Portland values him enormously as a catcher-eligible player with genuine 25-HR upside — a scarcity in CBA that's difficult to replace.
663728.recentAnalysis: Raleigh has had a rough start at the plate for the Chinook: .163/.275/.253 with 2 HR and 11 RBI through 21 games — 29 strikeouts in 91 PA is a brutal rate, and Portland is waiting for the power that defines his value to show up.

663743.name: Nick Fortes
663743.background: A backup catcher with solid bat-to-ball skills on the North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms roster, Nick Fortes has impressed with his contact-first approach and line-drive hitting that generates useful batting average from a traditionally weak offensive position. The Fuzzy Bottoms have used him as their starting catcher while Rutschman recovers, and his ability to maintain a .280-plus average while taking care of the position defensively has made him a useful placeholder. His strikeout avoidance and contact skills are genuinely elite for a catcher.
663743.recentAnalysis: Fortes has been productive for the Fuzzy Bottoms: .327/.356/.490 (0.828 OPS) with 1 HR, 7 RBI, and only 7 strikeouts in 59 PA — excellent contact production filling in admirably at catcher.

663757.name: Esteury Ruiz
663757.background: One of the most electrifying base stealers in baseball, Esteury Ruiz stole 67 bases in 2023 to set an Athletics franchise record. His batting average and on-base percentage are inconsistent, but his pure speed can make him one of the most valuable fantasy players alive in a strong stretch — the stolen base volume when running is extraordinary.
663757.recentAnalysis: Ruiz has just 2 PA in 2026 and is not currently in a regular role — his stolen base value is locked away until he secures consistent playing time.

663837.name: Matt Vierling
663837.background: A free agent utility player in CBA with solid all-around skills, Matt Vierling has built a reputation as a versatile contributor who can play multiple positions at a high level while posting decent offensive numbers. Available for any CBA team looking for multi-position roster flexibility, he profiles as a .255-hitting utility bat with 12-HR upside who contributes across the board without excelling in any single category.
663837.recentAnalysis: Vierling has had limited action: .233/.250/.367 with 1 HR and 4 RBI across 15 games in 32 PA — filling a utility role with modest early production.

663968.name: Jake Mangum
663968.background: A free agent outfielder in CBA with excellent speed and contact skills, Jake Mangum has shown the ability to maintain a high batting average and generate stolen base value throughout his professional career. Available for any CBA team looking for speed-first depth, he profiles as a .290-hitting outfielder with double-digit stolen base upside and excellent bat-to-ball skills that make him a useful high-average contributor.
663968.recentAnalysis: Mangum has been useful in limited playing time: .283/.340/.340 with 0 HR, 1 RBI, and 3 SB across 17 games — the speed is contributing and the contact is solid, though counting stats are limited by his part-time role.

664023.name: Ian Happ
664023.background: Ian Happ is a switch-hitting outfielder for the Cubs who has become one of the most dependable multi-category contributors in the NL, combining consistent power with a solid average and decent stolen base numbers from the outfield. The West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls have him as a reliable lineup piece alongside De La Cruz and Abbott, counting on his consistent 20-25 HR production and improving contact rate to deliver predictable value week over week. Happ's positional flexibility and consistency make him a smart keeper.
664023.recentAnalysis: Happ has 6 HR and an .885 OPS through 19 games for the Cubs — a strong early power display that has the Pepperoni Rolls very pleased with their veteran outfielder.

664040.name: Brandon Lowe
664040.background: Brandon Lowe is a second baseman for the Rays whose compact left-handed swing produces elite power numbers for the position, making him one of the most productive second basemen in fantasy baseball when his bat is clicking. The Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets have him as a middle infield power piece alongside Pena and Chisholm, giving their lineup a second baseman who can provide genuine home run production. Lowe's pop from the middle infield position is a premium commodity that the Folksy Ferrets are smart to hold.
664040.recentAnalysis: Lowe is off to a massive start with 7 HR, 18 RBI, and a .975 OPS through 19 games — the Folksy Ferrets are getting elite power production from their second baseman early in 2026.

664059.name: Sam Haggerty
664059.background: A free agent utility player in CBA whose best tool is pure speed, Sam Haggerty has carved out a bench role with his ability to steal bases and play multiple positions at an adequate level. Available for any CBA team specifically looking to boost stolen base totals in a tight week, he's a specialist rather than a featured contributor. His value is entirely concentrated in SB categories.
664059.recentAnalysis: Haggerty has seen very limited action in 2026 with just 13 PA across 10 games — not generating meaningful fantasy production in a backup role.

664126.name: Pete Fairbanks
664126.background: A high-leverage reliever and save contributor for the Bristol Banshees, Pete Fairbanks features a power fastball-splitter combination that makes him one of the more dangerous late-game options in Tampa Bay's bullpen. The Banshees have relied on his saves and holds to stay competitive in the bullpen categories, and his ability to miss bats late in games gives him genuine closer upside. Bristol has built a bullpen with multiple save contributors, and Fairbanks sits at the core of that strategy alongside Chapman and Strider.
664126.recentAnalysis: Fairbanks has had a rough start for Bristol: 6 G (including 1 spot start), 6.0 IP, 9.00 ERA, 1.33 WHIP, 8 K, 2 SV — the saves are coming but the ERA has been significantly elevated in the early going.

664285.name: Ty France
664285.background: A contact-first first baseman who had a breakout year with Seattle in 2021-2022, Ty France relies on his ability to spray line drives to all fields and draw walks. He lacks elite power for a first baseman but provides excellent batting average stability and is a useful OBP league asset. His production declined in his final Seattle seasons.
664285.recentAnalysis: France is off to a solid start with .265/.444/.529 and 3 HR through 36 PA — the walk rate is exceptional and the power has shown up early, making him a viable 1B option in daily formats.

664702.name: Myles Straw
664702.background: A free agent center fielder in CBA whose primary asset is elite speed, Myles Straw has built a career on his plus-plus wheels and exceptional defense in center field — generating stolen base totals and run-scoring value through his ability to reach base and create havoc on the basepaths. Available for any CBA team specifically looking to boost stolen base totals, he's one of the fastest players in the game and can deliver double-digit steals in limited playing time.
664702.recentAnalysis: Straw has been excellent in limited action: .391/.423/.565 (1.005 OPS) with 1 HR, 3 RBI across 16 games in 26 PA — surprising power alongside his usual speed in a very small sample.

664761.name: Alec Bohm
664761.background: A free agent third baseman in the CBA with significant power upside, Alec Bohm was once one of the Phillies' most highly regarded hitting prospects and has shown the ability to post .280-plus averages with 20-plus home runs in a full healthy season. Available to any CBA team looking to add third base depth or a power bat, he profiles as a .270-hitting corner infielder with 20-HR potential if he finds his swing. His track record with Philadelphia suggests he can return to that level of production.
664761.recentAnalysis: Bohm has had a very difficult start in 2026: .145/.197/.217 (0.414 OPS) with 1 HR and 10 RBI through 19 games — one of the coldest early stretches for any available free agent, though 10 RBI suggests at least some lineup opportunities.

664770.name: Nathan Lukes
664770.background: A free agent utility outfielder in CBA with solid contact skills, Nathan Lukes has made the most of his MLB opportunities with consistent line-drive contact and professional at-bats that produce useful batting average in part-time roles. Available for any CBA team looking for depth outfield production, he profiles as a .280-hitting bench piece who won't hurt you in any category and contributes modest counting stats when given regular opportunities. A safe, low-ceiling add for roster depth.
664770.recentAnalysis: Lukes has had modest production in limited action: .205/.220/.282 with 0 HR, 5 RBI across 16 games in 42 PA — not generating meaningful fantasy numbers in a part-time role so far in 2026.

664854.name: Ryan Helsley
664854.background: The closer for the Delmarva Emus and one of the most dominant relievers in the NL, Ryan Helsley combines a 100-plus-mph fastball with a filthy changeup to produce elite whiff rates and saves for St. Louis. The Emus have one of the best closer situations in all of CBA, and Helsley's ability to strand inherited runners while converting saves has been a major factor in Delmarva's competitive success. He and the Emus' offensive core make them one of the most complete rosters in the league.
664854.recentAnalysis: Helsley has been solid for the Emus: 9 G, 8.1 IP, 2.16 ERA, 1.44 WHIP, 12 K, 6 SV — 6 saves in the early going is a major contribution to Delmarva's weekly totals.

664983.name: Jake McCarthy
664983.background: A free agent outfielder in CBA whose best fantasy asset is speed, Jake McCarthy has built a career on stolen base ability and gap-to-gap contact that generates runs when given regular playing time. Available for any CBA team looking to boost their stolen base totals, he profiles as a .265-hitting outfielder with 20-SB upside who contributes in runs and steals categories. His best value comes when he's batting near the top of a lineup where his speed can be weaponized.
664983.recentAnalysis: McCarthy has had modest counting stats in his early opportunities: .200/.271/.275 with 0 HR, 3 RBI, and 5 SB across 18 games — the stolen base upside is showing up (5 SB already) even if the bat has been quiet.

665019.name: Kody Clemens
665019.background: A free agent utility player in CBA with solid athleticism and positional flexibility, Kody Clemens has carved out a bench role by playing multiple infield and outfield positions while contributing modestly with the bat. Available for any CBA team looking for multi-position coverage, he profiles as a .255-hitting utility bat with speed upside and enough versatility to cover multiple roster spots. A depth piece whose primary value is positional flexibility.
665019.recentAnalysis: Clemens has had limited opportunities in 2026: .158/.227/.263 with 2 HR and 3 SB in 44 PA across 13 games — modest counting stats in a part-time role with the speed contributing.

665161.name: Jeremy Pena
665161.background: Jeremy Pena is the World Series MVP shortstop for the Astros who stepped into Carlos Correa's shoes and immediately delivered at the highest level, combining elite defense with solid offensive production and clutch performances on the biggest stage. The Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets have him as their starting shortstop alongside Tucker and Chisholm, counting on his contact ability and defensive excellence to give them a premium middle infield presence. Pena's floor and baseball IQ make him a dependable everyday contributor.
665161.recentAnalysis: Pena is on the D10 IL after a 10-game start — the Folksy Ferrets are watching his recovery timeline and hoping he returns soon to stabilize their infield.

665487.name: Fernando Tatis Jr.
665487.background: One of the most electrifying free agents in the CBA and the most physically gifted shortstop in baseball, Fernando Tatis Jr. features 35-homer power, plus stolen base ability, and a highlight-reel athleticism that makes him must-watch at all times. Available to any CBA team willing to invest their trade capital, his upside is that of the single best player in the league when he's locked in and playing at full health. His EROSP projects him in the top five CBA-available players.
665487.recentAnalysis: Tatis has had a mixed early stretch: .250/.333/.319 (0.644 OPS) with 0 HR, 5 SB, and 12 RBI through 22 games — the steals and RBI are contributing but the power that makes him elite has been completely absent in the early going.

665489.name: Vladimir Guerrero Jr.
665489.background: The top power-hitting free agent in the CBA and one of the most dangerous right-handed hitters in baseball, Vladimir Guerrero Jr. is a perennial All-Star and MVP candidate whose combination of elite raw power, excellent contact, and RBI production makes him one of the most coveted first basemen in any fantasy format. Available to any CBA team willing to make a trade, he represents a franchise-altering pickup for a manager in need of a middle-of-the-lineup cornerstone. His EROSP puts him among the top five available players in the league.
665489.recentAnalysis: Guerrero has had a nice start: .320/.410/.413 with 1 HR, 10 RBI, and 12 walks through 21 games — elite OBP and batting average, though the power production is surprisingly quiet through the early going.

665742.name: Juan Soto
665742.background: Juan Soto is one of the most disciplined hitters in baseball, a perennial .400 OBP machine who has starred for Washington, San Diego, and the Yankees before landing with the Mets on a record-breaking contract. The Bristol Banshees are fortunate to have him in their lineup alongside Skubal and Carroll, giving them one of the most stacked rosters in the CBA. Soto's otherworldly walk rate ensures he produces even in cold stretches.
665742.recentAnalysis: Soto landed on the D10 IL after a blazing start (.355 AVG, .928 OPS, 1 HR in 8 games) — the Banshees will be watching his return timeline closely.

665795.name: Edward Cabrera
665795.background: A high-upside starting pitcher in the Syracuse Sky Chiefs rotation, Edward Cabrera features one of the most electric fastball-slider combinations in the Marlins' system — a mid-to-upper-90s heater paired with a wipeout breaking ball that generates elite strikeout rates when he's locked in. His career has been interrupted by injury and command inconsistency, but the Sky Chiefs held his rights knowing that when Cabrera is right he's a genuine fantasy ace. Syracuse is counting on him to deliver on that considerable potential.
665795.recentAnalysis: Cabrera has been solid for the Sky Chiefs: 4 GS, 22.2 IP, 2.38 ERA, 1.28 WHIP, 17 K — excellent run prevention in the early going, though the walk rate will need to improve to sustain these results.

665833.name: Oneil Cruz
665833.background: Oneil Cruz is one of the most physically imposing shortstops in baseball history, a 6-foot-7 speedster for the Pirates whose combination of extraordinary athleticism, plus power, and 80-grade speed make him a genuine 40-40 threat when healthy. The Sugar Land Space Cowboys have him as one of their most electric assets alongside Lindor and Rice, betting on his unique combination of tools to produce at a historically rare clip once he's fully healthy and producing. Cruz's potential when locked in is among the highest of any player in the CBA.
665833.recentAnalysis: Cruz is off to a stunning start with 6 HR, 19 RBI, and 9 SB with a .915 OPS through 22 games — the Space Cowboys are getting the elite multi-category performance they hoped for.

665862.name: Jazz Chisholm Jr.
665862.background: Jazz Chisholm Jr. is one of the most electrifying personalities and players in baseball, a freewheeling third baseman for the Yankees whose elite athleticism, power, and speed make him a fantasy force when he's healthy and producing. The Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets grabbed him as a high-ceiling keeper alongside Tucker and Bellinger, knowing that when Jazz is locked in he can carry their offense for entire stretches. His swagger and motor make him must-watch entertainment and a high-variance fantasy play.
665862.recentAnalysis: Chisholm is off to a frustrating start with a .164 AVG and .498 OPS through 21 games, despite 8 stolen bases — the legs are there but the Folksy Ferrets need the bat to come around.

665926.name: Andres Gimenez
665926.background: A versatile middle infield piece for the Bristol Banshees, Andres Gimenez's breakout season with Cleveland established him as one of the best defensive second basemen in the AL while also showcasing offensive skills that surprised many — elite contact, useful power, and surprising stolen base ability. His ability to contribute in multiple offensive categories while playing premium defense has made him a valuable fantasy asset, and Bristol has used his multi-category upside to stay competitive across the board.
665926.recentAnalysis: Gimenez has been solid early for the Banshees: .290/.338/.484 with 3 HR, 11 RBI, and 4 SB through 19 games — solid across-the-board production with the stolen base contributions adding real value to Bristol's weekly totals.

666018.name: Jonathan Aranda
666018.background: Jonathan Aranda is a patient, contact-oriented first baseman/DH for the Rays who has shown an excellent eye at the plate and surprising extra-base pop when given consistent playing time in Tampa Bay's deep lineup. The Manhattan Mega Rats grabbed him as a high-floor lineup complement, pairing him with Acuna and Caminero to create a lineup that produces runs from multiple spots. Aranda's on-base skills and clutch hitting give the Mega Rats a reliable run-producer at a position of value.
666018.recentAnalysis: Aranda has 3 HR and 16 RBI through 21 games for the Rays — the counting stats are solid for the Mega Rats, though the .218 average leaves room for growth.

666139.name: Josh Lowe
666139.background: A free agent outfielder in CBA with solid athleticism and power-speed tools, Josh Lowe has shown the ability to contribute in both the home run and stolen base categories during his Tampa Bay tenure. Available for any CBA team looking for multi-category outfield depth, he profiles as a .250-hitting OF with 20-HR and 15-SB upside when given consistent playing time. His tools are legitimate and he's worth a roster spot in CBA.
666139.recentAnalysis: Lowe has had a tough early stretch: .158/.225/.389 with 3 HR, 9 RBI, and 1 SB through 20 games — the power is showing up but the batting average is well below his usual level in the early going.

666142.name: Matthew Boyd
666142.background: A crafty left-handed starter who spent most of his career with Detroit, Matthew Boyd missed significant time due to elbow surgery before making a strong comeback in 2025. He relies on a deceptive delivery and varied pitch speeds rather than overpowering stuff — when healthy and commanding he can be a solid backend rotation piece.
666142.recentAnalysis: Boyd is off to a rough start with a 5.79 ERA and 1.14 WHIP across 14 IP for Chicago — the WHIP suggests he is generating contact well but the ERA implies home runs or bad luck. A small sample worth watching before writing off.

666157.name: Nick Lodolo
666157.background: A key rotation piece for the Delmarva Emus, Nick Lodolo is a tall left-hander for Cincinnati whose deceptive extension and plus fastball-slider combination makes him one of the more difficult pitchers for left-handed hitters to handle. When healthy he profiles as a legitimate SP2 who generates solid strikeout rates and keeps walks manageable. The Emus have been patient through his injury history knowing that a full season of Lodolo pitching in CBA gives them a genuine difference-maker at the back of their rotation.
666157.recentAnalysis: Lodolo is currently on the 15-day IL and has not made a 2026 start yet — Delmarva is waiting for their left-handed rotation anchor to return healthy.

666160.name: Mickey Moniak
666160.background: Mickey Moniak is an outfielder for the Rockies whose career has been a long journey from first-overall bust to legitimate power threat, developing a pull-happy approach that plays well in Coors Field. The Ghent Whistlepigs took a chance on him as a Coors Field power play, betting that his emerging pop in Colorado's thin air gives them a double-digit home run boost to their already deep lineup. Moniak's raw power and ballpark context make him a high-floor Coors play for the Whistlepigs.
666160.recentAnalysis: Moniak has 5 HR and a .902 OPS through just 16 games — the Coors Field power boost is real and delivering early value for the Whistlepigs.

666176.name: Jo Adell
666176.background: Jo Adell is a toolsy outfielder for the Angels whose massive raw power, elite athleticism, and improving contact rate have made him one of baseball's most tantalizing boom-or-bust talents as he continues to develop. The Delmarva Emus have him rounding out their deep outfield lineup, betting on his physical gifts and emerging plate discipline to finally unlock his long-anticipated power numbers in a meaningful way. When Adell gets hot, he's one of the most dangerous hitters the Emus have on the roster.
666176.recentAnalysis: Adell is hitting .289 with 3 HR, 14 RBI, and a .744 OPS through 23 games — a nice early stretch that has the Emus encouraged about his development.

666181.name: Will Benson
666181.background: A free agent outfielder in CBA with power upside and speed, Will Benson has shown the raw tools to be a useful contributor — featuring the kind of bat speed and athleticism that generated attention as a prospect. Available for any CBA team looking for outfield depth with raw power potential, he profiles as a .250-hitting OF with 15-HR and 10-SB ceiling if given regular opportunities.
666181.recentAnalysis: Benson has had limited action in 2026: .188/.324/.250 with 0 HR and 1 RBI across 18 games in 37 PA — not generating counting stats in a part-time role so far.

666182.name: Bo Bichette
666182.background: Bo Bichette is a high-energy shortstop for the Blue Jays who combines excellent contact skills with surprising power and stolen base ability, giving him legitimate five-category upside when locked in. The Bristol Banshees have him as a middle infield depth piece alongside Henderson and Gunnar, counting on his consistent contact to provide a steady floor of hits and runs. Bichette's aggressive approach and line-drive stroke make him a reliable producer even in down stretches.
666182.recentAnalysis: Bichette is off to a slow 2026 with a .217 AVG and .538 OPS through 22 games — the Banshees need him to heat up and provide more offensive support alongside their big bats.

666200.name: Jesus Luzardo
666200.background: Jesus Luzardo is a left-handed starter for the Phillies with a dominant three-pitch arsenal headlined by a plus fastball-changeup combination that when healthy makes him a legitimate top-of-the-rotation arm. The West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls have him as a potential ace-level talent in their rotation, and when Luzardo is clicking with his full arsenal he's one of the most dominant pitchers the Pepperoni Rolls can throw out there. His upside on his best days rivals any SP in the CBA.
666200.recentAnalysis: Luzardo has a 7.94 ERA through 4 starts — a very rough early stretch, but the Pepperoni Rolls are keeping the faith given his elite stuff and upside.

666464.name: Jerar Encarnacion
666464.background: A free agent outfielder in CBA with plus raw power, Jerar Encarnacion has shown impressive exit velocities and home run distance during his Marlins appearances — featuring elite bat speed and the strength to drive the ball out of any park. Available for any CBA team looking for power-oriented outfield depth, he profiles as a boom-or-bust 20-HR bat who profiles best against left-handed pitching.
666464.recentAnalysis: Encarnacion has had very limited action in 2026 with just 25 PA across 10 games (.240 AVG, 0 HR) — not generating meaningful counting stats in limited opportunities.

666969.name: Adolis Garcia
666969.background: A power outfielder for the Ghent Whistlepigs, Adolis Garcia established himself as one of the most exciting young hitters in the AL with back-to-back 25-plus HR seasons in Texas, combining impressive raw power with solid RBI production in a productive lineup. His ALCS MVP performance in 2023 showcased his ability to deliver in the biggest moments, and Ghent has counted on his power bat to contribute in the home run and RBI categories consistently. His high strikeout rate is the main knock on an otherwise compelling fantasy profile.
666969.recentAnalysis: Garcia has had a slow start for the Whistlepigs: .214/.284/.319 with 2 HR and 5 RBI through 20 games — 21 strikeouts in 81 PA is his usual profile but the power hasn't arrived yet in the early going.

666971.name: Darell Hernaiz
666971.background: A young, light-hitting middle infielder for the Athletics, Darell Hernaiz brings speed and multi-position eligibility but has yet to show the bat to be more than a streaming option. He can steal 15-20 bases when playing regularly and has improved his contact rates in the minors, but the pop and on-base ability needed to be a starter are still developing.
666971.recentAnalysis: Hernaiz is off to a modest start with .222/.281/.375 and 1 HR and 2 SB in 32 PA — the speed is showing early and if the A's give him a full-time role the stolen base ceiling is worth monitoring.

667670.name: Brent Rooker
667670.background: Brent Rooker is a power-first DH/outfielder for the Athletics who broke out with a historic 2024 season, smashing 39 home runs to establish himself as one of the premier power threats in the AL. The West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls locked him in as a potent middle-of-the-order bat alongside De La Cruz and Rodriguez, giving them a lineup that can pound the ball out of the park from multiple spots. Rooker's elite exit velocity and disciplined approach make him a legitimate 35-HR producer.
667670.recentAnalysis: Rooker is on the D10 IL after a slow 12-game stretch (.146 AVG, 2 HR) — the Pepperoni Rolls will welcome him back once healthy, hoping for his typical power burst.

667755.name: Jose Soriano
667755.background: The ace of the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets rotation and one of the most electrifying young starters in the AL, Jose Soriano features a triple-digit fastball and a devastating breaking ball that generate elite swing-and-miss rates throughout the lineup. Long viewed as one of the Angels' most exciting pitching prospects, he has emerged into a full-fledged fantasy ace whose pure stuff is as good as anyone in the game. The Ferrets struck gold keeping Soriano, and he's rapidly establishing himself as one of the most dominant pitchers in all of CBA.
667755.recentAnalysis: Soriano has been absolutely untouchable for Ft. Meade to open 2026: 5 GS, 32.2 IP, 0.28 ERA, 0.73 WHIP, 39 K, 5 W — one of the most dominant early-season pitching performances in recent CBA history and already the frontrunner for CBA Pitcher of the Year.

668227.name: Randy Arozarena
668227.background: A popular piece of the Portland Chinook outfield, Randy Arozarena became a folk hero during the 2020 postseason with a record-setting performance, then validated that debut with back-to-back 20-HR, 20-SB seasons for Tampa Bay before his trade to Seattle. His combination of power, speed, and clutch performance has made him a fantasy mainstay, and Portland relies on his stolen base contributions and run-scoring to stay competitive in those categories. His Mariners move suppresses some power but the speed travels anywhere.
668227.recentAnalysis: Arozarena has made a steady start for the Chinook: .284/.371/.392 with 1 HR, 7 RBI, 16 runs, and 5 SB through 21 games — the stolen base contributions are excellent and the OBP skills are showing up nicely.

668674.name: Lucas Erceg
668674.background: The closer for the Delmarva Emus and a power reliever with a premium fastball, Lucas Erceg has carved out a save situation in Oakland's bullpen with his ability to generate strikeouts and miss bats in the highest-leverage spots. The Emus have committed to his save opportunities as part of their multi-pronged late-game strategy, and his velocity and secondaries give him the ceiling of a legitimate double-digit save contributor. Delmarva's bullpen depth is one of their core CBA strengths.
668674.recentAnalysis: Erceg has had a rough start for the Emus: 8 G, 7.1 IP, 6.14 ERA, 1.50 WHIP, 5 K, 5 SV — the 5 saves are valuable to Delmarva despite the elevated ERA in the early going.

668678.name: Zac Gallen
668678.background: A reliable mid-rotation starter for the Syracuse Sky Chiefs, Zac Gallen has been one of the better groundball pitchers in the NL for the Diamondbacks, combining exceptional command with a five-pitch arsenal that keeps hitters off balance throughout games. He was a key contributor to Arizona's surprise World Series run and demonstrated big-game ability as well as regular-season consistency. Syracuse counts on his durability and steady ratios to anchor the middle of their staff week to week.
668678.recentAnalysis: Gallen has had a mixed early stretch for the Sky Chiefs: 5 GS, 25.2 IP, 3.51 ERA — the ERA is solid but a 1.48 WHIP and only 14 K suggests some elevated contact rates early in the season.

668715.name: Spencer Steer
668715.background: A free agent utility player in CBA with multi-position eligibility, Spencer Steer has shown solid power upside during his Cincinnati tenure — posting 20-plus homer seasons with a pull-heavy approach that generates hard contact at a useful rate. Available for any CBA team looking for corner infield depth, he profiles as a .255-hitting multi-position bat with 20-HR upside who contributes counting stats without being a liability in on-base categories.
668715.recentAnalysis: Steer has had a slow start: .209/.260/.373 with 3 HR and 5 RBI through 20 games — the power is trickling in but the average and strikeout rate (22 K in 73 PA) have been a drag on his early production.

668804.name: Bryan Reynolds
668804.background: A reliable outfield piece for the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets, Bryan Reynolds is one of the most underrated outfielders in baseball — a switch-hitter who consistently posts .270-plus averages with 20-plus homers and excellent walk rates for Pittsburgh. His patient approach and ability to hit to all fields make him a model of offensive efficiency, and Ft. Meade has relied on his steady production to stay competitive in average, runs, and power categories year after year.
668804.recentAnalysis: Reynolds has made a solid start for the Ferrets: .264/.379/.449 with 3 HR, 12 RBI, 14 runs, and 13 walks through 20 games — the walks are excellent and he's accumulating counting stats in a productive Pittsburgh lineup.

668885.name: Austin Martin
668885.background: A free agent utility infielder/outfielder in CBA with excellent plate discipline, Austin Martin was a top-5 overall draft pick who arrived with one of the best contact and walk profiles of any prospect in recent memory. Available for any CBA team to claim, he profiles as a .295-hitting multi-position contributor whose extraordinary patience at the plate generates walks at an elite rate. His OBP profile and positional flexibility make him one of the more underrated available free agents.
668885.recentAnalysis: Martin has been patient and productive: .295/.492/.477 (0.884 OPS) with 1 HR, 4 RBI, 11 runs, and 14 walks through 20 games — an extraordinary OBP driven by elite plate discipline, making him one of the better available free agents in CBA.

668901.name: Mark Vientos
668901.background: A free agent third baseman in CBA with big power upside, Mark Vientos has shown the raw strength and bat speed to be a legitimate 25-HR bat during his Mets opportunities — featuring impressive exit velocities and the ability to drive the ball out to all fields. Available for any CBA team looking for corner infield power depth, he profiles as a high-variance 20-HR bat who could hit .260 with 25 homers if given consistent playing time.
668901.recentAnalysis: Vientos has had a modest early showing: .226/.246/.358 with 2 HR and 6 RBI through 17 games — the power is present in small doses but the batting average and approach need refinement.

668909.name: Gavin Williams
668909.background: The ace of the North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms rotation and one of their most prized rotation pieces, Gavin Williams features a devastating fastball-curveball combination that generates elite strikeout rates for Cleveland. His power stuff and ability to miss bats deep into games has made him one of the AL's more exciting young starters, and the Fuzzy Bottoms secured him as a keeper knowing he had genuine top-of-the-rotation upside. North Georgia is getting ace production from one of CBA's more underrated pitching investments.
668909.recentAnalysis: Williams has been exceptional for the Fuzzy Bottoms: 5 GS, 29.2 IP, 2.12 ERA, 1.01 WHIP, 40 K — a stunning 12.1 K/9 with only 10 walks in nearly 30 IP is elite stuff, and he's been one of the best pitchers in CBA to start 2026.

668930.name: Brice Turang
668930.background: Brice Turang is an energetic second baseman for the Brewers who has developed into a multi-category fantasy asset, combining stolen base ability with surprising pop and an excellent approach at the plate. The Syracuse Sky Chiefs have him slotted in their infield alongside Perdomo and Judge, giving them a middle infield that can impact all five statistical categories. Turang's speed and emerging power make him one of the more versatile pieces on the Sky Chiefs' roster.
668930.recentAnalysis: Turang has been excellent through 19 games with a .300 AVG, 1.008 OPS, 4 HR, 14 RBI, and 6 SB — the Sky Chiefs are getting premium value from their young second baseman.

668939.name: Adley Rutschman
668939.background: A former top overall draft pick and cornerstone of the North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms roster, Adley Rutschman is one of the most complete catchers in baseball — combining elite defensive skills with a switch-hitting approach that generates .280-plus averages, excellent walk rates, and solid power from behind the plate. The Fuzzy Bottoms secured him as a marquee keeper knowing his combination of catcher eligibility and offensive production is essentially unmatched at the position in CBA. When healthy, he's the gold standard for fantasy catchers.
668939.recentAnalysis: Rutschman is on the 10-day IL, having appeared in just 10 games before the injury (.294 AVG, 0 HR, 3 RBI, 5 BB) — North Georgia is waiting on their most important catcher to return.

668941.name: JoJo Romero
668941.background: A lefty setup specialist in the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets bullpen, JoJo Romero has become a trusted high-leverage arm who generates excellent strikeout rates and keeps left-handed hitters completely off balance. The Ferrets have used him consistently in the seventh and eighth innings to protect leads, and his holds production has been a meaningful part of their weekly CBA strategy. He contributes the kind of steady, unflashy relief value that adds up meaningfully over a long CBA season.
668941.recentAnalysis: Romero has been excellent for the Ferrets: 11 G, 12.0 IP, 0.75 ERA, 1.08 WHIP, 6 K, 5 HD — a scoreless-adjacent ERA with multiple holds contributing to Ft. Meade's weekly pitching totals.

669003.name: JJ Bleday
669003.background: A former fourth overall pick, JJ Bleday is a left-handed hitting outfielder for the Athletics who has shown legitimate power potential in the majors but has struggled with strikeout rates that drag down his batting average. He profiles as a 20-HR threat with acceptable walk rates when given regular playing time, though Oakland's roster volatility makes consistent at-bats uncertain.
669003.recentAnalysis: Bleday has just 2 PA in 2026 and has not established regular playing time yet. His 2025 foundation gives reason for optimism but his role needs to solidify before he's worth rostering.

669016.name: Brandon Marsh
669016.background: Brandon Marsh is a big, athletic outfielder for the Phillies who has developed into a steady contributor with solid power, improving contact, and plus defense in the corners. The North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms have him in their outfield mix alongside Betts and Burleson, counting on his extra-base hit production and improved plate approach to provide consistent value in the middle of their lineup. Marsh's power and improving contact give the Fuzzy Bottoms a durable everyday outfielder.
669016.recentAnalysis: Marsh has 2 HR, 11 RBI, and 2 SB through 18 games with a .281 AVG — a nice early run for the Fuzzy Bottoms from their veteran outfielder.

669022.name: MacKenzie Gore
669022.background: A left-handed ace candidate for the Manhattan Mega Rats, MacKenzie Gore arrived with first-overall-pick pedigree and a power arsenal — mid-90s fastball, elite curveball, and developing changeup — that made him one of the most anticipated pitching prospects in years. His Washington tenure has shown both the ceiling and the inconsistency of a young pitcher still refining his command, but the Mega Rats kept him knowing when it all clicks together he's a genuine fantasy ace. Manhattan is counting on 2026 being the year it comes together.
669022.recentAnalysis: Gore has been excellent for the Mega Rats: 5 GS, 22.0 IP, 3.27 ERA, 1.09 WHIP, 31 K — a 12.7 K/9 is exactly the elite strikeout production Manhattan drafted him for, and he looks poised for a big season.

669065.name: Kyle Stowers
669065.background: Kyle Stowers is a left-handed power-hitting outfielder for the Marlins who has shown genuine raw pop and improving plate discipline across his first few big-league seasons, establishing himself as a legitimate run-producing threat in Miami's lineup. The Bristol Banshees have him as depth outfield insurance, knowing that when Stowers gets hot he can provide a powerful bat that outperforms his draft position. His raw power and improving contact make him a high-upside depth piece for the Banshees.
669065.recentAnalysis: Stowers is on the D10 IL after appearing in just 1 game — the Banshees are waiting for his return and hoping he can contribute once healthy.

669127.name: Shea Langeliers
669127.background: Shea Langeliers is a power-hitting catcher for the Athletics who has developed into one of baseball's best offensive backstops, combining plus raw power with a solid contact approach and excellent throw rates that make him valuable in both standard and traditional fantasy formats. The Sugar Land Space Cowboys have him as their primary catcher alongside Cruz and Lindor, giving them premium offensive production from a position that often fails to deliver big fantasy numbers. Langeliers' 30-HR ceiling at catcher is an enormous asset for the Space Cowboys.
669127.recentAnalysis: Langeliers is off to a fantastic start with a .317 AVG, .970 OPS, and 6 HR through 21 games — the Space Cowboys' catcher is producing at an elite clip early in the season.

669194.name: Ryne Nelson
669194.background: A free agent starting pitcher in CBA with solid mid-rotation upside, Ryne Nelson has shown flashes of an effective power sinker approach with Arizona that generates groundballs and keeps the ball in the park. Available for any CBA team looking to add rotation depth, he profiles as a steady innings-eater whose command and pitch mix give him a respectable ERA floor. He's a streaming-friendly option rather than a featured starter, but there's ceiling if his command sharpens.
669194.recentAnalysis: Nelson has had a tough start in 2026: 5 GS, 20.2 IP, 6.97 ERA, 1.45 WHIP — though 20 K shows the strikeout stuff is present and the ERA may be running hotter than his underlying numbers suggest.

669236.name: Jeremiah Jackson
669236.background: A high-upside power hitter for the West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls, Jeremiah Jackson arrived with enormous raw power — one of the loudest exit velocities among prospect hitters — and the Pepperoni Rolls secured him as a keeper anticipating a breakout. His early MLB exposure featured the boom-or-bust profile typical of plus-power prospects, but the home run ceiling is genuinely elite. West Virginia is betting this is the year his bat-to-ball skills catch up to his prodigious strength.
669236.recentAnalysis: Jackson has shown real pop for the Pepperoni Rolls early on: .302/.302/.577 (0.879 OPS) with 5 HR and 17 RBI through 19 games — though 0 walks in 65 PA is an extreme approach that will need to be addressed.

669242.name: Tommy Edman
669242.background: A free agent utility player in CBA and one of the best multi-position contributors available, Tommy Edman has been one of the most valuable infielders in fantasy baseball through his combination of speed, contact, positional flexibility across the diamond, and consistent run production. On the 10-day IL, he's available for any CBA team to claim and a healthy Edman immediately upgrades any roster's speed and average categories. His multi-position eligibility is exceptionally rare.
669242.recentAnalysis: Edman is currently on the 10-day IL and has not recorded 2026 stats — CBA teams can target him for a pickup ahead of his return from the IL.

669302.name: Logan Gilbert
669302.background: Logan Gilbert is a tall, methodical right-hander for the Mariners whose excellent command, high strikeout totals, and elite ground-ball rate have made him one of the most underrated starters in baseball. The Portland Chinook have him as their co-ace alongside Cole Ragans, and when Gilbert is pitching with his full arsenal he's capable of dominating any lineup in the CBA. His durability and ability to work deep into games make him especially valuable in a six-starter format.
669302.recentAnalysis: Gilbert has a 4.03 ERA with 32 K over 29 innings through 5 starts — solid volume and strikeout numbers for the Chinook, with the ERA expected to settle lower.

669330.name: Tyler Wells
669330.background: A free agent reliever in CBA with multi-role value, Tyler Wells has been used as both a long reliever and rotation depth piece for Baltimore — featuring solid command and a diverse pitch mix that keeps hitters off balance in multiple leverage situations. Available for any CBA team looking to add bullpen depth, he profiles as a holds-accumulating setup arm who can spot start when needed. His flexibility gives any roster an extra gear for managing pitching match-ups.
669330.recentAnalysis: Wells has been decent in relief for 2026: 10 G, 13.0 IP, 4.15 ERA, 1.23 WHIP, 16 K — solid strikeout numbers out of the bullpen with a modest ERA in high-leverage appearances.

669364.name: Xavier Edwards
669364.background: Xavier Edwards is a speedy switch-hitter for the Marlins whose elite contact rate and emerging stolen base game have made him one of Miami's most exciting young players and a coveted fantasy asset for his run-scoring and steals upside. The Bristol Banshees grabbed him as a speed play to complement their power-heavy roster, knowing that Edwards' ability to get on base and run makes him a unique contributor who helps them in categories where other teams fall short. His contact-first game gives the Banshees a reliable high-average producer.
669364.recentAnalysis: Edwards is having a fantastic start with a .337 AVG, .879 OPS, and 2 SB through 22 games — the Banshees' leadoff machine is delivering exactly what they expected.

669373.name: Tarik Skubal
669373.background: Tarik Skubal emerged as the best pitcher in baseball in 2024, winning the AL Cy Young with a dominant season that showcased his elite strikeout stuff and pinpoint command for the Tigers. The Bristol Banshees have him anchoring their rotation as arguably the most valuable SP in the entire CBA — when Skubal is dealing, no matchup is unwinnable. He's the kind of ace who single-handedly flips the pitching calculus for any week he takes the mound.
669373.recentAnalysis: Skubal has been outstanding in 2026 with a 2.08 ERA, 33 K, and 0.96 WHIP across 30.1 innings over 5 starts — the Banshees are getting exactly what they paid for from their ace.

669394.name: Jake Burger
669394.background: A free agent first baseman and third baseman in CBA with big-time power, Jake Burger has produced 25-plus homer seasons with Miami and Pittsburgh — a pure pull-power hitter whose launch angle and exit velocity make him a legitimate home run threat in any ballpark. Available for any CBA team looking to add corner infield power, he profiles as a .255-hitting first/third baseman with 25-HR upside who hits the ball extremely hard when he makes contact.
669394.recentAnalysis: Burger has been productive: .259/.289/.494 with 5 HR and 17 RBI through 22 games — the power is fully present even as the walk rate (3 BB) and strikeout count (26 K) reflect his all-or-nothing approach.

669432.name: Trevor Rogers
669432.background: Trevor Rogers is a tall left-handed starter for the Orioles who flashed genuine ace potential in his early career with the Marlins before injuries derailed his development, but has regained his form in Baltimore as a quality rotation starter. The Delmarva Emus have him in their rotation alongside Framber Valdez and Joe Ryan, counting on his strikeout ability and improving command to give them a reliable three-starter core. Rogers' fastball-changeup combination gives him real upside when he's dealing.
669432.recentAnalysis: Rogers has a 4.08 ERA with 25 K over 28.2 innings through 5 starts — a decent start for the Emus' rotation depth piece, with room to improve as he settles in.

669456.name: Shane Bieber
669456.background: A high-ceiling starter on the Sugar Land Space Cowboys roster, Shane Bieber was the 2020 AL Cy Young winner and one of the most dominant pitchers in baseball during his peak — posting historic strikeout rates and pinpoint command that made him essentially unhittable. Tommy John surgery derailed his 2024-25 campaigns, but the Space Cowboys have held his rights knowing a healthy Bieber is a fantasy ace in waiting. He is on the 60-day IL, and Sugar Land is patiently managing his return to form.
669456.recentAnalysis: Bieber is on the 60-day IL and has not appeared in 2026 — the Space Cowboys are hoping for a return later in the season from one of their highest-upside roster investments.

669461.name: Matthew Liberatore
669461.background: A free agent starting pitcher in CBA with solid left-handed stuff, Matthew Liberatore has developed into a rotation piece for St. Louis with a diverse pitch mix and developing command that make him a reliable innings-eater. Available for any CBA team looking for rotation depth, he profiles as a steady mid-rotation lefty who generates groundballs and keeps hitters off-balance through pitch variety. A solid streaming-tier pickup for teams in need.
669461.recentAnalysis: Liberatore has been decent in 2026: 5 GS, 27.0 IP, 3.67 ERA, 1.41 WHIP, 16 K — solid innings total and a respectable ERA with moderate strikeouts from a lefty who could be a useful streaming option.

669701.name: Josh Smith
669701.background: A free agent utility infielder in CBA with solid plate discipline, Josh Smith has shown excellent walk rates and a patient approach during his Texas and San Diego appearances. Available for any CBA team looking for multi-position infield depth, he profiles as a .255-hitting utility player whose walks and on-base skills make him a useful depth piece without great counting stat upside.
669701.recentAnalysis: Smith has had a slow start: .177/.282/.266 with 0 HR and 5 RBI through 21 games — the walk rate is there but the contact and power haven't shown up yet in the early going.

669743.name: Alex Call
669743.background: A free agent outfielder in CBA with excellent patience at the plate, Alex Call has shown elite walk rates and a disciplined approach that generates high OBP despite modest power. Available for any CBA team looking for on-base-driven outfield depth, he profiles as a .270-hitting OF with outstanding walk ability who accumulates runs and contributes in on-base categories.
669743.recentAnalysis: Call has been excellent in very limited action: .385/.526/.538 (1.117 OPS) with 0 HR, 1 RBI, and 5 walks in 19 PA across 7 games — the elite OBP skills are fully on display in a small sample.

669923.name: George Kirby
669923.background: George Kirby is one of the most refined young starters in baseball, a Mariners right-hander with elite command who barely walks anyone while still generating strikeouts through precise pitch location and excellent sequencing. The Portland Chinook have him as a third starter behind Ragans and Gilbert, and Kirby's ability to consistently throw strikes and limit free passes makes him one of the most reliable ratio-managers in the entire CBA. His near-zero walk rate makes him a model of pitching efficiency.
669923.recentAnalysis: Kirby has been excellent with a 2.97 ERA, 27 K, and 1.05 WHIP over 33.1 innings through 5 starts — the Chinook's rotation depth continues to impress.

670242.name: Matt Wallner
670242.background: A free agent outfielder in CBA with plus raw power, Matt Wallner has shown the ability to produce home runs at a useful rate during his Twins tenure — featuring plus bat speed and a pull-heavy approach that generates hard contact against right-handed pitching. Available for any CBA team looking for power-oriented outfield depth, he profiles as a high-strikeout, 20-HR bat with a boom-or-bust profile. His power upside is real even if the contact rate limits his ceiling.
670242.recentAnalysis: Wallner has had a very rough start: .181/.247/.357 with 3 HR and 6 RBI through 20 games — the power is trickling in but 34 strikeouts in 81 PA is an extremely elevated rate in the early going.

670280.name: David Bednar
670280.background: The closer for the Sugar Land Space Cowboys and one of the better save sources in the NL, David Bednar has been Pittsburgh's primary ninth-inning option for multiple years — a power reliever with a premium fastball and plus slider who generates strikeouts and converts saves at a reliable rate. The Space Cowboys have leveraged his save totals as a consistent CBA advantage, and his durability makes him a trustworthy bullpen anchor week to week. Sugar Land's rotation and bullpen depth have made them one of the more complete CBA rosters.
670280.recentAnalysis: Bednar has been reliable for the Space Cowboys: 9 G, 8.2 IP, 4.15 ERA, 1.85 WHIP, 12 K, 6 SV — leading the Ferrets in saves with 6 conversions despite some elevated ratios in the early going.

670329.name: Rico Garcia
670329.background: A high-leverage setup reliever for the North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms, Rico Garcia has become one of the most effective middle relievers in the NL with a deceptive delivery and plus secondary stuff that generates strikeouts and keeps inherited runners from scoring. The Fuzzy Bottoms have used him as a key bridge to their closer, and his holds production has been a meaningful part of North Georgia's dominant early-season relief performance. He's a quiet contributor who helps the Fuzzy Bottoms win pitching-heavy CBA weeks.
670329.recentAnalysis: Garcia has been dominant for the Fuzzy Bottoms: 10 G, 10.0 IP, 0.00 ERA, 0.30 WHIP, 10 K, 4 HD — an absolutely untouchable early stretch that has been one of the best relief performances in CBA to open 2026.

670541.name: Yordan Alvarez
670541.background: Yordan Alvarez is arguably the most feared left-handed hitter in baseball, a massive power bat for the Astros who has posted elite OPS numbers every healthy season since his debut. The Sugar Land Space Cowboys — fittingly, given Yordan's Houston roots — have him anchoring their cleanup spot as their premier offensive weapon. His combination of power and on-base ability puts him in the conversation for the best pure hitter in the entire CBA.
670541.recentAnalysis: Alvarez is scorching hot to open 2026 with a 1.276 OPS, 10 HR, and 21 RBI in just 23 games — the Space Cowboys are getting peak Yordan right out of the gate.

670623.name: Isaac Paredes
670623.background: Isaac Paredes is a disciplined third baseman/DH now with the Astros who has established himself as a valuable fantasy contributor through his exceptional plate patience and surprising power, annually posting on-base percentages north of .360. The Portland Chinook have him as a lineup complement alongside Turner and Olson, trusting his walk rate and improving power to produce solid counting stats in a productive Houston lineup. Paredes' on-base ability and power make him a quiet but reliable contributor for the Chinook.
670623.recentAnalysis: Paredes has a .190 AVG and .565 OPS through 18 games — a slow start for the Chinook's third baseman, who is expected to heat up and post his typical strong on-base numbers.

670770.name: TJ Friedl
670770.background: TJ Friedl is a speedy outfielder for the Reds whose elite sprint speed and developing contact game make him one of the most valuable pure stolen base options in fantasy baseball when he's getting consistent at-bats. The Ghent Whistlepigs grabbed him as a speed play to complement their power-heavy lineup, counting on his legs and improving on-base skills to give them a stolen base edge in weekly matchups. Friedl's wheels make him relevant in categories where the rest of the Whistlepigs' roster doesn't contribute.
670770.recentAnalysis: Friedl has 4 stolen bases through 21 games but is hitting just .154 — the speed is there for the Whistlepigs, but the average needs serious improvement to justify his roster spot.

671056.name: Ivan Herrera
671056.background: Ivan Herrera is a young catcher for the Cardinals who has flashed impressive offensive skills and a mature approach at the plate that belie his age, combining solid contact with emerging power to develop into one of baseball's more intriguing backstops. The Ghent Whistlepigs have him as their primary catcher alongside their deep lineup, knowing that premium offensive production from the catcher position is a rare and valuable commodity in the CBA. Herrera's developing bat and defensive skills give the Whistlepigs a capable everyday catcher.
671056.recentAnalysis: Herrera has 2 HR and 11 RBI through 21 games with a .200 AVG — modest numbers that the Whistlepigs are hoping improve as the young catcher finds his rhythm.

671096.name: Andrew Abbott
671096.background: Andrew Abbott is a left-handed starter for the Reds who has developed a plus arsenal headlined by a devastating slider, giving him real strikeout upside in a favorable home park. The West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls have him as a co-ace alongside Shane McClanahan, and when Abbott is operating with his best stuff he's capable of controlling a game from start to finish. His ability to miss bats and limit hard contact makes him a compelling rotation piece for the Pepperoni Rolls.
671096.recentAnalysis: Abbott has a 5.84 ERA over 24.2 innings through 5 starts — a rough early stretch for the Pepperoni Rolls, though his strikeout and walk numbers suggest better results are coming.

671218.name: Weston Wilson
671218.background: A journeyman utility outfielder-infielder for Philadelphia, Weston Wilson has carved out a big-league career through positional versatility and surprising pop at the plate. He's not a regular starter but can contribute power in limited at-bats when given a run — more of a deeper-league stash than a mainstream add.
671218.recentAnalysis: Wilson has just 13 PA in 2026 but has hit .300/.462/.400 in that small sample, suggesting he's seeing the ball well in a bench role. Not enough volume to act on yet.

671277.name: Luis Garcia Jr.
671277.background: A free agent middle infielder in the CBA with speed and contact upside, Luis Garcia Jr. has shown the ability to contribute in multiple offensive categories during his Astros appearances — combining decent contact skills with stolen base ability and gap power from the shortstop/second base positions. Available for any CBA team looking to add infield depth, he profiles as a useful multi-position contributor with 15-SB upside and a solid batting average if given regular at-bats.
671277.recentAnalysis: Garcia has had a modest start in 2026: .221/.264/.309 with 1 HR, 12 RBI, and 1 SB through 21 games — the RBI production is a bright spot but the overall offensive line leaves room for improvement.

671289.name: Tyler Freeman
671289.background: A free agent utility option in the CBA with multi-position flexibility, Tyler Freeman has shown flashes of a useful contact-oriented bat during his Cleveland appearances — making consistent contact with a compact swing and contributing in multiple infield positions. Available for any CBA team to claim, he profiles as a .275-hitting depth piece with limited power but solid batting average and positional flexibility. He's best deployed as a multi-eligible utility bat rather than a featured lineup piece.
671289.recentAnalysis: Freeman has seen limited action in 2026 with 37 PA across 11 games (.281 AVG, 0 HR, 4 RBI, 2 SB) — solid contact in a small sample but not generating the playing time needed for meaningful fantasy impact.

671732.name: Lawrence Butler
671732.background: Lawrence Butler is a developing outfielder for the Athletics who has shown flashes of big raw power and improving plate discipline, giving Oakland's rebuilding lineup a young bat with real upside as he gains experience. The Bristol Banshees kept him as a depth outfield piece, betting on his physical tools and improving contact rate to produce a breakout contribution alongside their more established stars. Butler's raw power gives the Banshees a sleeper option capable of surpassing expectations.
671732.recentAnalysis: Butler has a .197 AVG and .592 OPS through 20 games — a slow start for the Banshees' young outfielder, who still has time to show the power that earned him this roster spot.

671737.name: Taj Bradley
671737.background: One of the Delmarva Emus' most exciting young starting pitchers, Taj Bradley features a premium fastball and developing secondary arsenal that have made him one of the more highly anticipated rotation prospects in Tampa Bay's deep system. The Emus secured him as a long-term keeper knowing his tools project toward a frontline starter, and his early MLB appearances have showcased the raw ability that generated so much pre-draft excitement. Delmarva is building their rotation around his upside as a potential ace.
671737.recentAnalysis: Bradley has been excellent for the Emus: 5 GS, 27.2 IP, 1.63 ERA, 1.23 WHIP, 34 K — a 11.1 K/9 is elite strikeout production and he's been one of the best young starters in CBA to open 2026.

671739.name: Michael Harris II
671739.background: Michael Harris II is an elite defensive center fielder for the Braves who has developed a surprising offensive game, combining excellent contact skills with emerging power and solid stolen base ability to become a genuine five-category fantasy asset. The West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls grabbed him as a complementary piece alongside De La Cruz and Rodriguez, adding a center fielder who can contribute across every scoring category. Harris' athleticism and improving bat give the Pepperoni Rolls a well-rounded outfield.
671739.recentAnalysis: Harris has 3 HR, 8 RBI, and 2 SB through 19 games for Atlanta — a solid early contribution that the Pepperoni Rolls can build on through the rest of the season.

671922.name: Cade Smith
671922.background: A versatile bullpen piece for the Ghent Whistlepigs, Cade Smith has carved out a role in Cleveland's strong relief corps with a deceptive delivery, solid command, and the ability to miss bats in multiple high-leverage situations. The Whistlepigs have used him in their closer and setup rotation, collecting saves and holds as opportunities arise. His profile as a reliable bullpen arm with save upside makes him a useful fantasy piece for Ghent's staff.
671922.recentAnalysis: Smith has been solid for the Whistlepigs: 11 G, 11.0 IP, 4.09 ERA, 1.27 WHIP, 14 K, 5 SV, 2 HD — good strikeout numbers with multiple saves and holds contributing to Ghent's weekly totals.

672386.name: Alejandro Kirk
672386.background: The veteran catcher for the Syracuse Sky Chiefs, Alejandro Kirk is one of the best pure contact catchers in baseball — posting AVG figures consistently above .280 with a patient approach and gap power that makes him an above-average offensive backstop. His framing and blocking skills make him a defensive asset as well, and the Sky Chiefs have relied on his rare combination of contact hitting and catcher eligibility to anchor that roster slot. For a position that typically produces weak hitters in CBA, Kirk is a genuine standout.
672386.recentAnalysis: Kirk is currently on the 10-day IL and has appeared in just 5 games (.150 AVG, 1 HR) before the injury — Syracuse is waiting on his return to fill their catcher slot.

672580.name: Maikel Garcia
672580.background: Maikel Garcia is a versatile, contact-oriented infielder for the Royals who has made himself into a useful fantasy asset with his stolen base ability and improving plate discipline. The Delmarva Emus added him as a complementary piece to their deep lineup, counting on his speed and improving on-base skills to round out their already powerful offense. Garcia's athleticism and ability to play multiple infield spots give the Emus valuable flexibility.
672580.recentAnalysis: Garcia is hitting .265 with 2 HR, 9 RBI, and 1 SB through 22 games for KC — a modest but solid start for the Emus' utility infielder.

672640.name: Otto Lopez
