672640.background: Otto Lopez is a versatile utility hitter for the Marlins whose ability to play multiple positions and produce contact-driven offense make him a valuable piece on any roster. The West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls have him as a complementary bat in their lineup, contributing across the board with his solid average, improving power, and baserunning ability. Lopez's positional flexibility and contact-first game give the Pepperoni Rolls roster flexibility that's hard to quantify.
672640.recentAnalysis: Lopez is off to a fantastic start with a .338 AVG, .936 OPS, 3 HR, and 10 RBI through 21 games — a productive early run that has the Pepperoni Rolls very pleased with their utility man.

672695.name: Geraldo Perdomo
672695.background: Geraldo Perdomo is the engine of the Diamondbacks' lineup, a switch-hitting shortstop who trades raw power for elite on-base skills, speed, and positional versatility. The Syracuse Sky Chiefs kept him as a valued keeper for his stolen base upside and consistent run-scoring ability, counting on him to complement Aaron Judge at the top of their lineup. His walk rate and glove make him the prototypical high-floor fantasy shortstop for the Sky Chiefs.
672695.recentAnalysis: Perdomo has 6 stolen bases through 21 games for Arizona with a .229 average — the legs are producing for the Sky Chiefs even as the bat takes time to wake up.

672761.name: Wenceel Perez
672761.background: A free agent utility infielder in CBA with speed and contact skills, Wenceel Perez has shown solid athleticism and contact ability during his early Tigers appearances. Available for any CBA team looking for middle infield depth, he profiles as a .270-hitting utility player with stolen base upside and the ability to play multiple positions at an adequate level.
672761.recentAnalysis: Perez has had very limited action through 7 games and 24 PA (.045 AVG, 1 HR, 2 SB) — not yet generating consistent at-bats to evaluate his 2026 production.

672820.name: Lenyn Sosa
672820.background: A free agent utility infielder in CBA with solid contact skills, Lenyn Sosa has shown the ability to post useful batting averages during his White Sox appearances while providing flexibility across multiple infield positions. Available for any CBA team looking for infield depth, he profiles as a .280-hitting utility player whose contact-first approach produces decent averages without great power or speed upside.
672820.recentAnalysis: Sosa has had modest production in limited action: .227/.227/.273 with 0 HR and 4 RBI in 45 PA across 16 games — filling a bench role without generating standout fantasy numbers.

673237.name: Yainer Diaz
673237.background: A catcher and first base piece for the Manhattan Mega Rats, Yainer Diaz has established himself as a legitimate offensive backstop with Houston — combining a contact-first approach with solid gap power and the ability to hit in a productive lineup. His multi-position eligibility at C and 1B gives the Mega Rats flexibility in constructing their roster, and his Houston lineup protection means consistent RBI opportunities. Manhattan values him as one of the more productive catcher options available in CBA.
673237.recentAnalysis: Diaz has had a slow start for the Mega Rats: .194/.242/.258 (0.501 OPS) with 1 HR and 9 RBI through 16 games — the contact and production that define his value haven't shown up in the early going.

673357.name: Luis Robert Jr.
673357.background: One of the Syracuse Sky Chiefs' most dynamic outfielders, Luis Robert Jr. has one of the highest tool ceilings of any player at his position — combining elite speed, a cannon arm, and plus raw power that generated one of the best defensive seasons in recent CF history. His injury history has been the primary obstacle to becoming a perennial top-10 outfielder, but when healthy his 25-HR, 15-SB combination is among the best in CBA. Syracuse has been patient through his IL stints banking on the ceiling.
673357.recentAnalysis: Robert has made a patient start: .242/.365/.363 with 2 HR, 7 RBI, and 2 SB through 18 games — the walk rate (12 BB) is notably improved from previous seasons, though the power production the Sky Chiefs are counting on hasn't fully arrived.

673540.name: Kodai Senga
673540.background: A high-upside starter on the Portland Chinook rotation, Kodai Senga arrived from Japan with one of the most unique arsenals in baseball — featuring a ghost forkball that baffles hitters and helped him post one of the best debut seasons in recent history for the Mets. Injuries derailed his follow-up campaign, but Portland held his rights knowing a healthy Senga is a top-15 SP in all fantasy formats. The Chinook are counting on him to anchor their rotation when healthy.
673540.recentAnalysis: Senga has had a brutal start for Portland: 4 GS, 17.1 IP, 8.83 ERA, 1.90 WHIP, 22 K — the strikeout stuff is still there (11.4 K/9) but he's been hit extremely hard and the Chinook are hoping he can find the command that made him elite.

673548.name: Seiya Suzuki
673548.background: Seiya Suzuki is a polished right-handed hitter for the Cubs who came over from Japan with an excellent reputation for plate discipline and power, and has delivered with multiple quality seasons in Chicago. The Portland Chinook have him as a complementary outfielder alongside Turner and Olson, counting on his consistent contact and developing power to provide solid counting stats throughout the season. Suzuki's patient approach and ability to hit for average make him a reliable contributor for the Chinook.
673548.recentAnalysis: Suzuki has only played 9 games with a .235 AVG — the Chinook are waiting for him to get more consistent playing time and heat up as the season progresses.

675911.name: Spencer Strider
675911.background: The Bristol Banshees' most prized rotation investment and one of the most dominant pitchers in baseball when healthy, Spencer Strider holds the all-time MLB record for strikeouts by a right-hander in a single season — a testament to the terrifying power of his fastball-slider combination. Tommy John surgery cost him 2025, but Bristol held his rights knowing that a healthy Strider returning to form would instantly make them a CBA pitching powerhouse. The upside is as high as any pitcher in the league.
675911.recentAnalysis: Strider is on the 15-day IL and has not yet appeared in 2026 — Bristol's rotation is waiting on the return of their most important pitcher.

676106.name: Emerson Hancock
676106.background: A promising young starter for the North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms, Emerson Hancock was a top-10 draft pick who has worked his way through Seattle's system with a premium fastball and developing secondary arsenal that generated strong strikeout numbers at every minor league level. The Fuzzy Bottoms locked him up as a keeper anticipating a full breakthrough at the MLB level, and his early results have rewarded that faith. North Georgia views him as a cornerstone rotation piece for years to come.
676106.recentAnalysis: Hancock has been excellent for the Fuzzy Bottoms: 4 GS, 23.2 IP, 2.28 ERA, 0.76 WHIP, 25 K — a phenomenal start with elite ratios and 25 strikeouts in under 24 innings, immediately justifying North Georgia's keeper investment.

676254.name: Ryan Walker
676254.background: A setup reliever and multi-role bullpen piece for the Portland Chinook, Ryan Walker has carved out a high-leverage role in San Francisco's bullpen with a deceptive sinker and plus slider that generate excellent groundball rates and strikeouts. The Chinook have used him to accumulate holds and bridge to their closer, and his ability to handle high-leverage situations gives Portland reliable late-game depth. He's the kind of trustworthy setup arm that helps a CBA roster win pitching-heavy matchups.
676254.recentAnalysis: Walker has been decent for the Chinook: 9 G, 9.0 IP, 4.00 ERA, 1.56 WHIP, 10 K, 1 SV, 2 HD — the strikeouts are there but the WHIP reflects some hard contact in the early going.

676391.name: Ernie Clement
676391.background: Ernie Clement is a versatile utility man for the Blue Jays who has reinvented himself as a productive multi-position contributor, bringing consistent contact ability and solid baserunning to whichever spot he's asked to play. The Delmarva Emus have him as a lineup complement and positional flexibility piece, able to fill in at multiple spots while providing steady hitting production. Clement's contact-first game and ability to play anywhere give the Emus a valuable bench presence who can slot into the lineup without missing a beat.
676391.recentAnalysis: Clement is hitting .301 with 25 hits and 1 SB through 21 games — solid contact production that the Emus value from their versatile utility player.

676440.name: Tanner Bibee
676440.background: Tanner Bibee is a crafty right-hander for the Guardians who emerged as one of Cleveland's most reliable starters, relying on excellent command and a deceptive delivery to generate weak contact and miss bats despite pedestrian velocity. The Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets have him in their rotation alongside Glasnow and Webb, giving them a dependable innings-eater who can stabilize their pitching staff over the course of a week. Bibee's consistency and command make him a steady SP3 who rarely blows up.
676440.recentAnalysis: Bibee has a 4.81 ERA with 23 K through 5 starts — a workable if rocky early stretch for the Folksy Ferrets' rotation piece.

676475.name: Alec Burleson
676475.background: Alec Burleson is a productive corner outfielder/first baseman for the Cardinals who has developed into a reliable run-producer with surprising pop and a solid contact approach. The North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms have him in the middle of their lineup alongside Naylor and Sale, counting on his steady extra-base hit production to give their offense some muscle. Burleson's ability to play multiple positions gives the Fuzzy Bottoms valuable roster flexibility.
676475.recentAnalysis: Burleson is off to a strong start with a .284 AVG, .829 OPS, 3 HR, and 15 RBI through 21 games — solid early production from the Fuzzy Bottoms' corner bat.

676477.name: Garrett Whitlock
676477.background: A versatile arm for the West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls who can contribute as both a starter and a reliever, Garrett Whitlock has been one of Boston's most valuable pitching assets — providing elite strikeout rates and superb command in whatever role he's needed. The Pepperoni Rolls have used him primarily as a high-leverage reliever to accumulate holds, and his stuff is good enough to serve as an emergency starter when needed. West Virginia values his roster flexibility as a genuine competitive advantage.
676477.recentAnalysis: Whitlock has been solid for the Pepperoni Rolls: 8 G, 8.0 IP, 2.25 ERA, 1.25 WHIP, 12 K, 3 HD — nice strikeout production and solid ratios contributing to West Virginia's weekly pitching totals.

676617.name: Riley O'Brien
676617.background: A dominant high-leverage reliever for the North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms, Riley O'Brien has emerged as one of the most effective multi-inning relievers in baseball with a power sinker and excellent command that generates elite groundball rates and near-zero walks. The Fuzzy Bottoms have leaned on his saves and holds as a major competitive advantage in weekly CBA matchups, and his ability to eat innings without allowing runners makes him one of the most valuable relief pieces on their roster. North Georgia's bullpen is a genuine strength.
676617.recentAnalysis: O'Brien has been absolutely dominant for the Fuzzy Bottoms: 11 G, 11.0 IP, 0.00 ERA, 0.36 WHIP, 12 K, 6 SV, 1 HD — one of the best early-season relief performances in all of CBA and a huge reason for North Georgia's early pitching success.

676962.name: Ben Brown
676962.background: A free agent starter/reliever in CBA with upside, Ben Brown has shown an advanced pitch mix and promising command in his Cubs appearances — featuring a power fastball and a curveball that generates swing-and-miss rates worthy of a legitimate rotation piece. Available for any CBA team to claim, he profiles as a mid-rotation starter with strikeout upside if given consistent opportunities. His youth and stuff make him an appealing developmental add.
676962.recentAnalysis: Brown has been effective in a hybrid role: 7 appearances, 16.2 IP, 2.70 ERA, 1.08 WHIP, 15 K — solid results across flexible usage with an encouraging ERA that makes him an appealing addition.

676979.name: Garrett Crochet
676979.background: Garrett Crochet made the leap from elite reliever to ace starter in 2024, posting a historic strikeout season for the White Sox that earned him a blockbuster trade to the Red Sox. The Manhattan Mega Rats grabbed him as a top rotation piece, betting on his triple-digit velocity and swing-and-miss stuff to dominate hitters for years to come. When Crochet is right, he's a 200-K pitcher capable of single-handedly carrying any fantasy pitching staff.
676979.recentAnalysis: Crochet has had a rough early stretch with a 7.65 ERA over 20 innings across 5 starts for Boston — the Mega Rats need him to get his command in order quickly.

677587.name: Brayan Rocchio
677587.background: A slick-fielding shortstop developing into a reliable bat for the Ghent Whistlepigs, Brayan Rocchio has carved out an everyday role in Cleveland with his plus defense and improving offensive contributions. His contact-first approach, good walk rates, and developing power make him a well-rounded middle infield piece, and Ghent values his multi-category contributions at a premium defensive position. As Cleveland's lineup continues to develop around him, his counting stats and offensive profile should continue to grow.
677587.recentAnalysis: Rocchio has been solid for the Whistlepigs: .254/.342/.373 with 3 HR, 14 RBI, and 9 runs through 22 games — a well-rounded line with good walks and consistent contact from the shortstop position.

677594.name: Julio Rodriguez
677594.background: Julio Rodriguez is a five-tool outfielder for the Mariners who has backed up his prospect hype with elite speed, emerging power, and a strong arm in center field, establishing himself as one of the game's most exciting young players. The West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls locked him in as their offensive centerpiece, counting on his stolen base ability and growing power to deliver across all five categories. J-Rod's ceiling as a 40-40 threat is what gives the Pepperoni Rolls their highest-upside asset.
677594.recentAnalysis: Rodriguez is off to a slow start in 2026 with a .202 AVG and .576 OPS through 23 games — the Pepperoni Rolls are trusting the profile and waiting for his typical production to surface.

677800.name: Wilyer Abreu
677800.background: Wilyer Abreu is a dynamic outfielder for the Red Sox who has established himself as one of Boston's best everyday players, combining a contact-first approach with plus athleticism and solid extra-base hit production. The Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets have him as a key piece of their outfield alongside Tucker and Bellinger, counting on his contact ability and emerging power to contribute counting stats week over week. Abreu's all-around game gives the Folksy Ferrets a versatile and reliable outfielder.
677800.recentAnalysis: Abreu is hitting a scorching .311 with 3 HR, 10 RBI, and an .877 OPS through 20 games — the Folksy Ferrets are getting excellent production from their Red Sox outfielder.

677951.name: Bobby Witt Jr.
677951.background: The most coveted free agent in the CBA and one of the most talented young players in all of baseball, Bobby Witt Jr. is a five-category monster — combining 30-homer power, 30-plus stolen bases, a plus batting average, and elite defensive shortstop play into the most complete offensive package at any position in the game. He's currently available in CBA as a free agent, and any team that can land him in a trade or unclaimed pick adds an instant superstar to their roster. His EROSP projects him as the top player in the league.
677951.recentAnalysis: Witt has been solid but not yet explosive: .275/.330/.360 with 0 HR, 8 SB, and 8 walks through 22 games — the steals are excellent but the power is notably absent so far and the OPS is well below his usual elite level.

678246.name: Miguel Vargas
678246.background: A versatile infield piece on the Ghent Whistlepigs roster, Miguel Vargas is a switch-hitter with strong on-base skills and patient plate approach who has worked his way into a regular role after earning his stripes in the Dodgers' deep system. His ability to play multiple infield positions gives Ghent roster flexibility, and his walk rates and line-drive contact profile project toward a solid .270 average with gap power as he matures. The Whistlepigs are waiting for that offensive potential to fully click.
678246.recentAnalysis: Vargas has had a tough start for Ghent: .153/.264/.333 (0.593 OPS) with 2 HR and 3 SB across 20 games — the walks are there (12 BB) but the batting average has been extremely poor through 87 PA.

678394.name: Brayan Bello
678394.background: A mid-rotation piece for the Bristol Banshees, Brayan Bello emerged as one of the Red Sox's better young starters with a sinker-heavy approach that generates ground balls and weak contact at a high rate. His stuff is not flashy — he won't rack up elite strikeout totals — but his command and pitch mix make him a reliable WHIP and ERA contributor for Boston's rotation. Bristol values him as a steady innings-eater who keeps the team competitive in pitching categories week to week.
678394.recentAnalysis: Bello has had a rough start to 2026: 3 GS, 14.2 IP, 6.14 ERA, 1.91 WHIP, 9 K — the control has been off and he's been hit hard in his early outings, which will concern Bristol's pitching staff.

678662.name: Ezequiel Tovar
678662.background: Ezequiel Tovar is a young shortstop for the Rockies who has developed into one of the NL's more productive offensive contributors at his position, combining solid contact with surprising power that plays even better in the thin air of Coors Field. The Manhattan Mega Rats have him as a middle infield complement to Neto and Aranda, counting on his Coors Field-aided power and solid average to provide consistent counting stats. Tovar's home park and improving approach give the Mega Rats a reliable shortstop option.
678662.recentAnalysis: Tovar has a .238 AVG and .649 OPS through 21 games — steady if unspectacular early numbers for the Mega Rats, who are expecting more power to come from their Coors Field shortstop.

678882.name: Ceddanne Rafaela
678882.background: Ceddanne Rafaela is an electric outfielder/shortstop for the Red Sox whose plus speed and athleticism have made him one of Boston's most exciting young players, combining elite defensive ability with developing offensive tools. The Bristol Banshees have him in their outfield as a complementary piece to Skubal and their power arms, adding a speedy defender who can contribute stolen bases and runs scored when he gets on base. Rafaela's athleticism and developing bat make him a high-upside contributor for the Banshees.
678882.recentAnalysis: Rafaela has a .276 AVG and .726 OPS through 20 games for Boston — solid early numbers that the Banshees can build on from their do-everything outfielder.

679529.name: Spencer Torkelson
679529.background: A free agent first baseman in CBA with enormous raw power, Spencer Torkelson was the first overall draft pick with one of the best pure power profiles of any hitter in recent drafts — featuring elite bat speed and the ability to drive the ball out of any park. Available for any CBA team to claim, he profiles as a high-variance 25-HR first baseman who has struggled with contact rate but retains massive ceiling as he continues to develop.
679529.recentAnalysis: Torkelson has had a very difficult start: .190/.336/.253 with 0 HR and 6 RBI through 20 games — 13 walks show elite patience but the power and average that were expected of the first overall pick have been completely absent.

680574.name: Matt McLain
680574.background: A developmental shortstop for the Manhattan Mega Rats, Matt McLain arrived as one of Cincinnati's most polished middle infield prospects with excellent contact skills, above-average speed, and the ability to hit for a solid average while providing defensive value. His early career has been interrupted by injury but the Mega Rats have been patient with his development knowing the upside — a .280-hitting shortstop with 15-homer power — is worth the wait. Manhattan views him as part of their long-term middle infield solution.
680574.recentAnalysis: McLain has had a difficult early stretch for the Mega Rats: .183/.345/.239 (0.558 OPS) with 0 HR and 3 RBI through 21 games — the walks are excellent (14 BB) but the contact and power have been absent in the early going.

680694.name: Jack Kochanowicz
680694.background: A big right-handed starting pitcher for the Angels, Jack Kochanowicz is a ground-ball heavy sinker-curveball pitcher who limits hard contact and generates weak grounders. His strikeout rate is modest for a starter, but his command and ability to pitch deep into games make him a viable backend SP option despite pitching for a losing team.
680694.recentAnalysis: Kochanowicz is showing real promise in 2026 with a 3.10 ERA and 1.24 WHIP across 29 IP with 19 Ks — the ratios are excellent and he's earning a streaming start anytime he's not facing an elite offense.

680700.name: Richie Palacios
680700.background: A free agent utility outfielder/infielder in CBA with multi-position flexibility, Richie Palacios has shown a solid contact approach and useful speed contributions during his Cleveland and Tampa Bay appearances. Available for any CBA team looking for versatile depth, he profiles as a .280-hitting utility player with double-digit stolen base upside and the ability to cover multiple positions without being a liability. His speed and contact skills make him a useful situational piece.
680700.recentAnalysis: Palacios has been productive in limited action: .290/.359/.516 (0.855 OPS) with 1 HR, 4 RBI, and 3 SB across 14 games — a strong early line from a player getting opportunities in a new role.

680718.name: Addison Barger
680718.background: A free agent infielder in CBA on the 10-day IL, Addison Barger is a young hitter who arrived with solid tools and a developing offensive profile from Toronto's system. Available for any CBA team to monitor, a return from the IL could give him an opportunity to contribute at the MLB level as the season progresses.
680718.recentAnalysis: Barger is on the 10-day IL and has appeared in just 8 games (23 PA, .053 AVG) before the injury — not yet generating fantasy production at the MLB level.

680730.name: Mitchell Parker
680730.background: A free agent left-handed pitcher in CBA with solid stuff, Mitchell Parker has shown a promising arsenal and developing command in his early Washington appearances — featuring a fastball-slider combination that generates strikeouts when his mechanics are dialed in. Available for any CBA team looking for bullpen or rotation depth, he profiles as a flexible arm who can contribute in multiple roles as he develops.
680730.recentAnalysis: Parker has been effective in relief appearances: 2 G, 5.0 IP, 5.40 ERA, 1.40 WHIP, 7 K, 1 W — the strikeout rate is encouraging even if the ERA has been slightly elevated.

680757.name: Will Brennan
680757.background: An outfielder for Cleveland with solid contact skills and gap power, Will Brennan has shown the ability to hit for a reasonable average when given regular at-bats. He's a depth outfielder rather than a star, offering batting average contributions with modest power and speed in a good Cleveland lineup.
680757.recentAnalysis: Brennan is hitless in 7 PA to start 2026 — a very small sample that means nothing. Monitor his playing time status.

680776.name: Jarren Duran
680776.background: Jarren Duran is a blazing-fast outfielder for the Red Sox who broke out as a 30-steal, .280-hitting leadoff weapon, giving the Delmarva Emus one of the most dynamic top-of-the-lineup weapons in the CBA. His speed, gap power, and ability to score runs make him the perfect complement to the power bats around him on the Emus' roster. Duran's electric first-step quickness and elite range in center field round out an already impressive fantasy profile.
680776.recentAnalysis: Duran has been slowed by a rough patch with a .172 AVG and .520 OPS through 16 games — the Emus are expecting better from their leadoff sparkplug as the season heats up.

680777.name: Michael Massey
680777.background: A second baseman for Kansas City who has shown promise as a contact hitter with some pop, Michael Massey is a left-handed bat who fits well in the middle of the Royals lineup. He has developing power and a solid approach that could produce useful counting stats in a competitive Kansas City offense.
680777.recentAnalysis: Massey is hitting .250/.333/.375 with 1 HR through 47 PA — steady if unexciting production that makes him a viable option in deeper formats when getting regular at-bats.

680885.name: Spencer Schwellenbach
680885.background: A young rotation piece for the Delmarva Emus, Spencer Schwellenbach quickly established himself as one of Atlanta's best young starters with a power sinker and command-oriented approach that generated groundballs and weak contact at an impressive rate. The Emus secured him as a keeper knowing his development trajectory is pointing toward a frontline starter role as he enters his prime years. He is on the 60-day IL and Delmarva is managing his return carefully to maximize what could still be a strong second half.
680885.recentAnalysis: Schwellenbach is on the 60-day IL and has not appeared in 2026 — the Emus are monitoring his recovery and hoping for a strong return later in the season.

680977.name: Brendan Donovan
680977.background: Brendan Donovan is one of baseball's most underrated multi-position contributors, a Cardinals utility man whose excellent contact ability and emerging power have made him a legitimate everyday player at multiple spots. The Syracuse Sky Chiefs have him as a versatile piece in their lineup, able to slide into any role the team needs while still contributing across multiple fantasy categories. Donovan's high-contact approach and improving power give him a strong floor and growing ceiling for the Sky Chiefs.
680977.recentAnalysis: Donovan is off to a hot start with a .304 AVG, .955 OPS, and 3 HR through 18 games — a nice early contribution for the Sky Chiefs from their do-everything utility man.

681082.name: Bryson Stott
681082.background: Bryson Stott is a young middle infielder for the Phillies who has developed into a reliable contact hitter with solid stolen base ability and the versatility to play multiple positions. The North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms have him as a complementary piece to their offense, counting on his athleticism and improving plate approach to provide steady counting stats and speed. Stott's contact-first game and baserunning make him a useful floor-raiser for the Fuzzy Bottoms.
681082.recentAnalysis: Stott has 4 SB through 15 games but is hitting just .204 — the legs are working for the Fuzzy Bottoms while the bat lags in the early going.

681190.name: Randy Vasquez
681190.background: A young rotation piece for the Bristol Banshees, Randy Vasquez has developed quickly through the Yankees' and Padres' systems with a deceptive delivery, excellent command, and a willingness to attack hitters with multiple pitch types. The Banshees secured him as a developmental keeper knowing his stuff projects toward a solid mid-rotation profile as he enters his prime. Bristol views him as part of their long-term rotation alongside Robbie Ray and Spencer Strider, and his early results have been encouraging.
681190.recentAnalysis: Vasquez has been solid for the Banshees: 4 GS, 21.2 IP, 2.49 ERA, 1.29 WHIP, 25 K — 25 strikeouts in under 22 innings is an excellent rate and the ERA marks him as one of Bristol's more productive rotation investments so far.

681217.name: Chase Silseth
681217.background: A free agent reliever in the CBA with versatile usage, Chase Silseth has shown the ability to contribute in both starting and bullpen roles with a power fastball and developing secondary stuff. Available for any CBA team looking to add bullpen depth, he profiles as a high-leverage setup arm with strikeout upside who can spot start when needed. His stuff is legitimate and any manager who adds him gets a useful multi-role arm with ceiling to grow into a featured reliever.
681217.recentAnalysis: Silseth has been effective in relief appearances in 2026: 11 G, 7.2 IP, 2.35 ERA, 1.43 WHIP, 9 K, 3 HD — solid results out of the bullpen contributing holds to the teams he's been pitching for.

681347.name: Mike Burrows
681347.background: A free agent starting pitcher in CBA with high strikeout upside, Mike Burrows features a power fastball and excellent secondary pitches that generate elite whiff rates in Pittsburgh's rotation. Available for any CBA team looking for a high-variance, high-ceiling rotation add, he profiles as a potential SP2 with a 10-plus K/9 ceiling but command inconsistencies that create volatility. His stuff is among the best of any pitcher in the free agent pool.
681347.recentAnalysis: Burrows has had a tough start: 5 GS, 26.2 IP, 6.75 ERA, 1.69 WHIP, 25 K — the 8.4 K/9 shows the elite stuff is present but the ERA and WHIP reflect significant command issues in the early going.

681481.name: Kerry Carpenter
681481.background: Kerry Carpenter is a left-handed hitting outfielder for the Tigers who broke out as an unexpected power threat, generating elite exit velocity with a direct, line-drive swing that plays in any park. The Bristol Banshees grabbed him as a power piece alongside Henderson and Skubal's battery, knowing that when Carpenter is connecting he can produce at a clip that outperforms his modest draft price. His raw power gives the Banshees a dark-horse contributor capable of winning matchups on a hot streak.
681481.recentAnalysis: Carpenter has 4 HR and 12 RBI through 18 games — solid early power production that the Banshees will happily take from their corner outfielder.

681624.name: Andy Pages
681624.background: Andy Pages is an outfielder for the Dodgers who emerged from their deep minor league system with impressive raw power and the athleticism to play all three outfield spots, giving Los Angeles a high-upside everyday bat. The North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms grabbed him as a power piece to complement Sale and Arraez, betting on his improving plate approach and plus raw power to deliver run production in a loaded Dodger lineup. Pages' combination of pop and athleticism gives the Fuzzy Bottoms one of their most exciting younger contributors.
681624.recentAnalysis: Pages is absolutely on fire to open 2026 with a 1.042 OPS, .373 AVG, 5 HR, and 21 RBI through 21 games — the Fuzzy Bottoms are getting a massive early return on their investment.

681715.name: Heriberto Hernandez
681715.background: A free agent corner outfielder in CBA with raw power potential, Heriberto Hernandez has shown the ability to generate impressive exit velocities in his limited MLB opportunities — featuring the kind of bat speed that projects toward 20-plus home runs if given regular playing time. Available for any CBA team to claim, he profiles as a boom-or-bust power bat who will strike out at a high rate but also hit the ball extremely hard when he makes contact. A high-risk, high-reward speculative add.
681715.recentAnalysis: Hernandez has had a very difficult start: .137/.267/.176 (0.443 OPS) with 0 HR, 7 RBI, and 3 SB through 17 games — the walk rate is decent but the power and contact have been extremely poor in the early going.

681911.name: Alex Vesia
681911.background: A premium left-handed reliever for the Syracuse Sky Chiefs, Alex Vesia features a mid-90s fastball and a devastating sweeper that makes him one of the most effective one-out lefty specialists in the Dodgers' bullpen. The Sky Chiefs have used him consistently in high-leverage situations alongside Mason Miller, and his holds production has been a major contributor to Syracuse's weekly CBA pitching totals. His elite stuff against left-handed hitters makes him a matchup weapon that Sky Chiefs managers love deploying.
681911.recentAnalysis: Vesia has been outstanding for the Sky Chiefs: 9 G, 8.1 IP, 0.00 ERA, 0.60 WHIP, 10 K, 2 SV, 3 HD — a scoreless ERA with multiple holds and saves is exactly the kind of dominant relief contribution Syracuse is built around.

682052.name: Jacob Lopez
682052.background: A free agent starting pitcher in CBA with promising young stuff, Jacob Lopez has shown an advanced feel for pitching with solid secondary pitches and developing command that project toward a reliable mid-rotation profile. Available for any CBA team looking to add young rotation depth, he profiles as a development-track starter with the ceiling of a solid fantasy SP if his command continues to refine. He's worth a look for teams willing to roster developing arms.
682052.recentAnalysis: Lopez has had a difficult early stretch: 4 GS, 18.1 IP, 6.38 ERA, 1.96 WHIP — though 16 K shows the swing-and-miss stuff is present and a small sample may be masking his true level.

682622.name: Noelvi Marte
682622.background: A free agent third baseman in CBA with significant upside, Noelvi Marte arrived as one of the top position player prospects in baseball with a premium offensive profile — elite raw power, plus athleticism, and the kind of bat speed that projects toward 30-plus homers at maturity. Currently on non-active roster status (RM), any CBA team holding his rights is invested in his return and the enormous ceiling that comes with him. He's a long-term bet with a genuinely electric upside.
682622.recentAnalysis: Marte has seen very limited action in 2026 (11 G, 31 PA, .138 AVG) and is currently on non-active roster status — CBA managers are watching for his return and activation.

682626.name: Jonny DeLuca
682626.background: An outfield prospect for Tampa Bay with solid speed and developing power, Jonny DeLuca has impressed in his early major league opportunities with a combination of athleticism and a mature offensive approach. He has multi-category upside and fits well in Tampa's patient organizational philosophy.
682626.recentAnalysis: DeLuca is off to a solid start with .286/.381/.423 and 2 HR and 2 SB through 59 PA — contributing across multiple categories and worth starting in daily leagues when in the Rays lineup.

682641.name: Luis Matos
682641.background: A free agent outfielder in CBA with speed and developing tools, Luis Matos has shown the athleticism and base-running ability to be a useful contributor when given regular playing time in San Francisco's outfield. Available for any CBA team looking for outfield depth with speed upside, he profiles as a .265-hitting CF with stolen base potential once he earns more consistent at-bats at the MLB level.
682641.recentAnalysis: Matos has had very limited action in 2026 with just 17 PA across 7 games (.188 AVG) — not generating meaningful production in a fringe roster role.

682657.name: Angel Martinez
682657.background: A versatile utility piece for the Syracuse Sky Chiefs, Angel Martinez has carved out an everyday role in Cleveland with his ability to play all over the diamond — shortstop, second base, third base, and outfield — while posting a solid offensive profile with developing power and speed. The Sky Chiefs value his positional flexibility enormously, as he gives them the ability to fill any roster gap without sacrificing offensive production. As Cleveland's lineup matures around him, his counting stats should continue to grow.
682657.recentAnalysis: Martinez has been a steady contributor for Syracuse: .262/.292/.468 with 2 HR, 9 RBI, and 4 SB through 21 games — the speed is contributing and the power is showing up, making him a useful multi-category piece.

682663.name: Agustin Ramirez
682663.background: A young catcher with enormous upside on the Delmarva Emus roster, Agustin Ramirez has drawn consistent praise for his receiving skills, arm strength, and an offensive approach that projects toward above-average production at a premium defensive position. The Emus secured him as a developmental keeper knowing that a catching prospect with his tools and athleticism is exceptionally rare. If his bat develops as projected, he could become one of the most valuable catchers in all of CBA.
682663.recentAnalysis: Ramirez has had a modest start for the Emus: .221/.287/.349 with 1 HR and 10 RBI through 21 games — still finding his footing at the MLB level, but the counting stat contributions show he's getting opportunities.

682668.name: Luisangel Acuna
682668.background: A free agent middle infielder in CBA and younger brother of Ronald Acuna Jr., Luisangel Acuna has shown elite speed and developing offensive tools during his early career with the Mets. Available for any CBA team looking for stolen base upside in the infield, he profiles as a .250-hitting middle infielder with 20-SB potential if given regular playing time. His speed is the primary fantasy asset and any team in need of steals should consider him.
682668.recentAnalysis: Acuna has had a quiet start: .203/.248/.219 with 0 HR, 3 RBI, and 6 SB through 19 games — the speed is contributing (6 SB already) even as the bat finds its footing at the MLB level.

682829.name: Colton Cowser
682829.background: A lefty-hitting outfielder who was drafted by Baltimore with the fifth overall pick, Colton Cowser has a professional approach with strong walk rates and developing power. He profiles as a .270+, 20-HR outfielder with solid on-base skills in a contending Orioles lineup — a solid mid-tier outfield asset.
682829.recentAnalysis: Cowser is off to a slow start with .200/.317/.293 and 0 HR through 63 PA — below expectations for a player of his profile. His track record suggests a turnaround is coming.

682842.name: Abner Uribe
682842.background: A key bullpen piece for the North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms, Abner Uribe features explosive triple-digit velocity and a late-breaking slider that makes him one of the most electric relievers in the NL. His role in Milwaukee's bullpen has included both save opportunities and high-leverage work, and the Fuzzy Bottoms have benefited from his holds and occasional saves in their weekly CBA matchups. His raw stuff is as good as any reliever they roster, and North Georgia is invested in his development toward a full-time closer role.
682842.recentAnalysis: Uribe has been decent for the Fuzzy Bottoms: 10 G, 9.0 IP, 4.00 ERA, 1.22 WHIP, 9 K, 2 SV, 4 HD — contributing in both saves and holds, though the ERA has been slightly elevated in the early going.

682928.name: CJ Abrams
682928.background: CJ Abrams is a fast-rising shortstop for the Nationals who has developed into one of the most electric fantasy producers in the game, pairing elite athleticism with surprising pop and one of the most explosive stolen base totals in baseball. The Delmarva Emus locked him in as one of their most dynamic assets, and when Abrams is running and hitting he can single-handedly swing a matchup for the Emus. His five-tool ceiling makes him one of the most exciting players to own in the CBA.
682928.recentAnalysis: Abrams is off to a scorching start with a 1.029 OPS, .320 AVG, 6 HR, 19 RBI, and 4 SB through 21 games — the Emus are getting full value from their dynamic shortstop.

682985.name: Riley Greene
682985.background: Riley Greene is a young outfielder for the Tigers who has established himself as one of Detroit's best everyday players, combining a patient left-handed approach with improving power and solid athleticism in center field. The Delmarva Emus have him in their outfield mix alongside Ohtani and Duran, counting on his on-base skills and developing pop to contribute consistently week over week. Greene's contact ability and improving power give the Emus a high-floor outfield bat who won't hurt them.
682985.recentAnalysis: Greene has 1 HR, 12 RBI, and a .714 OPS through 22 games — steady if unspectacular early production from the Emus' corner outfielder.

682990.name: Quinn Priester
682990.background: A free agent starting pitcher in CBA on the 15-day IL, Quinn Priester was one of Pittsburgh's top pitching prospects with a power arsenal and developing command that projected toward a mid-rotation ceiling. Available for any CBA team willing to stash him through his IL stint, he profiles as a development-track starter with legitimate upside if his command develops. He's a higher-variance pickup but the stuff is real.
682990.recentAnalysis: Priester is on the 15-day IL and has not appeared in 2026 — he's available for CBA teams willing to wait for his return before evaluating his contributions.

682998.name: Corbin Carroll
682998.background: Corbin Carroll is one of the most dynamic young outfielders in baseball, a speedy left-handed hitter for the Diamondbacks who won NL Rookie of the Year in 2023 and has developed into a five-category fantasy machine. The Bristol Banshees secured him as a cornerstone asset alongside Skubal, giving them elite talent on both sides of the ball. Carroll's combination of speed, on-base ability, and pop makes him a fantasy manager's dream.
682998.recentAnalysis: Carroll is off to a strong 2026 start, slashing .304/1.004 OPS with 3 HR, 16 RBI, and 2 SB through 20 games — the Banshees' outfield is in great hands.

683002.name: Gunnar Henderson
683002.background: Gunnar Henderson is the centerpiece of the Orioles' rebuild, a switch-hitting shortstop who hits for power, steals bases, and plays premium defense at the most important position on the field. The Bristol Banshees have him locked in as part of their deep talent base, and Henderson's combination of tools makes him one of the most exciting young players in the CBA. His ceiling as a 35-HR shortstop is what every manager covets and what the Banshees are counting on.
683002.recentAnalysis: Henderson has 7 HR and 15 RBI through 22 games for Baltimore with a .763 OPS — the raw power is producing for the Banshees even as the average (.204) lags.

683004.name: Jack Leiter
683004.background: One of the West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls' most exciting young rotation investments, Jack Leiter arrived as one of the most anticipated pitching prospects in years — the son of a MLB veteran with a premium fastball-slider combination and pedigree that generated enormous excitement. His path through the Rangers' system featured strong strikeout numbers, and his MLB debut has shown the raw ability to miss bats at the highest level. West Virginia is betting on continued development from a pitcher with genuine ace upside.
683004.recentAnalysis: Leiter has had a rocky start for the Pepperoni Rolls: 4 GS, 20.1 IP, 4.87 ERA, 1.48 WHIP, 24 K — the strikeout rate is impressive at 10.6 K/9 but walks and hard contact have pushed the ERA above acceptable levels in his early outings.

683146.name: Brett Baty
683146.background: A developmental third base prospect for the West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls, Brett Baty arrived as one of the Mets' best position player prospects with a projectable frame, advanced feel for hitting, and the bat speed to project toward 25-plus homers at maturity. His early MLB opportunities have shown the raw tools but also the growing pains of a young hitter still refining his approach at the highest level. West Virginia is holding his rights as a long-term keeper investment in a player who may not reach his ceiling for another year or two.
683146.recentAnalysis: Baty has had a tough start for the Pepperoni Rolls: .190/.197/.254 (0.467 OPS) with 0 HR and 7 RBI through 18 games — 21 strikeouts in 66 PA with almost no walks is a difficult early profile for the developing third baseman.

683737.name: Coby Mayo
683737.background: A top-100 prospect and the centerpiece of the Baltimore organization's future at third base, Coby Mayo features plus raw power and solid plate discipline that give him 30-HR upside at full development. He was one of the most anticipated Orioles call-ups and profiles as a future 3B cornerstone.
683737.recentAnalysis: Mayo is off to a modest start with .188/.312/.323 and 3 HR and 1 SB through 77 PA — the power is showing in spurts and the walk rate is solid, but the batting average needs to come up for him to be a reliable fantasy starter.

683766.name: Christian Koss
683766.background: A fringe free agent infielder in CBA, Christian Koss has worked his way to the major league level as a utility player with solid defensive skills and a modest offensive profile. Available as a purely speculative depth add, he profiles as a bench piece whose value is almost entirely in positional flexibility rather than offensive production.
683766.recentAnalysis: Koss has seen just 7 PA in 5 games in 2026 — not generating meaningful fantasy production and primarily serving as roster depth.

684007.name: Shota Imanaga
684007.background: Shota Imanaga is one of baseball's most celebrated recent international arrivals, a left-handed starter for the Cubs who combines an elite cutter with exceptional command to generate weak contact and miss bats without overpowering stuff. The North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms have him as a key piece of their rotation alongside Chris Sale and Jacob deGrom, and when Imanaga is dealing he's one of the most dominant pitchers in the CBA. His walk rate and ground-ball tendencies make him a model of pitching efficiency.
684007.recentAnalysis: Imanaga has been brilliant through 4 starts with a 2.45 ERA, 31 K, and 0.77 WHIP over 22 innings — the Fuzzy Bottoms are getting ace-level production from their Japanese lefty.

685299.name: Tanner Gordon
685299.background: A free agent starting pitcher in CBA with solid command and developing stuff, Tanner Gordon has shown the ability to pitch efficiently and generate weak contact during his early MLB opportunities with Kansas City. Available for any CBA team looking for rotation depth, he profiles as a command-oriented mid-rotation piece whose floor ERA is respectable if not exciting. A useful streaming option for teams needing to fill rotation innings.
685299.recentAnalysis: Gordon has made just one appearance in 2026 (4.0 IP, 0.00 ERA, 0.75 WHIP) — an encouraging but extremely small sample size.

686217.name: Sal Frelick
686217.background: Sal Frelick is a speedy, contact-first outfielder for the Brewers whose elite bat-to-ball skills and developing stolen base game have made him one of Milwaukee's most useful everyday players. The Syracuse Sky Chiefs have him rounding out their outfield alongside Harper and Donovan, counting on his on-base ability and speed to contribute at the top of the lineup. Frelick's low strikeout rate and glove in center field give him a dependable floor for the Sky Chiefs.
686217.recentAnalysis: Frelick has a .191 AVG and .560 OPS through 20 games — a rough start for the Sky Chiefs' speedster, who needs to get on base more to generate his typical stolen base value.

686218.name: Emmet Sheehan
686218.background: Emmet Sheehan is a hard-throwing right-hander who burst onto the scene for the Dodgers as a high-strikeout starter with a plus fastball-slider combination that baffles hitters. The Ghent Whistlepigs grabbed him as a high-upside SP1 alongside Brandon Woodruff and Freddy Peralta, forming one of the deeper starting rotations in the CBA. If he stays healthy and delivers on his ceiling, Sheehan is a potential league-winner for the Whistlepigs.
686218.recentAnalysis: Sheehan has a 5.85 ERA over 20 IP through 4 starts for the Dodgers — the Whistlepigs are waiting for the velocity-driven upside to overtake the early command issues.

686469.name: Vinnie Pasquantino
686469.background: Vinnie Pasquantino is a disciplined first baseman for the Royals whose patient approach and smooth left-handed swing have made him one of the more reliable on-base producers in the AL, even as he develops his power game. The Bristol Banshees kept him as a steady depth piece alongside their more explosive hitters, trusting his walk rate and contact ability to contribute consistent production even when the power isn't popping. His floor as a high-OBP first baseman is what the Banshees value.
686469.recentAnalysis: Pasquantino is off to a very slow start with a .163 AVG and .517 OPS through 22 games — the Banshees need him to heat up and start contributing offensively.

686527.name: Dominic Canzone
686527.background: A free agent corner outfielder in CBA with solid power and contact balance, Dominic Canzone has shown the ability to post useful batting averages while generating 15-plus home runs in his MLB opportunities with Arizona and Seattle. Available for any CBA team looking for outfield depth with pop, he profiles as a .270-hitting corner OF with 15-HR upside who contributes in multiple categories without being a liability anywhere. A solid mid-tier free agent add.
686527.recentAnalysis: Canzone has been productive in his opportunities: .255/.302/.490 (0.810 OPS) with 2 HR, 9 RBI, and 8 runs across 18 games — solid power contributions in a limited sample from a player who could provide more if given consistent at-bats.

686555.name: Isaac Collins
686555.background: A free agent outfielder in CBA with solid speed and contact skills, Isaac Collins has shown the ability to post solid batting averages and contribute in the speed categories during his early MLB opportunities. Available for any CBA team looking for outfield depth, he profiles as a contact-first outfielder with stolen base upside who adds depth without being a liability. A low-ceiling but floor-oriented free agent add.
686555.recentAnalysis: Collins has had a quiet start: .196/.302/.261 with 1 HR and 4 RBI through 18 games — limited counting stat production so far in 2026 in a part-time role.

686611.name: Dylan Crews
686611.background: A former top overall draft pick on the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets roster, Dylan Crews arrived with immense pedigree as one of the most polished college hitters in recent draft history — combining elite contact skills with solid power and strong makeup that scouts rave about. The Ferrets secured him as a long-term keeper knowing his ceiling is that of a .290-hitting outfielder with 20-homer upside. His development timeline may be longer than expected but Ft. Meade is committed to the investment.
686611.recentAnalysis: Crews does not currently have 2026 MLB stats on record — he may be in the minors or on a non-standard roster status as the Folksy Ferrets await his callup or roster activation.

686613.name: Mason Black
686613.background: A highly-touted right-handed pitching prospect for the Giants, Mason Black features a power sinker-slider combination that generates significant groundball rates. He was one of the more anticipated pitching call-ups of 2025 and has solid strikeout potential despite not being a true swing-and-miss ace. His floor is elevated by the ground ball profile even when the strikeouts aren't there.
686613.recentAnalysis: Black has just 3.2 IP in 2026 with a 0.00 ERA but a 2.18 WHIP — a very small sample that suggests he's been in and out of the rotation or recently activated. Monitor his role before committing.

686668.name: Max Schuemann
686668.background: A middle infield prospect for the Athletics, Max Schuemann is a contact-first hitter with solid speed who is developing his professional game. He has some fantasy appeal in deep leagues for stolen bases but hasn't yet established himself as a regular at the MLB level.
686668.recentAnalysis: Schuemann has no 2026 stats on record and is not currently a major league roster asset.

686701.name: Ryan Bergert
686701.background: A free agent pitcher in CBA currently on non-active roster status (RM), Ryan Bergert has shown promising stuff during his development through the system with a power arsenal and developing command. Available as a speculative pick for CBA teams willing to monitor his status, his return would add a young arm with upside to any rotation.
686701.recentAnalysis: Bergert has no 2026 MLB stats recorded — he is currently on non-active roster status and CBA managers are monitoring his situation.

686752.name: Ryan Pepiot
686752.background: A mid-rotation starter on the North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms roster, Ryan Pepiot has shown flashes of legitimate SP upside with a deceptive delivery and plus changeup that generates weak contact and strikeouts against right-handed hitters. He's been limited by injury over the course of his career, but the Fuzzy Bottoms have valued his ceiling enough to keep him on their roster. He is on the 60-day IL, and North Georgia is hoping for a healthy return to contribute to their rotation later in the year.
686752.recentAnalysis: Pepiot is on the 60-day IL and has not appeared in 2026 — the Fuzzy Bottoms are waiting on his return as they manage their rotation depth.

686930.name: Mason Barnett
686930.background: A free agent pitcher in CBA currently on non-active roster status (RM), Mason Barnett is a developing arm with solid prospects in the system. Available as a long-shot speculative add for teams looking for young pitching lottery tickets.
686930.recentAnalysis: Barnett has no 2026 MLB stats on record — he is on non-active roster status and not yet contributing to CBA rosters.

686948.name: Drake Baldwin
686948.background: Drake Baldwin is a young catcher for the Braves who has burst onto the scene as one of the most exciting hitting catchers in the game, combining plus bat speed with a disciplined approach that gives him unusual offensive upside at a typically light-hitting position. The Portland Chinook grabbed him as one of the most exciting keepers at the catcher position in the CBA, betting on his emerging power and contact skills to deliver elite production at a scarce position. Baldwin's offensive tools make him a potential franchise-building piece for the Chinook.
686948.recentAnalysis: Baldwin is having a spectacular start with a .318 AVG, .898 OPS, 5 HR, and 19 RBI through 21 games — the Chinook's catcher is one of the best values in the entire league right now.

687263.name: Zach Neto
687263.background: Zach Neto is a young shortstop for the Angels who has developed into one of the more versatile offensive contributors at his position, combining solid contact skills with emerging power and improving baserunning. The Manhattan Mega Rats added him as a middle infield complement alongside their big bats, counting on his balanced approach and consistent production to contribute across multiple categories. Neto's ability to hit for average while producing power gives the Mega Rats a dependable shortstop.
687263.recentAnalysis: Neto has 5 HR, 10 RBI, and an .854 OPS through 23 games — a strong start for the Mega Rats' shortstop, who is producing across the board early in the season.

687401.name: Joey Ortiz
687401.background: A free agent middle infielder in CBA with solid all-around skills, Joey Ortiz has shown useful contact ability, speed, and positional flexibility during his early career with Baltimore. Available for any CBA team looking for shortstop or second base depth, he profiles as a .265-hitting utility infielder with double-digit stolen base upside and the athleticism to handle multiple positions capably.
687401.recentAnalysis: Ortiz has had a modest start: .196/.259/.304 with 0 HR, 4 RBI, and 4 SB through 17 games — the speed is contributing but the bat has been quiet in the early going.

687462.name: Spencer Horwitz
687462.background: A free agent first baseman and outfielder in CBA with elite on-base skills, Spencer Horwitz has developed into one of the most patient hitters in the AL — posting extraordinary walk rates and solid contact numbers in Toronto's lineup. Available for any CBA team looking for OBP-driven offensive production, he profiles as a .280-hitting first baseman who accumulates runs and walks at a high rate while contributing modest power. His plate discipline alone makes him a useful addition.
687462.recentAnalysis: Horwitz has had a decent start: .255/.397/.418 (0.782 OPS) with 2 HR, 5 RBI, and 11 walks in 68 PA — the walk rate is elite as always and he's been on base consistently for any team that adds him.

687473.name: Ryan Gusto
687473.background: A free agent pitcher in CBA currently on non-active roster status (RM), Ryan Gusto is a developing arm with promising stuff working his way toward a regular MLB role. Available as a speculative stash for teams monitoring young pitchers.
687473.recentAnalysis: Gusto has made just 1 appearance (1.0 IP, 0.00 ERA) and is primarily on non-active roster status — minimal fantasy impact in 2026 to this point.

687597.name: Jordan Beck
687597.background: A free agent outfielder in CBA with developing tools, Jordan Beck is a young hitter working through the early stages of his MLB career with Colorado — featuring raw power and athleticism that are still being refined at the professional level. Available as a speculative developmental pick for CBA managers, his Coors Field home games give him a favorable backdrop if he earns regular playing time.
687597.recentAnalysis: Beck has had a difficult start: .122/.182/.245 with 1 HR and 6 RBI across 14 games — still finding his footing at the MLB level with extremely limited production so far.

687637.name: Dylan Beavers
687637.background: A free agent outfielder in CBA with developing tools, Dylan Beavers was a top draft pick who has shown flashes of his plus raw power and speed combination in his early MLB opportunities with Baltimore. Available for any CBA team to claim, he profiles as a 20-HR, 15-SB upside bat still figuring out his MLB approach. The tools are legitimate and any manager who adds him is betting on his development timeline accelerating.
687637.recentAnalysis: Beavers has had a modest early showing: .212/.300/.288 with 1 HR, 6 RBI, and 1 SB through 17 games — still developing at the MLB level with the tools that convinced teams to draft him highly still waiting to fully emerge.

687859.name: Troy Johnston
687859.background: A free agent first baseman in CBA with strong contact skills, Troy Johnston has shown a mature approach at the plate and solid contact numbers during his early MLB opportunities — projecting toward a .285-hitting first baseman with gap power as he develops. Available for any CBA team looking for first base depth with a higher ceiling than his EROSP suggests, he's a solid streaming option who could earn more regular playing time.
687859.recentAnalysis: Johnston has been productive in his opportunities: .317/.373/.540 (0.879 OPS) with 2 HR and 10 RBI through 19 games — strong contact and counting stat production from a player who looks ready for regular MLB at-bats.

689414.name: Liam Hicks
689414.background: A breakout hitter on the North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms roster, Liam Hicks arrived as a raw power bat with exceptional contact skills that have allowed him to translate his tools quickly at the MLB level. The Fuzzy Bottoms are among the earliest believers in his potential, having secured him as a keeper before his star had fully risen. His combination of elite bat-to-ball skills and surprising power make him one of the most exciting young bats on any CBA roster, and North Georgia is thrilled to have his rights locked up.
689414.recentAnalysis: Hicks has been absolutely on fire for the Fuzzy Bottoms: .328/.373/.552 (0.925 OPS) with 4 HR, 19 RBI, and 13 runs through 21 games — and only 5 strikeouts in 75 PA is a truly elite contact rate that makes his line even more impressive.

690953.name: Mick Abel
690953.background: A free agent starting pitcher in CBA with significant upside, Mick Abel was Philadelphia's top pitching prospect with a premium fastball, plus curveball, and advanced feel for pitching that generated top-100 prospect buzz throughout his development. Available for any CBA team to claim, he profiles as a mid-to-top-rotation starter with genuine ace upside once his command fully develops at the MLB level. His youth and stuff make him one of the more compelling developmental free agent pitcher options.
690953.recentAnalysis: Abel has had an up-and-down early stretch: 4 appearances (3 GS), 20.1 IP, 3.98 ERA, 1.57 WHIP, 23 K — the 10.2 K/9 shows the swing-and-miss stuff is very real, and the ERA is manageable despite some command issues.

690986.name: Kyle Harrison
690986.background: A young left-handed starter in the West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls rotation, Kyle Harrison came up through San Francisco's system as one of the better pitching prospects in the NL — featuring a power fastball and a slider that generates elite swing-and-miss rates against both lefties and righties. His debut showed the ceiling of a mid-rotation arm with upside for more, and the Pepperoni Rolls kept him as a young keeper building toward his prime years. West Virginia has invested in his development as part of their long-term rotation plan.
690986.recentAnalysis: Harrison has been solid for the Pepperoni Rolls in his first 3 starts: 14.2 IP, 3.07 ERA, 1.09 WHIP, 15 K — good command and encouraging ratios in an early sample that bodes well for the season ahead.

690993.name: Colt Keith
690993.background: Colt Keith is a young second baseman for the Tigers who has quickly developed into one of Detroit's best everyday hitters, combining a smooth contact approach with emerging power and solid baserunning in a Tigers lineup that's been producing at an elite level. The Syracuse Sky Chiefs grabbed him as a high-floor infield piece alongside Judge and Perdomo, betting on his contact-first approach and improving power to provide consistent production in the middle of their lineup. Keith's bat control and high average floor make him a reliable Sky Chiefs contributor.
690993.recentAnalysis: Keith is off to a great start with a .317 AVG and .777 OPS through 19 games for Detroit — the Sky Chiefs are getting the contact production they expected from their young second baseman.

690997.name: Nolan McLean
690997.background: A young rotation piece for the Sugar Land Space Cowboys, Nolan McLean is a high-upside arm who combined raw power stuff with developing command through the system and has pushed his way into a MLB rotation opportunity. His fastball and breaking ball generate strong swing-and-miss rates, and the Space Cowboys view him as part of a long-term rotation core that also includes Chase Burns and Parker Messick. Together they give Sugar Land one of the most exciting young pitching trios in CBA.
690997.recentAnalysis: McLean has been excellent for the Space Cowboys: 4 GS, 23.2 IP, 2.28 ERA, 0.76 WHIP, 28 K — outstanding ratios across the board and he's demonstrating the stuff that convinced Sugar Land to keep him as a long-term rotation piece.

691016.name: Tyler Soderstrom
691016.background: Tyler Soderstrom is a power-hitting catcher/DH for the Athletics who was one of the most sought-after bat-first catchers in recent draft classes, with a left-handed swing built for extra-base hits. The Ghent Whistlepigs kept him alongside Kurtz and Hoerner as part of their youth-driven core, banking on Soderstrom's pop to carry the lineup when he gets hot. His ability to play catcher adds roster flexibility that makes him even more valuable to the Whistlepigs.
691016.recentAnalysis: Soderstrom is hitting .198 with 2 HR and 15 RBI through 22 games for Oakland — the RBI total is solid but the average needs to climb for the Whistlepigs to get full value.

691019.name: Kyle Teel
691019.background: A highly regarded catching prospect for the Bristol Banshees, Kyle Teel arrived as one of the top backstop prospects in the 2023 draft class — a left-handed hitter with outstanding plate discipline, solid catch-and-throw skills, and a mature approach at the plate well beyond his years. The Banshees secured him as a developmental keeper knowing that patient, contact-oriented catchers with his profile are extraordinarily rare in the draft. If his bat develops as projected, he could become one of the most valuable catchers in all of CBA.
691019.recentAnalysis: Teel is on the 10-day IL and has not yet recorded 2026 stats — Bristol is waiting on their top catching prospect to return and begin contributing.

691023.name: Jordan Walker
691023.background: An explosive young outfielder and one of the most exciting hitters on the Delmarva Emus roster, Jordan Walker arrived as the Cardinals' top prospect with elite raw power, plus athleticism, and a bat speed that immediately impressed at the MLB level. The Emus secured him as a keeper anticipating a breakout season where his tools would fully translate, and Walker has developed into a genuine five-category threat with home run, speed, and batting average contributions. Delmarva views him as a cornerstone of their offensive future.
691023.recentAnalysis: Walker has been outstanding for the Emus to start 2026: .309/.367/.654 (1.025 OPS) with 8 HR, 16 RBI, 16 runs, and 3 SB through 21 games — a spectacular start that's making him one of the most valuable hitters in all of CBA.

691026.name: Masyn Winn
691026.background: A free agent shortstop in CBA with developing tools, Masyn Winn has shown solid athleticism and improving offensive skills during his Cardinals tenure — combining decent contact with speed and the ability to play premium defense at short. Available for any CBA team looking for shortstop depth with upside, he profiles as a .260-hitting SS with 15-SB potential as he continues to develop. His defensive floor makes him a safe roster addition even as his bat matures.
691026.recentAnalysis: Winn has had a modest start: .210/.315/.254 with 1 HR, 5 RBI, and 2 SB through 17 games — still finding his offensive footing, though the walk rate and defense provide a useful floor.

691176.name: Jasson Dominguez
691176.background: A free agent outfielder in CBA with enormous upside, Jasson Dominguez arrived as the Yankees' most anticipated prospect in decades — drawing comparisons to multiple Hall of Famers with five-tool athleticism, elite bat speed, and a power-speed combination that could make him a perennial MVP candidate. He is currently not on an active MLB roster (RM status), but any CBA team holding his rights is investing in the possibility of his return and the ceiling that comes with him.
691176.recentAnalysis: Dominguez has not appeared in 2026 MLB games — he is currently on a non-active roster status and CBA managers with his rights are waiting for his return to the field.

691406.name: Junior Caminero
691406.background: Junior Caminero is one of the most physically gifted young hitters in baseball, a massive third baseman for the Rays who has showcased jaw-dropping power and athleticism in his first big-league stints. The Manhattan Mega Rats kept him as a prized keeper alongside Acuna and Keaschall, betting on his raw power to develop into a middle-of-the-order cornerstone. His combination of physicality and advanced bat-to-ball skills for his age gives him a sky-high ceiling.
691406.recentAnalysis: Caminero has 5 HR, 10 RBI, and a .804 OPS through 21 games for Tampa Bay — the Mega Rats are seeing real production from their young corner bat early in 2026.

691594.name: Javier Sanoja
691594.background: A free agent middle infielder in CBA with excellent contact skills, Javier Sanoja has impressed with a mature plate approach and consistent line-drive contact during his early Marlins appearances. Available for any CBA team looking for middle infield depth with a high batting average profile, he profiles as a .300-hitting utility infielder who makes exceptional contact and contributes runs without hurting in any category.
691594.recentAnalysis: Sanoja has been productive in his opportunities: .320/.358/.380 with 0 HR, 9 RBI, and 2 SB through 16 games — the contact skills are elite (only 6 K in 53 PA) and he's been one of the more pleasant early-season free agent surprises.

691718.name: Pete Crow-Armstrong
691718.background: Pete Crow-Armstrong is an elite defensive outfielder for the Cubs who has developed surprising pop and stolen base ability to complement his glove, turning himself into a five-tool asset with legitimate fantasy upside. The Sugar Land Space Cowboys grabbed him as a speed-and-defense keeper, hoping his bat catches up with his tools and gives them a premium center fielder for years to come. PCA's athleticism and contact ability give him a high floor even in down offensive stretches.
691718.recentAnalysis: Crow-Armstrong has 4 SB through 21 games but is hitting just .222 with 1 HR — the legs are working for the Space Cowboys, but the power needs to develop to reach his ceiling.

693304.name: Nick Gonzales
693304.background: A developing second baseman on the North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms roster, Nick Gonzales arrived as one of Pittsburgh's top offensive prospects with a compact, line-drive swing and strong contact approach that projected toward a .290 average with gap power at maturity. His MLB debut has been gradual, but the Fuzzy Bottoms are holding his rights as a long-term keeper knowing his tools are legitimate. North Georgia views him as a future everyday middle infield piece with a high batting average ceiling.
693304.recentAnalysis: Gonzales has had a modest start for the Fuzzy Bottoms: .258/.286/.345 with 0 HR, 11 RBI through 19 games — solid contact and RBI production but the power hasn't shown up yet in the early going.

693307.name: Dillon Dingler
693307.background: A young catcher developing into a starting role for the Bristol Banshees, Dillon Dingler has impressed with his defensive skills and a more-than-adequate bat that makes him a useful two-way contributor at a premium position. The Banshees secured him as a developmental catcher keep knowing that his combination of arm strength, receiving, and improving offensive production gives them a long-term solution behind the plate. Bristol is banking on his power upside continuing to develop as he gets more MLB at-bats.
693307.recentAnalysis: Dingler has made a solid start for Bristol: .259/.309/.534 (0.865 OPS) with 4 HR and 14 RBI through 18 games — the power is showing up in a big way and he's quickly justifying Bristol's decision to keep him.

693433.name: Bryan Woo
693433.background: Bryan Woo is one of the most underrated young starters in baseball, a sinker-slider right-hander for the Mariners who generates elite weak contact and has quietly posted excellent ratios since earning a rotation spot. The Syracuse Sky Chiefs have him as a pillar of their pitching staff alongside Nathan Eovaldi, counting on his ground-ball tendencies and durability to anchor their weekly rotation. Woo's stuff plays up in pitcher-friendly parks and makes him a matchup-friendly ace for the Sky Chiefs.
693433.recentAnalysis: Woo has been outstanding through 5 starts with a 1.95 ERA, 21 K, and 0.87 WHIP over 27.2 innings — one of the Sky Chiefs' most reliable assets in the season's first month.

693821.name: Bryce Elder
693821.background: A rotation piece and pleasant surprise for the Sugar Land Space Cowboys, Bryce Elder has developed into a reliable mid-rotation starter for Atlanta through elite command and a groundball-heavy approach that generates weak contact without premium velocity. The Space Cowboys acquired him knowing his floor is that of a solid innings-eater with useful ERA and WHIP contributions, and his early 2026 results have dramatically exceeded those expectations. Sugar Land has one of the deepest starting rotations in CBA between Elder, Burns, Messick, and McLean.
693821.recentAnalysis: Elder has been spectacular for the Space Cowboys: 4 GS, 23.1 IP, 0.77 ERA, 1.03 WHIP, 23 K — one of the best ERA marks in all of CBA early on and an enormous unexpected contribution to Sugar Land's dominant rotation.

694037.name: Daniel Palencia
694037.background: A high-upside reliever in the Manhattan Mega Rats bullpen, Daniel Palencia features a power arsenal with a fastball-slider combination that generates elite strikeout rates and has flashed closer-level stuff in his early Cubs appearances. The Mega Rats secured him as a bullpen piece with save upside, knowing that if he steps into a closer role he has the raw stuff to post elite numbers. Manhattan views him as a long-term bullpen investment with the ceiling of a legitimate fantasy closer.
694037.recentAnalysis: Palencia has been perfect for the Mega Rats in his early appearances: 5 G, 5.0 IP, 0.00 ERA, 1.00 WHIP, 5 K, 1 SV — clean outings and a save conversion as he establishes himself in Chicago's bullpen hierarchy.

694192.name: Jackson Chourio
694192.background: Jackson Chourio is one of baseball's most exciting young players, a Brewers outfielder who broke out with a massive 2024 season and has established himself as a 30-30 threat with plus athleticism and premium bat speed. The Sugar Land Space Cowboys secured him as a cornerstone young asset alongside Lindor and Rice, and when Chourio is fully locked in he's a matchup-winning performer who can dominate all five categories. His tools and age give the Space Cowboys one of the highest ceilings in the CBA.
694192.recentAnalysis: Chourio is currently on the D10 IL — the Space Cowboys are watching his timeline and hoping he returns to deliver on his big-league breakthrough production.

694212.name: Samuel Basallo
694212.background: One of the Sugar Land Space Cowboys' most exciting young catching prospects, Samuel Basallo arrived from the international market as a switch-hitting backstop with enormous raw power, excellent defensive tools, and a maturity well beyond his age. The Space Cowboys secured him as a long-term developmental keep knowing his ceiling — a .280-hitting catcher with 25-HR power — could make him the most valuable CBA player at his position by the time he fully matures. Sugar Land is investing in his future while managing his development carefully.
694212.recentAnalysis: Basallo has had a difficult start for the Space Cowboys: .140/.234/.298 (0.532 OPS) with 3 HR and 4 RBI through 16 games — 17 strikeouts in 64 PA reflects the typical growing pains of a young prospect at the MLB level.

694384.name: Nolan Schanuel
694384.background: A free agent first baseman in CBA with a standout on-base profile, Nolan Schanuel arrived with one of the best college walk rates in recent draft history and has maintained that patient approach at the MLB level for the Angels. Available for any CBA team looking to add first base production, he profiles as a .260-hitting first baseman with useful walks and developing power as he continues to grow into his frame. His OBP skills alone make him worth rostering.
694384.recentAnalysis: Schanuel has had a mixed early stretch: .228/.308/.354 with 2 HR and 13 RBI through 20 games — the RBI production is decent and the walks are showing up, though the average and power leave room for improvement.

694477.name: Chad Patrick
694477.background: A free agent starting pitcher in CBA with impressive early results, Chad Patrick has shown excellent command and contact-management skills during his early MLB opportunities — featuring a pitch mix that keeps hitters off balance and generates weak contact. Available for any CBA team looking to add rotation depth, he profiles as a solid floor starter whose ERA tends to outperform his stuff-based projections through elite command.
694477.recentAnalysis: Patrick has been excellent in his early appearances: 4 outings (3 GS), 19.0 IP, 0.95 ERA, 1.16 WHIP, 9 K — one of the best ERAs in the league through any role and a compelling pickup for teams in need of rotation depth.

694497.name: Evan Carter
694497.background: Evan Carter is a polished young outfielder for the Rangers who debuted with a memorable 2023 postseason run and has developed into a patient, multi-category hitter with solid on-base skills and emerging power. The North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms have him in their outfield mix alongside Naylor and Burleson, betting on his advanced plate approach to translate into consistent counting stats as he matures. Carter's contact ability and walks give him a high floor even in stretches when the power isn't popping.
694497.recentAnalysis: Carter is hitting .183 with 2 HR and 3 SB through 22 games — a rough start for the Fuzzy Bottoms' young outfielder, who will need to raise his average to be productive.

694671.name: Wyatt Langford
694671.background: Wyatt Langford is a toolsy outfielder for the Rangers who broke onto the scene as one of the most polished college bats in recent memory, combining a plus hit tool with power and speed that make him a potential all-star in the making. The West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls grabbed him as a complementary piece to Elly De La Cruz and Julio Rodriguez, building an exciting and athletic outfield capable of producing across all five categories. Langford's contact skills and baserunning give the Pepperoni Rolls a versatile offensive weapon.
694671.recentAnalysis: Langford has a .237 AVG and .634 OPS through 19 games — an underwhelming start, but the Pepperoni Rolls are confident the hit tool will carry him to better numbers.

694819.name: Jacob Misiorowski
694819.background: The Portland Chinook's most electrifying young arm, Jacob Misiorowski features a mid-to-upper-90s fastball combined with a devastating slider that generates historically elite swing-and-miss rates. His rapid development has drawn comparisons to some of the best recent pitching prospects, and Portland secured him as a keeper anticipating exactly this kind of ace emergence. In a rotation that also includes Story-era acquisitions, Misiorowski represents the Chinook's long-term pitching future.
694819.recentAnalysis: Misiorowski has been electric in his early MLB starts for Portland: 4 GS, 21.2 IP, 3.32 ERA, 1.02 WHIP, 33 K — a 13.7 K/9 rate is elite by any measure and he's immediately delivering on the massive prospect hype that convinced the Chinook to keep him.

694973.name: Christian Scott
694973.background: A right-handed pitching prospect for the Mets who had a highly anticipated debut, Christian Scott throws with excellent extension and a plus fastball with late life. He has strikeout upside and was one of the more talked-about 2024 call-ups before suffering an elbow injury.
694973.recentAnalysis: Scott has just 1.1 IP in 2026 with a 6.75 ERA — he appears to be working back cautiously from his injury and is not a viable fantasy asset yet.

695243.name: Mason Miller
695243.background: The closer for the Syracuse Sky Chiefs and one of the most terrifying relievers in baseball, Mason Miller features a mid-to-upper-100s fastball that is routinely among the hardest pitches thrown in any given season — an untouchable heater that generates elite strikeout rates and virtually no hard contact. The Sky Chiefs have one of the most dominant save situations in all of CBA, and Miller's save totals have been a major competitive advantage in their weekly matchups. He is the gold standard for CBA closers.
695243.recentAnalysis: Miller has been absolutely unhittable for Syracuse: 10 G, 10.1 IP, 0.00 ERA, 0.39 WHIP, 25 K, 7 SV — one of the most dominant closer stretches in recent CBA history and an enormous asset for the Sky Chiefs.

695505.name: Chase Burns
695505.background: One of the Sugar Land Space Cowboys' most exciting young arms, Chase Burns arrived as the Reds' most prized pitching prospect with a power arsenal featuring a triple-digit fastball and a devastating slider that generates elite whiff rates. His rapid ascent through Cincinnati's system and immediate MLB success demonstrated that his raw stuff translates at the highest level, and he's quickly become one of the NL's more exciting young starters.
